name: API.Format
comment:
Format Elm source code according to the standard style guide. This module
provides a programmatic interface to the Elm formatter, parsing source code and
pretty-printing it with consistent indentation, spacing, and line breaks.


# Formatting

@docs run

values.name: run
values.comment:
Format Elm source code according to the standard style guide.
Parses the source and returns formatted code, or an error message if parsing fails.

name: API.Install
comment:
Install Elm packages into an application or package project. This module
handles dependency resolution, constraint solving, and updating the elm.json file.

The installer can promote packages from indirect to direct dependencies, from test
to production dependencies, or add entirely new packages while maintaining version
compatibility across the dependency graph.


# Installation

@docs run

values.name: run
values.comment:
Install a package into the project's dependencies and update elm.json.
Resolves version constraints and updates the dependency graph while maintaining compatibility.

name: API.Main
comment:
Entry point for the Elm compiler API server. This module dispatches commands
from a JSON-based protocol to the appropriate API modules and returns JSON responses.

Supported commands include building projects (make), formatting code (format),
installing and uninstalling packages, and running diagnostics on source files.


# Program

@docs main

values.name: main
values.comment:
Entry point for the Elm compiler API server.
Reads JSON commands from stdin and dispatches to the appropriate API modules.

name: API.Make
comment:
Build Elm projects into JavaScript or HTML. This module handles the complete
build pipeline including dependency resolution, compilation, and code generation.

Supports debug mode for enhanced development experience, optimized production builds,
and optional source map generation.


# Build Pipeline

@docs run, Flags

unions.name: Flags
unions.comment: Compilation flags controlling debug mode, optimization, and source map generation.

values.name: run
values.comment:
Build an Elm project from a source file path with the specified flags.
Returns the compiled output as a string or an error describing what went wrong.
values.type: String.String -> API.Make.Flags -> Task.Task Basics.Never (Result.Result Builder.Reporting.Exit.Make String.String)

name: API.Uninstall
comment:
Remove Elm packages from an application or package project. This module handles
removing packages from elm.json and recalculating the dependency graph to ensure
all remaining dependencies are satisfied.

For applications, the solver recomputes the dependency solution after removal. For
packages, direct removal is performed since package dependencies use version ranges
rather than exact versions.


# Uninstallation

@docs run

values.name: run
values.comment:
Remove a package from the project's dependencies and update elm.json.
Recalculates the dependency graph to ensure all remaining dependencies are satisfied.

name: Builder.BackgroundWriter
comment:
Asynchronous file writing for improved build performance.

This module provides a mechanism to write files in the background without blocking
the main build process. All write operations within a scope are tracked, and the
scope ensures all writes complete before returning control to the caller.


# Scope Management

@docs Scope, withScope


# Background Writing

@docs writeBinary

unions.name: Scope
unions.comment: Represents a scope in which background writes can occur.

values.name: withScope
values.comment: Executes a callback with a background write scope, ensuring all writes complete before returning.
values.type: (Builder.BackgroundWriter.Scope -> Task.Task Basics.Never a) -> Task.Task Basics.Never a

values.name: writeBinary
values.comment: Writes binary data to a file in the background within the given scope.
values.type: (a -> Bytes.Encode.Encoder) -> Builder.BackgroundWriter.Scope -> String.String -> a -> Task.Task Basics.Never ()

name: Builder.Build

unions.name: Artifacts
unions.comment: Complete build artifacts including compiled modules, dependency interfaces, and root modules.

unions.name: BResult
unions.comment:
Build result for a single module after compilation or cache lookup.

Tracks whether the module was freshly compiled, unchanged from cache, or encountered errors.

unions.name: CachedInterface
unions.comment: State of a cached module interface: unneeded, successfully loaded, or corrupted.

unions.name: DocsGoal
unions.comment:
Specifies how to handle documentation during compilation.

Can keep docs in memory, write to a file, or ignore them entirely.
cases: Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Elm.ModuleName.Raw Builder.Build.BResult -> Task.Task Basics.Never docs

unions.name: Module
unions.comment: Represents a compiled module, either freshly compiled or loaded from cache.

unions.name: ReplArtifacts
unions.comment: Build artifacts specific to REPL sessions, including type information for interactive evaluation.

unions.name: Root
unions.comment: Represents a root module, either from within the project or from a dependency.

aliases.name: ArtifactsData
aliases.comment: Data contained within build artifacts.
aliases.type: { pkg : Compiler.Elm.Package.Name, deps : Builder.Build.Dependencies, roots : Compiler.Data.NonEmptyList.Nonempty Builder.Build.Root, modules : List.List Builder.Build.Module }

aliases.name: Dependencies
aliases.comment: Map of dependency module interfaces needed for type checking.

aliases.name: ReplArtifactsData
aliases.comment: Data contained within REPL build artifacts.
aliases.type: { home : System.TypeCheck.IO.Canonical, modules : List.List Builder.Build.Module, localizer : Compiler.Reporting.Render.Type.Localizer.Localizer, annotations : Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Compiler.AST.Canonical.Annotation }

values.name: cachedInterfaceDecoder
values.comment: Decode a cached interface from bytes.

values.name: fromExposed
values.comment:
Build a project by compiling a specific list of exposed modules (e.g., for package builds).

This entry point compiles the given modules and their dependencies, respecting the
documentation goal (keep, write, or ignore). It performs parallel compilation with
incremental rebuilding based on modification times and interface changes.
values.type: Bytes.Decode.Decoder docs -> (docs -> Bytes.Encode.Encoder) -> Builder.Reporting.Style -> Utils.Main.FilePath -> Builder.Elm.Details.Details -> Builder.Build.DocsGoal docs -> Compiler.Data.NonEmptyList.Nonempty Compiler.Elm.ModuleName.Raw -> Task.Task Basics.Never (Result.Result Builder.Reporting.Exit.BuildProblem docs)

values.name: fromPaths
values.comment:
Build a project by compiling modules from specific file paths (e.g., for application builds).

This entry point discovers modules from the given file paths, crawls their dependencies,
and performs parallel incremental compilation.
values.type: Builder.Reporting.Style -> Utils.Main.FilePath -> Builder.Elm.Details.Details -> Basics.Bool -> Compiler.Data.NonEmptyList.Nonempty Utils.Main.FilePath -> Task.Task Basics.Never (Result.Result Builder.Reporting.Exit.BuildProblem Builder.Build.Artifacts)

values.name: fromRepl
values.comment:
Compile Elm source code for evaluation in a REPL session.

Parses the source, type checks it against available dependencies, and produces
artifacts suitable for interactive evaluation.
values.type: Utils.Main.FilePath -> Builder.Elm.Details.Details -> String.String -> Task.Task Basics.Never (Result.Result Builder.Reporting.Exit.Repl Builder.Build.ReplArtifacts)

values.name: getRootNames
values.comment: Extract the module names of all root modules from the build artifacts.

values.name: ignoreDocs
values.comment: Ignore documentation generation during compilation.

values.name: keepDocs
values.comment: Keep generated documentation in memory as a dictionary.

values.name: writeDocs
values.comment: Write generated documentation to a JSON file at the specified path.

name: Builder.Deps.Bump
comment:
Generates possible version bump candidates for package publishing.

Given the known version history of a package, this module computes all valid next
versions for MAJOR, MINOR, and PATCH bumps. It identifies appropriate base versions
by grouping existing versions by major and minor version numbers.

For example, with versions [1.0.0, 1.0.1, 1.1.0, 2.0.0]:

  - MAJOR bump from 2.0.0 to 3.0.0
  - MINOR bumps from 1.1.0 to 1.2.0 and 2.0.0 to 2.1.0
  - PATCH bumps from 1.0.1 to 1.0.2, 1.1.0 to 1.1.1, and 2.0.0 to 2.0.1


# Version Candidates

@docs getPossibilities

values.name: getPossibilities
values.comment:
Generate all valid version bump possibilities from a package's version history.
Returns triples of (base version, new version, bump magnitude) for all valid MAJOR, MINOR, and PATCH bumps.

name: Builder.Deps.Diff
comment:
Computes API differences between package versions to determine semantic version bumps.

This module compares package documentation across versions, detecting breaking changes,
new additions, and patches. It determines whether changes require MAJOR, MINOR, or PATCH
version bumps according to semantic versioning rules.

Key checks include:

  - Type signature compatibility (including type variable renaming)
  - Module additions and removals
  - Public API changes in unions, aliases, values, and operators
  - Operator precedence and associativity changes


# Change Types

@docs PackageChanges, ModuleChanges, ModuleChangesData, Changes


# Diffing

@docs diff, getDocs


# Version Bumping

@docs toMagnitude, moduleChangeMagnitude, bump

unions.name: Changes
unions.comment: Generic change tracking: added items, changed items (with old and new values), and removed items.

unions.name: ModuleChanges
unions.comment: Changes within a single module between versions.

unions.name: PackageChanges
unions.comment:
Changes between two package versions: added modules, changed modules, and removed modules.
Only includes module changes that are not just patches (MAJOR or MINOR changes).

aliases.name: ModuleChangesData
aliases.comment: Changes within a module broken down by declaration type: unions, aliases, values, and binops.
aliases.type: { unions : Builder.Deps.Diff.Changes String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Compiler.Elm.Docs.Union, aliases : Builder.Deps.Diff.Changes String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Compiler.Elm.Docs.Alias, values : Builder.Deps.Diff.Changes String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Compiler.Elm.Docs.Value, binops : Builder.Deps.Diff.Changes String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Compiler.Elm.Docs.Binop }

values.name: bump
values.comment: Compute the new version by applying the appropriate bump (MAJOR, MINOR, or PATCH) to the current version.

values.name: diff
values.comment:
Compare two versions of package documentation to determine what changed.
Filters out patch-level changes, returning only MAJOR and MINOR changes.

values.name: getDocs
values.comment:
Fetch documentation for a specific package version, either from local cache or the package server.
Caches downloaded documentation locally for future use.
values.type: Builder.Stuff.PackageCache -> Builder.Http.Manager -> Compiler.Elm.Package.Name -> Compiler.Elm.Version.Version -> Task.Task Basics.Never (Result.Result Builder.Reporting.Exit.DocsProblem Compiler.Elm.Docs.Documentation)

values.name: moduleChangeMagnitude
values.comment:
Determine the semantic version magnitude required by changes within a single module.
Returns the maximum magnitude across all declaration types (unions, aliases, values, binops).

values.name: toMagnitude
values.comment:
Determine the semantic version magnitude (MAJOR, MINOR, or PATCH) required by package changes.
MAJOR for removed items or breaking changes, MINOR for additions, PATCH for no changes.

name: Builder.Deps.Registry

unions.name: KnownVersions
unions.comment: Available versions of a package, with the latest version first followed by older versions.

unions.name: Registry
unions.comment:
The package registry maps package names to their available versions.
Contains a count of entries for incremental updates and a dictionary of packages.

values.name: fetch
values.comment:
Fetch the complete registry from the package server and cache it locally.
Use this for initial registry download or when the cache is missing.
values.type: Builder.Http.Manager -> Builder.Stuff.PackageCache -> Task.Task Basics.Never (Result.Result Builder.Reporting.Exit.RegistryProblem Builder.Deps.Registry.Registry)

values.name: getVersions
values.comment: Look up available versions for a package name. Returns Nothing if the package isn't in the registry.

values.name: getVersions_
values.comment:
Look up available versions for a package name, providing suggested alternatives if not found.
Returns nearby package names (by edit distance) when the package doesn't exist.

values.name: latest
values.comment:
Get the latest registry, either by reading the cache and updating it, or fetching it fresh.
This is the primary entry point for obtaining an up-to-date registry.
values.type: Builder.Http.Manager -> Builder.Stuff.PackageCache -> Task.Task Basics.Never (Result.Result Builder.Reporting.Exit.RegistryProblem Builder.Deps.Registry.Registry)

values.name: read
values.comment: Read the cached registry from disk. Returns Nothing if the cache doesn't exist or is invalid.

values.name: registryDecoder
values.comment: Binary decoder for reading a registry from disk cache.

values.name: registryEncoder
values.comment: Binary encoder for writing a registry to disk cache.

values.name: update
values.comment:
Update an existing registry by fetching only new package versions since the last sync.
Returns the updated registry, or the original if no updates are available.
values.type: Builder.Http.Manager -> Builder.Stuff.PackageCache -> Builder.Deps.Registry.Registry -> Task.Task Basics.Never (Result.Result Builder.Reporting.Exit.RegistryProblem Builder.Deps.Registry.Registry)

name: Builder.Deps.Solver
comment:
Constraint-based dependency solver for Elm packages.

This module implements a backtracking constraint solver that finds compatible versions
of all dependencies in an Elm project. It handles both application and package dependency
resolution, attempting multiple strategies to find valid solutions.

The solver works by:

1.  Starting with direct dependency constraints
2.  Recursively loading transitive dependencies
3.  Checking constraint compatibility and backtracking on conflicts
4.  Attempting progressively relaxed constraints if exact solutions fail


# Solver Types

@docs Solver, SolverResult, State


# Environment

@docs Env, EnvData, Connection, initEnv


# Dependency Details

@docs Details


# Application Solutions

@docs AppSolution, addToApp, addToTestApp, removeFromApp


# Package Verification

@docs verify


# Serialization

@docs envEncoder, envDecoder

unions.name: AppSolution
unions.comment:
Complete solution for an application's dependencies.

Contains the old dependency set, the new dependency set, and the updated
outline with properly categorized direct and indirect dependencies for
both production and test contexts.

unions.name: Connection
unions.comment:
Network connection state for the solver.

Online means packages can be fetched from the package website.
Offline means only locally cached packages are available.

unions.name: Details
unions.comment:
Package details containing the resolved version and its dependencies.

Used when verifying existing dependency solutions or loading package information.

unions.name: Env
unions.comment: Opaque environment type wrapping EnvData.

unions.name: Solver
unions.comment:
Monadic solver type that encapsulates backtracking constraint solving.

Represents a computation that can succeed with a result, backtrack to try
alternatives, or fail with an error. The solver maintains state containing
the package cache, registry, and constraint dictionary.

unions.name: SolverResult
unions.comment:
Result type for solver operations.

  - SolverOk: Successfully found a solution
  - NoSolution: No compatible version combination exists (online mode)
  - NoOfflineSolution: No solution with locally cached packages (offline mode)
  - SolverErr: Encountered an error during solving (network, cache corruption, etc.)

unions.name: State
unions.comment: Opaque wrapper around StateData for type safety.

aliases.name: EnvData
aliases.comment:
Environment data for solver operations.

Contains the package cache directory, HTTP manager for network requests,
connection state (online/offline), and the package registry.
aliases.type: { cache : Builder.Stuff.PackageCache, manager : Builder.Http.Manager, connection : Builder.Deps.Solver.Connection, registry : Builder.Deps.Registry.Registry }

values.name: addToApp
values.comment:
Add a package to an application's dependencies.

Attempts to find a solution that includes the new package while maintaining
compatibility with existing dependencies. Tries progressively relaxed constraint
strategies: exact versions, then untilNextMinor, then untilNextMajor, and finally
unconstrained.

The forTest parameter determines whether the package is added to test dependencies
or production dependencies.
values.type: Builder.Stuff.PackageCache -> Builder.Deps.Solver.Connection -> Builder.Deps.Registry.Registry -> Compiler.Elm.Package.Name -> Builder.Elm.Outline.AppOutline -> Basics.Bool -> Task.Task Basics.Never (Builder.Deps.Solver.SolverResult Builder.Deps.Solver.AppSolution)

values.name: addToTestApp
values.comment:
Add a package with a specific constraint to test dependencies.

Similar to addToApp but for test-specific dependencies with an explicit version
constraint. Used when the test framework requires a particular package version range.
values.type: Builder.Stuff.PackageCache -> Builder.Deps.Solver.Connection -> Builder.Deps.Registry.Registry -> Compiler.Elm.Package.Name -> Compiler.Elm.Constraint.Constraint -> Builder.Elm.Outline.AppOutline -> Task.Task Basics.Never (Builder.Deps.Solver.SolverResult Builder.Deps.Solver.AppSolution)

values.name: envDecoder
values.comment:
Binary decoder for Env, enabling environment deserialization.

Decodes a binary representation back into an Env containing the package cache,
HTTP manager, connection state, and registry.

values.name: envEncoder
values.comment:
Binary encoder for Env, enabling environment serialization.

Encodes the package cache, HTTP manager, connection state, and registry
into a binary format for caching or transmission.

values.name: initEnv
values.comment:
Initialize the solver environment.

Creates an HTTP manager, loads the package cache, and fetches or updates the
package registry. Falls back to offline mode if registry update fails but a
cached registry exists.

values.name: removeFromApp
values.comment:
Remove a package from an application's dependencies.

Resolves dependencies without the specified package, automatically removing
it from direct dependencies and cleaning up any indirect dependencies that
are no longer needed.
values.type: Builder.Stuff.PackageCache -> Builder.Deps.Solver.Connection -> Builder.Deps.Registry.Registry -> Compiler.Elm.Package.Name -> Builder.Elm.Outline.AppOutline -> Task.Task Basics.Never (Builder.Deps.Solver.SolverResult Builder.Deps.Solver.AppSolution)

values.name: verify
values.comment:
Verify that a set of dependency constraints has a valid solution.

Attempts to find compatible versions for all packages satisfying the given
constraints. Returns the resolved versions along with each package's transitive
dependency constraints.
values.type: Builder.Stuff.PackageCache -> Builder.Deps.Solver.Connection -> Builder.Deps.Registry.Registry -> Data.Map.Dict ( String.String, String.String ) Compiler.Elm.Package.Name Compiler.Elm.Constraint.Constraint -> Task.Task Basics.Never (Builder.Deps.Solver.SolverResult (Data.Map.Dict ( String.String, String.String ) Compiler.Elm.Package.Name Builder.Deps.Solver.Details))

name: Builder.Deps.Website
comment:
Constructs URLs for the Elm package registry website.

This module provides utilities for building URLs to access package metadata, documentation,
and registry endpoints. It respects the GUIDA\_REGISTRY environment variable to support
custom package registries, defaulting to package.elm-lang.org.


# URL Construction

@docs route, metadata

values.name: metadata
values.comment:
Construct a URL for accessing package metadata files (like docs.json or endpoint.json).
Respects the GUIDA\_REGISTRY environment variable for custom registries.
values.type: Compiler.Elm.Package.Name -> Compiler.Elm.Version.Version -> String.String -> Task.Task Basics.Never String.String

values.name: route
values.comment:
Construct a URL for a registry API endpoint with optional query parameters.
Respects the GUIDA\_REGISTRY environment variable for custom registries.
values.type: String.String -> List.List ( String.String, String.String ) -> Task.Task Basics.Never String.String

name: Builder.Elm.Details

unions.name: Details
unions.comment: Project details tracking compilation status and dependency information.

unions.name: Extras
unions.comment: Build artifact state indicating whether artifacts are cached on disk or freshly loaded in memory.

unions.name: Foreign
unions.comment: A foreign module from a package dependency, tracking which packages provide it.

unions.name: Local
unions.comment: Status of a local project module.

unions.name: Status
unions.comment: Status of a module during crawling: local source, foreign interface, or kernel code.

unions.name: ValidOutline
unions.comment: A validated project outline, either an application with source directories or a package with exposed modules.

aliases.name: BuildID
aliases.comment: Incrementing identifier to track when modules need recompilation based on interface changes.

aliases.name: DetailsData
aliases.comment: Complete build state for a project including module status, dependencies, and build artifacts.
aliases.type: { time : Builder.File.Time, outline : Builder.Elm.Details.ValidOutline, buildID : Builder.Elm.Details.BuildID, locals : Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Elm.ModuleName.Raw Builder.Elm.Details.Local, foreigns : Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Elm.ModuleName.Raw Builder.Elm.Details.Foreign, extras : Builder.Elm.Details.Extras, deps : Data.Map.Dict ( String.String, String.String ) Compiler.Elm.Package.Name Compiler.Elm.Version.Version }

aliases.name: Interfaces
aliases.comment: Type interfaces for all dependency modules, indexed by canonical module name.

aliases.name: LocalData
aliases.comment: Status information for a local module including file location, modification time, and compilation timestamps.
aliases.type: { path : Utils.Main.FilePath, time : Builder.File.Time, deps : List.List Compiler.Elm.ModuleName.Raw, hasMain : Basics.Bool, lastChange : Builder.Elm.Details.BuildID, lastCompile : Builder.Elm.Details.BuildID }

values.name: detailsEncoder
values.comment: Binary encoder for writing project details to cache.

values.name: load
values.comment:
Load project details, verifying dependencies and building them if necessary.
Checks if elm.json has changed and regenerates details if needed. Used by build commands.
values.type: Builder.Reporting.Style -> Builder.BackgroundWriter.Scope -> Utils.Main.FilePath -> Basics.Bool -> Basics.Bool -> Task.Task Basics.Never (Result.Result Builder.Reporting.Exit.Details Builder.Elm.Details.Details)

values.name: loadInterfaces
values.comment:
Load type interfaces for all dependencies in a background thread.
Returns immediately with an MVar that will contain the interfaces when loading completes.

values.name: loadObjects
values.comment:
Load optimized objects for all dependencies in a background thread.
Returns immediately with an MVar that will contain the objects when loading completes.

values.name: loadTypedObjects
values.comment:
Load typed global objects for MLIR backend.
Loads both local typed objects and typed objects from all package dependencies.

values.name: localDecoder
values.comment: Binary decoder for local module status.

values.name: localEncoder
values.comment: Binary encoder for local module status.

values.name: verifyInstall
values.comment:
Verify and install all dependencies for a project without loading artifacts.
Used by the install command to download and build dependencies.
values.type: Builder.BackgroundWriter.Scope -> Utils.Main.FilePath -> Builder.Deps.Solver.Env -> Builder.Elm.Outline.Outline -> Task.Task Basics.Never (Result.Result Builder.Reporting.Exit.Details ())

name: Builder.Elm.Outline

unions.name: AppOutline
unions.comment: Wrapper type for application outline data.

unions.name: Exposed
unions.comment:
Represents how modules are exposed in a package.
Can be a simple list of modules or a dictionary with categorized sections.

unions.name: Outline
unions.comment:
Represents the top-level structure of an elm.json file.
Can be either an application outline or a package outline.

unions.name: PkgOutline
unions.comment: Wrapper type for package outline data.

unions.name: SrcDir
unions.comment:
Represents a source directory path.
Can be either absolute or relative to the project root.

aliases.name: AppOutlineData
aliases.comment:
Data structure for an application elm.json.
Contains Elm version, source directories, and direct/indirect dependencies
for both main dependencies and test dependencies.
aliases.type: { elm : Compiler.Elm.Version.Version, srcDirs : Compiler.Data.NonEmptyList.Nonempty Builder.Elm.Outline.SrcDir, depsDirect : Data.Map.Dict ( String.String, String.String ) Compiler.Elm.Package.Name Compiler.Elm.Version.Version, depsIndirect : Data.Map.Dict ( String.String, String.String ) Compiler.Elm.Package.Name Compiler.Elm.Version.Version, testDirect : Data.Map.Dict ( String.String, String.String ) Compiler.Elm.Package.Name Compiler.Elm.Version.Version, testIndirect : Data.Map.Dict ( String.String, String.String ) Compiler.Elm.Package.Name Compiler.Elm.Version.Version }

aliases.name: Decoder
aliases.comment: A decoder type specialized for outline parsing errors.

aliases.name: PkgOutlineData
aliases.comment:
Data structure for a package elm.json.
Contains package name, summary, license, version, exposed modules,
and dependency constraints for both main and test dependencies.
aliases.type: { name : Compiler.Elm.Package.Name, summary : String.String, license : Compiler.Elm.Licenses.License, version : Compiler.Elm.Version.Version, exposed : Builder.Elm.Outline.Exposed, deps : Data.Map.Dict ( String.String, String.String ) Compiler.Elm.Package.Name Compiler.Elm.Constraint.Constraint, testDeps : Data.Map.Dict ( String.String, String.String ) Compiler.Elm.Package.Name Compiler.Elm.Constraint.Constraint, elm : Compiler.Elm.Constraint.Constraint }

values.name: decoder
values.comment:
Decodes an Outline from JSON.
Checks the "type" field to determine whether to parse as an application or package.

values.name: defaultSummary
values.comment: The default placeholder summary text for new packages.

values.name: flattenExposed
values.comment:
Flattens an Exposed structure into a simple list of module names.
Works for both ExposedList and ExposedDict by extracting all modules.

values.name: getAllModulePaths
values.comment:
Recursively discovers all module file paths in the project and its dependencies.
Returns a dictionary mapping canonical module names to their file paths.
values.type: Utils.Main.FilePath -> Task.Task Basics.Never (Data.Map.Dict (List.List String.String) System.TypeCheck.IO.Canonical Utils.Main.FilePath)

values.name: read
values.comment:
Reads and validates an elm.json file from the specified root directory.
Returns either an error describing what went wrong or a validated Outline.

values.name: srcDirDecoder
values.comment:
Decodes a SrcDir from bytes.
Reads a tag byte (0 for absolute, 1 for relative) followed by the path string.

values.name: srcDirEncoder
values.comment:
Encodes a SrcDir to bytes for serialization.
Uses a tag byte (0 for absolute, 1 for relative) followed by the path string.

values.name: write
values.comment: Writes an Outline to an elm.json file at the specified root directory.

name: Builder.File

unions.name: Time
unions.comment: Represents a file modification time.

values.name: exists
values.comment: Checks if a file exists at the given path.

values.name: getTime
values.comment: Gets the modification time of a file.

values.name: readBinary
values.comment: Reads binary data from a file, returning Nothing if the file doesn't exist or is corrupt.
values.type: Bytes.Decode.Decoder a -> Utils.Main.FilePath -> Task.Task Basics.Never (Maybe.Maybe a)

values.name: readStdin

values.name: readUtf8
values.comment: Reads a UTF-8 encoded file as a string.

values.name: remove
values.comment: Removes a file if it exists, silently succeeding if it doesn't.

values.name: timeDecoder
values.comment: Decodes a file modification time from bytes.

values.name: timeEncoder
values.comment: Encodes a file modification time to bytes.

values.name: writeBinary
values.comment: Writes binary data to a file, creating parent directories if needed.

values.name: writePackage
values.comment: Extracts a package archive to a destination directory, filtering for relevant files.

values.name: writeUtf8
values.comment: Writes a UTF-8 encoded string to a file.

values.name: zeroTime
values.comment: Returns a zero timestamp, used to indicate a file has never been modified.

name: Builder.Generate

values.name: debug
values.comment: Generates debug-mode output with type information for runtime type checking.
values.type: Compiler.Generate.CodeGen.CodeGen -> Basics.Bool -> Basics.Int -> Utils.Main.FilePath -> Builder.Elm.Details.Details -> Builder.Build.Artifacts -> Task.Task Builder.Reporting.Exit.Generate Compiler.Generate.CodeGen.Output

values.comment: Generates development-mode output without optimization.
values.type: Compiler.Generate.CodeGen.CodeGen -> Basics.Bool -> Basics.Int -> Utils.Main.FilePath -> Builder.Elm.Details.Details -> Builder.Build.Artifacts -> Task.Task Builder.Reporting.Exit.Generate Compiler.Generate.CodeGen.Output

values.name: javascriptBackend

values.name: mlirBackend

name: Common.Format
comment:
Format Elm source code with consistent style and structure.

This module provides the main entry point for formatting Elm modules. It parses
source code and renders it using the Box layout engine with standardized formatting rules.


# Formatting

@docs format

values.name: format
values.comment:
Format Elm source code into a standardized style.

Takes a syntax version, project type, and source string, then parses and reformats
the code with consistent indentation and layout. Returns an error if the source
cannot be parsed.

name: Common.Format.Box
comment:
A box-based layout engine for pretty-printing formatted code.

This module provides a two-level abstraction for text layout: `Line` represents a single
line of text with proper handling of spaces and tabs, while `Box` represents potentially
multi-line content that can be stacked vertically or kept on a single line.

The layout algorithm automatically handles indentation, line breaking, and alignment to
produce consistently formatted output. Tab stops are aligned to 4-space boundaries.

Reference implementation: `elm-format-lib/src/Box.hs`


# Line Construction

@docs Line, identifier, keyword, punc, literal, row, space


# Box Construction

@docs Box, blankLine, line, mustBreak, stack_, stack1, andThen


# Box Properties

@docs allSingles, lineLength


# Box Transformations

@docs indent, prefix, addSuffix


# Rendering

@docs render

unions.name: Box
unions.comment:
Box contains Lines (at least one - can't be empty).
Box either:

  - can appear in the middle of a line
    (Stack someLine [], thus can be joined without problems), or
  - has to appear on its own
    (Stack someLine moreLines OR MustBreak someLine).

MustBreak is only used for `--` comments.

Stack contains two or more lines.

Sometimes (see `prefix`) the first line of Stack
gets different treatment than the other lines.

unions.name: Line
unions.comment:
A line is ALWAYS just one line.

Space is self-explanatory,
Tab aligns to the nearest multiple of 4 spaces,
Text brings any string into the data structure,
Row joins more of these elements onto one line.

values.name: addSuffix
values.comment: Add a suffix to the last line of a Box.

values.name: allSingles
values.comment: Check if all Boxes in a list are single lines. Returns Ok (List Line) if they all are, Err (List Box) otherwise.

values.name: identifier
values.comment: Create a Line from an identifier string.

values.name: indent
values.comment: Indent all lines in a Box by adding a Tab at the beginning of each line.

values.name: keyword
values.comment: Create a Line from a keyword string.

values.name: lineLength
values.comment: Calculate the total length of a Line in characters, accounting for tabs aligning to 4-space boundaries.

values.name: literal
values.comment: Create a Line from a literal string.

values.name: prefix
values.comment:
Add the prefix to the first line,
pad the other lines with spaces of the same length

EXAMPLE:
abcde
xyz
----->
myPrefix abcde
xyz

values.name: punc
values.comment: Create a Line from a punctuation string.

values.name: render
values.comment: Render a Box to a String with proper indentation and newlines.

values.name: row
values.comment: join more Line elements into one

values.name: space
values.comment: A single space element.

name: Common.Format.Cheapskate.Inlines
comment:
Markdown inline element parsing.

This module provides parsers for inline Markdown elements such as emphasis, links,
code spans, images, HTML tags, and entities. It handles the complex precedence rules
between different inline constructs (e.g., code backticks take precedence over emphasis).

@docs pHtmlTag, pLinkLabel, pReference, parseInlines

values.name: pHtmlTag
values.comment:
Parse an HTML tag and return its type and full text representation.
Returns a tuple of the tag type (Opening, Closing, or SelfClosing) and the complete tag string.

values.name: pLinkLabel
values.comment:
Parse a link label enclosed in square brackets.
Handles nested structures respecting precedence: code backticks have precedence over
brackets, which have precedence over emphasis markers. For example:

  - [a link `with a ](/url)` character - does NOT contain a link
  - [a link \*with emphasized ](/url) text\* - contains a link

values.name: pReference
values.comment:
Parse a link reference definition.
Format: [label]: url "optional title"
Returns a tuple of (label, url, title). Assumes input has no leading/trailing spaces.

values.name: parseInlines
values.comment:
Parse a text string into a list of inline elements.
Resolves link references using the provided reference map.

name: Common.Format.Cheapskate.Parse
comment:
Markdown parsing implementation based on the Cheapskate algorithm.

This module provides a two-phase Markdown parser that converts input text into a structured
document representation. The parser handles CommonMark-style Markdown with extensions for
Elm documentation features.

Phase 1 (processLines): Builds a container stack by processing input line-by-line, opening
and closing containers (blockquotes, lists, code blocks) as appropriate. This phase produces
a tree of containers with leaf nodes representing individual lines.

Phase 2 (processDocument): Transforms the container tree into a proper AST, grouping text
lines into paragraphs, consecutive list items into lists, and parsing inline content within
text blocks.

The parser supports standard Markdown features including headers, lists, code blocks, block
quotes, horizontal rules, HTML blocks, and link references. It also recognizes Elm-specific
constructs like documentation directives for referencing exposed values.

@docs markdown

values.name: markdown
values.comment:
Parse a Markdown document with the given rendering options.
Converts the input string into a structured Doc representation.

aliases.name: Scanner
aliases.comment:
A parser that consumes input without capturing any result.
Scanners are intended to operate on a single line of input (except scanSpnl).
They return unit () on success, indicating only that the pattern matched.

values.name: isEscapable

Check if a character can be backslash-escaped in Markdown.

values.comment:
Check if a character is a significant whitespace character for Markdown parsing.
Only recognizes space, tab, newline, and carriage return as whitespace.
Other Unicode whitespace characters like non-breaking space are treated as regular characters.

name: Common.Format.ImportInfo
comment:
Track and resolve import information for Elm modules.

This module analyzes a module's import declarations to build a comprehensive mapping
of exposed values, type aliases, module aliases, and direct imports. It handles both
explicit imports and exposing-all imports, resolving symbol names to their defining modules.

The import resolution system supports:

  - Direct unqualified imports (e.g., `import List`)
  - Module aliases (e.g., `import Dict as D`)
  - Exposed values (e.g., `import Maybe exposing (Maybe, withDefault)`)
  - Exposing-all imports (e.g., `import Html exposing (..)`)
  - Default imports (Basics, List, Maybe)


# Types

@docs ImportInfo


# Building Import Information

@docs fromModule

unions.name: ImportInfo
unions.comment:
Complete import information for a module, tracking all symbols and their sources.
Contains exposed values, module aliases, direct imports, ambiguous names, and unresolved imports.

values.name: fromModule
values.comment: Build import information from a parsed module, using known contents to resolve exposing-all imports.

name: Common.Format.RWS

aliases.name: RWS
aliases.comment:
Reader-Writer-State monad computation.
Takes an environment (r) and state (s), returns a result (a), updated state, and log.
aliases.type: r -> s -> ( a, s, Data.Map.Dict String.String String.String ( String.String, String.String ) )

values.name: andThen
values.comment:
Monadic bind for RWS computations.
Sequences two computations, passing the result of the first to a function producing the second.

values.name: error
values.comment:
Raise an error with the given message.
Crashes the computation.

values.name: evalRWS
values.comment:
Evaluate the RWS computation, returning the result and accumulated log.
Discards the final state.
values.type: Common.Format.RWS.RWS r s a -> r -> s -> ( a, Data.Map.Dict String.String String.String ( String.String, String.String ) )

values.name: get
values.comment: Get the current state.

values.name: mapM_
values.comment:
Map a monadic action over a list and discard the results.
Useful for performing side effects on each element.
values.type: (a -> Common.Format.RWS.RWS r s b) -> List.List a -> Common.Format.RWS.RWS r s ()

values.name: modify
values.comment: Modify the state using a transformation function.

values.name: put
values.comment: Replace the state with a new value.

values.name: replicateM
values.comment: Repeat a computation n times and collect the results.

values.name: return
values.comment: Lift a pure value into the RWS context.

values.name: tell
values.comment: Append to the log (writer component).

name: Common.Format.Render.Box
comment:
Render Elm AST structures to formatted Box layout.

This module is the core formatting engine that transforms parsed Elm syntax trees into
Box-based layouts. It handles the formatting of all Elm language constructs including:

  - Module headers and exports
  - Import declarations
  - Type definitions and annotations
  - Function declarations and patterns
  - Expressions (applications, let-bindings, case expressions, etc.)
  - Comments and documentation

The formatter makes intelligent decisions about single-line vs multi-line layout based
on content complexity and length, applying consistent indentation and spacing rules
throughout.


# Module Formatting

@docs formatModule

values.name: formatModule
values.comment:
Format a complete Elm module into a Box layout with configurable spacing and header options.
The first parameter controls whether to add a default module header if none exists.

name: Common.Format.Render.ElmStructure

unions.name: FunctionApplicationMultiline
unions.comment:
Controls multiline behavior for function applications.

  - FASplitFirst: Function name on one line, all arguments indented on subsequent lines
  - FAJoinFirst: Function name and first argument may share a line based on the Multiline setting

unions.name: Multiline
unions.comment:
Controls whether multiple elements should be joined on one line or split across lines.

  - JoinAll: Keep all elements on a single line if possible
  - SplitAll: Force each element onto its own line

values.name: application
values.comment:
Formats as:

    first rest0 rest1 rest2

    first rest0
        rest1
        rest2

    first
        rest0
        rest1
        rest2

values.name: definition
values.comment: An equalsPair where the left side is an application

values.name: equalsPair
values.comment:
Formats as:

    left =
        right
    left =
        right
    left =
        right

values.name: extensionGroup_
forceableRowOrStack
values.comment:
Formats as row or stack with forceable multiline layout.
Similar to forceableSpaceSepOrStack but without spaces between elements in single-line mode.

    first rest0 rest1

    first

    rest0

    rest1

values.name: forceableSpaceSepOrIndented
values.comment:
Formats as space-separated or indented with forceable multiline layout.
When forceMultiline is True or content doesn't fit on one line, the first element stays
on its own line and subsequent elements are indented.

    first rest0 rest1 rest2

    first
        rest0
        rest1
        rest2

values.name: forceableSpaceSepOrStack
values.comment:
Formats as:

    first rest0 rest1

    first

    rest0

    rest1

values.name: forceableSpaceSepOrStack1
values.comment: Same as `forceableSpaceSepOrStack`

values.name: group
values.comment:
`group True '<' ';' '>'` formats as:

    <>

    < child0 >

    < child0; child1; child2 >

    < child0
    ; child1
    ; child2
    >
values.type: Basics.Bool -> String.String -> String.String -> String.String -> Basics.Bool -> List.List Common.Format.Box.Box -> Common.Format.Box.Box

values.name: group_
values.comment:
Similar to `group` but with additional footer elements to insert before the closing delimiter.
Allows extra boxes (like trailing commas or comments) to be placed after the main children
but before the right delimiter.
values.type: Basics.Bool -> String.String -> String.String -> List.List Common.Format.Box.Box -> String.String -> Basics.Bool -> List.List Common.Format.Box.Box -> Common.Format.Box.Box

values.name: prefixOrIndented
values.comment:
Formats two boxes with space separation on single line, or stacks them with indentation.
Similar to spaceSepOrPrefix but without the special handling for short operators.

    a b

    a
        b

Formats as:

    first rest0 rest1 rest2

    first
        rest0
        rest1
        rest2

values.name: spaceSepOrPrefix

Formats as:

    op rest

        rest2

  - `Filled` - Alias has been fully expanded with concrete types

A type annotation with universally quantified type variables.

The `FreeVars` contains the names of type variables that are polymorphic.

Code generation optimization hints for constructors.

  - `Normal` - Standard constructor representation
  - `Enum` - All constructors are nullary (no arguments)
  - `Unbox` - Single constructor with single argument can be unboxed

unions.name: Decls
unions.comment:
A linked list of top-level declarations in a module.

  - `Declare` - A non-recursive definition
  - `DeclareRec` - A group of mutually recursive definitions

A value or function definition.

name: Compiler.AST.Monomorphized
comment:
Monomorphized AST for backends that can optimize using concrete types.

This IR makes all specialized definitions, layouts, and closure structures
explicit so that later stages can generate low-level code without needing
type inference or layout computation.

High‑level properties:

  - Each polymorphic Elm definition that is actually used at one or more
    type instantiations appears as one or more specialized nodes in
    `MonoGraph`, identified by a concrete `SpecId`.

  - Record, tuple, and custom types carry their computed runtime layouts
    (`RecordLayout`, `TupleLayout`, `CustomLayout`), so consumers of this
    IR can rely on fixed shapes and unboxing decisions.

  - Higher‑order functions are either represented as explicit closures
    (`MonoClosure` with captured variables and parameter types) or as
    specialized top‑level function nodes (`MonoDefine`, `MonoTailFunc`).

  - Remaining type variables in `MonoType` are limited to a small,
    backend‑aware set of constrained variables (`MVar` with `Constraint`)
    that do not require further inference. In particular, any unresolved
    numeric variables are intended to be rejected before final code
    generation. See `MonoType` and `Constraint` for the precise invariants.

This module defines the data structures for the monomorphized program
(`MonoGraph`, `MonoNode`, `MonoExpr`, etc.) along with utilities such as
`typeOf` and the layout computation functions.


# Types

@docs MonoType, Literal, Constraint


# Runtime Layouts

@docs RecordLayout, FieldInfo, CtorLayout, TupleLayout


# Lambda Sets

@docs LambdaId


# Globals and Specialization

@docs Global, SpecKey, SpecId, SpecializationRegistry, emptyRegistry, getOrCreateSpecId, lookupSpecKey


# Program Graph

@docs MonoGraph, MainInfo, MonoNode


# Expressions

@docs MonoExpr, ClosureInfo, MonoDef, MonoDestructor, MonoPath


# Pattern Matching

@docs Decider, MonoChoice


# Type Utilities

@docs typeOf, canUnbox


# Layout Computation

@docs computeRecordLayout, computeTupleLayout


# Comparison and Ordering

@docs toComparableSpecKey

unions.name: Constraint
unions.comment:
Constraint on an unspecialized type variable in `MonoType`.

These constraints record how much is known about a type variable after
monomorphization and determine what obligations remain before codegen.

  - `CEcoValue`:
    The variable's concrete Elm type is erased in the backend and is
    always represented as a boxed `eco.value`. Its precise source type
    does not influence layout or calling convention; it is only tracked
    for comparison/debugging purposes. It is safe (and expected) for
    `MVar _ CEcoValue` to survive to MLIR codegen, where it is lowered
    uniformly to `eco.value`.

  - `CNumber`:
    The variable is known to be a numeric type (`Int` or `Float` in Elm).
    This variable MUST be resolved to either `MInt` or `MFloat` by the
    monomorphization/specialization phase for all reachable code paths
    that perform numeric operations. Any occurrence of `MVar _ CNumber`
    in a `MonoType` that reaches MLIR codegen is a compiler bug.

In other words, `CEcoValue` marks "erased / always boxed" variables that can
remain polymorphic at the backend, while `CNumber` marks numeric variables
that must be fully specialized before code generation.

unions.name: Decider
unions.comment:
Decision tree for pattern matching.

This matches the structure of Opt.Decider from Compiler.AST.Optimized:

  - Chain carries a list of (Path, Test) pairs for the condition
  - FanOut carries the Path being tested

unions.name: Global
unions.comment: A reference to a top-level definition in a module.

unions.name: LambdaId
unions.comment: Identifier for lambda functions in lambda sets, distinguishing named functions from closures.

unions.name: Literal
unions.comment: Literal values in monomorphized expressions.

unions.name: MainInfo
unions.comment:
Information about the main entry point.

  - Static: A simple main value (Html, Svg, etc.)
  - Dynamic: An application with flags decoder (Browser.element, etc.)

unions.name: MonoChoice
unions.comment: Action to take when a pattern match succeeds.

unions.name: MonoDef
unions.comment: A local definition in monomorphized code.

unions.name: MonoDestructor
unions.comment:
Destructuring pattern for extracting values from data structures.
    Contains the variable name, path to navigate to the value, and the type of the destructured value.

unions.name: MonoExpr
unions.comment: A monomorphized expression with concrete types and explicit closures.

unions.name: MonoGraph
unions.comment: The complete monomorphized program graph containing all specialized definitions.
cases: { nodes : Data.Map.Dict Basics.Int Basics.Int Compiler.AST.Monomorphized.MonoNode, main : Maybe.Maybe Compiler.AST.Monomorphized.MainInfo, registry : Compiler.AST.Monomorphized.SpecializationRegistry }

unions.name: MonoNode
unions.comment: A node in the monomorphized dependency graph representing a specialized definition.

unions.name: MonoPath
unions.comment: Path for navigating into a data structure during destructuring.

unions.name: MonoType
unions.comment:
Monomorphized type used by the MLIR backend.

This type represents the fully elaborated runtime shape of values after
monomorphization. All concrete instantiations of source types (including
primitives, functions, lists, tuples, records, and custom types) must appear
here as `MInt`, `MFloat`, `MList`, `MTuple`, etc.

Type variables only remain in the form of `MVar` with an attached `Constraint`
when their exact runtime type is either:

  - Guaranteed to be represented as a boxed `eco.value` (`CEcoValue`), or
  - A numeric type (`CNumber`) that is waiting to be resolved to `MInt` or
    `MFloat` during specialization.

INVARIANTS BY PHASE:

  - Before codegen:
      - `MVar _ CNumber` is allowed as an intermediate result of
        monomorphization and must be resolved to either `MInt` or `MFloat`
        for any reachable code path that performs numeric operations.
      - `MVar _ CEcoValue` is allowed for positions whose concrete type does
        not affect layout (always boxed) and may remain until codegen.

  - At codegen time:
      - No `MVar _ CNumber` may remain in any reachable `MonoType`. Such
        a case indicates a failed specialization and is a compiler bug.
      - Any remaining `MVar _ CEcoValue` is treated as a boxed `eco.value`
        in the target representation.

The actual specialization to `MInt` or `MFloat` is expected tp be done at call
sites during code generation.

unions.name: SpecKey
unions.comment: Key identifying a unique specialization of a polymorphic function.

aliases.name: ClosureInfo
aliases.comment: Information about a closure including its lambda ID, captured variables, and parameters.
aliases.type: { lambdaId : Compiler.AST.Monomorphized.LambdaId, captures : List.List ( Compiler.Data.Name.Name, Compiler.AST.Monomorphized.MonoExpr, Basics.Bool ), params : List.List ( Compiler.Data.Name.Name, Compiler.AST.Monomorphized.MonoType ) }

aliases.name: CtorLayout
aliases.comment: Runtime layout information for a single constructor variant.

aliases.name: FieldInfo
aliases.comment: Information about a single field in a record or constructor.
aliases.type: { name : Compiler.Data.Name.Name, index : Basics.Int, monoType : Compiler.AST.Monomorphized.MonoType, isUnboxed : Basics.Bool }

aliases.name: RecordLayout
aliases.comment: Runtime layout information for records, including field order and unboxing.
aliases.type: { fieldCount : Basics.Int, unboxedCount : Basics.Int, unboxedBitmap : Basics.Int, fields : List.List Compiler.AST.Monomorphized.FieldInfo }

aliases.name: SpecId
aliases.comment: Unique integer identifier for a function specialization.

aliases.name: SpecializationRegistry
aliases.comment: Registry tracking all function specializations in the program.
aliases.type: { nextId : Basics.Int, mapping : Data.Map.Dict (List.List String.String) (List.List String.String) Compiler.AST.Monomorphized.SpecId, reverseMapping : Data.Map.Dict Basics.Int Basics.Int ( Compiler.AST.Monomorphized.Global, Compiler.AST.Monomorphized.MonoType, Maybe.Maybe Compiler.AST.Monomorphized.LambdaId ) }

aliases.name: TupleLayout
aliases.comment: Runtime layout information for tuples.
aliases.type: { arity : Basics.Int, unboxedBitmap : Basics.Int, elements : List.List ( Compiler.AST.Monomorphized.MonoType, Basics.Bool ) }

name: Compiler.AST.Source

unions.name: Alias
unions.comment: A type alias declaration.

unions.name: Def

unions.name: Module

unions.name: OpenCommentedList
unions.comment:
This represents a list of things that have a clear start delimiter but no
clear end delimiter.
There must be at least one item.
Comments can appear before the last item, or around any other item.
An end-of-line comment can also appear after the last item.

For example:
= a
= a, b, c

TODO: this should be replaced with (Sequence a)

unions.name: Pair
unions.comment:
Represents a delimiter-separated pair.

Comments can appear after the key or before the value.

For example:

key = value
key : value

unions.name: Pattern_
unions.comment: The different kinds of patterns used in destructuring and case expressions.

unions.name: Port
unions.comment: A port declaration for JavaScript interop.

unions.name: Privacy
