unions.name: Type_
unions.comment: The different kinds of type annotations in Elm source code.

unions.name: Union
unions.comment: A union type declaration with type parameters and constructors.

unions.name: Value
unions.comment: A top-level value or function definition.

unions.name: VarType
unions.comment: Distinguishes lowercase variables from uppercase constructors.

aliases.name: AliasData
aliases.comment: Data for a type alias declaration.
aliases.type: { comments : Compiler.AST.Source.FComments, name : Compiler.AST.Source.C2 (Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located Compiler.Data.Name.Name), args : List.List (Compiler.AST.Source.C1 (Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located Compiler.Data.Name.Name)), tipe : Compiler.AST.Source.C1 Compiler.AST.Source.Type }

aliases.name: C0Eol
aliases.comment: Value with optional end-of-line comment after it.

aliases.name: C1
aliases.comment: Value with comments before it.

aliases.name: C1Eol
aliases.comment: Value with comments before it and optional end-of-line comment after it.

aliases.name: C2
aliases.comment: Value with comments before and after it.

aliases.name: C2Eol
aliases.comment: Value with comments before and after it, plus optional end-of-line comment.

aliases.name: Expr
aliases.comment: Expression with source location.

aliases.name: FComments
aliases.comment: A list of formatting comments.

aliases.name: InfixData
aliases.comment: Data for an infix operator declaration.
aliases.type: { op : Compiler.AST.Source.C2 Compiler.Data.Name.Name, associativity : Compiler.AST.Source.C1 Compiler.AST.Utils.Binop.Associativity, precedence : Compiler.AST.Source.C1 Compiler.AST.Utils.Binop.Precedence, name : Compiler.AST.Source.C1 Compiler.Data.Name.Name }

aliases.name: ModuleData
aliases.comment: Data contained in a module, including all top-level declarations.
aliases.type: { syntaxVersion : Compiler.Parse.SyntaxVersion.SyntaxVersion, name : Maybe.Maybe (Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located Compiler.Data.Name.Name), exports : Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located Compiler.AST.Source.Exposing, docs : Compiler.AST.Source.Docs, imports : List.List Compiler.AST.Source.Import, values : List.List (Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located Compiler.AST.Source.Value), unions : List.List (Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located Compiler.AST.Source.Union), aliases : List.List (Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located Compiler.AST.Source.Alias), infixes : List.List (Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located Compiler.AST.Source.Infix), effects : Compiler.AST.Source.Effects }

aliases.name: Pattern
aliases.comment: Pattern with source location.

aliases.name: Type
aliases.comment: Type annotation with source location.

aliases.name: ValueData
aliases.comment: Data for a top-level value definition.
aliases.type: { comments : Compiler.AST.Source.FComments, name : Compiler.AST.Source.C1 (Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located Compiler.Data.Name.Name), args : List.List (Compiler.AST.Source.C1 Compiler.AST.Source.Pattern), body : Compiler.AST.Source.C1 Compiler.AST.Source.Expr, tipe : Maybe.Maybe (Compiler.AST.Source.C1 (Compiler.AST.Source.C2 Compiler.AST.Source.Type)) }

values.name: c0EolMap
values.comment: Map a function over the value in a C0Eol container, preserving end-of-line comment.

values.name: c1Value
values.comment: Extract the value from a C1 container, discarding comments.

values.name: c1map
values.comment: Map a function over the value in a C1 container, preserving comments.

values.name: c2EolMap
values.comment: Map a function over the value in a C2Eol container, preserving comments and end-of-line.

values.name: c2EolValue
values.comment: Extract the value from a C2Eol container, discarding comments and end-of-line.

values.name: c2Value
values.comment: Extract the value from a C2 container, discarding comments.

values.name: c2map
values.comment: Map a function over the value in a C2 container, preserving before and after comments.

values.name: getImportName
values.comment: Extract the imported module name from an Import.

values.name: getName
values.comment: Extract the module name, defaulting to the main module name if unnamed.

values.name: mapPair
values.comment: Map functions over both the key and value in a Pair.
values.type: (a1 -> a2) -> (b1 -> b2) -> Compiler.AST.Source.Pair a1 b1 -> Compiler.AST.Source.Pair a2 b2

values.name: moduleDecoder
values.comment: Decode a Module and all its components from bytes.

values.name: moduleEncoder
values.comment: Encode a Module and all its components to bytes.

values.name: openCommentedListMap
values.comment: Map a function over all elements in an OpenCommentedList.

values.name: sequenceAC2
values.comment: Sequence a list of C2 values, collecting all comments into a single C2.

values.name: typeDecoder
values.comment: Decode a Type with its location from bytes.

values.name: typeEncoder
values.comment: Encode a Type with its location to bytes.

name: Compiler.AST.TypedCanonical

unions.name: CaseBranch
unions.comment: A typed case branch pairing a pattern with a typed expression body.

unions.name: Decls
unions.comment:
A linked list of typed top-level declarations in a module.

  - `Declare` - A non-recursive definition
  - `DeclareRec` - A group of mutually recursive definitions
  - `SaveTheEnvironment` - Sentinel marking the end of declarations

unions.name: Def
unions.comment:
A typed definition.

  - `Def` - A definition without a type annotation
  - `TypedDef` - A definition with a type annotation and free type variables

unions.name: Expr_
unions.comment: A typed expression node containing the original canonical expression and its type.

unions.name: FieldUpdate
unions.comment: A typed field update with region and typed expression.

unions.name: Module
unions.comment: A typed canonical Elm module.

aliases.name: Expr
aliases.comment: A typed expression with source location annotation.

aliases.name: ExprTypes
aliases.comment:
Dictionary mapping node IDs (expressions and patterns) to their canonical types.
This is produced by the solver after constraint solving.

This is an alias for `NodeTypes` to maintain backwards compatibility.

aliases.name: ModuleData
aliases.comment: Internal data for a typed canonical module.
aliases.type: { name : System.TypeCheck.IO.Canonical, exports : Compiler.AST.Canonical.Exports, docs : Compiler.AST.Source.Docs, decls : Compiler.AST.TypedCanonical.Decls, unions : Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Compiler.AST.Canonical.Union, aliases : Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Compiler.AST.Canonical.Alias, binops : Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Compiler.AST.Canonical.Binop, effects : Compiler.AST.Canonical.Effects }

aliases.name: NodeTypes
aliases.comment:
Dictionary mapping node IDs to their canonical types.

Node IDs include both expression IDs and pattern IDs, providing a unified
mapping for all typed AST nodes. This is produced by the solver after
constraint solving.

values.name: fromCanonical
values.comment:
Build a TypedCanonical module from a canonical module and expression type map.

Takes a fully canonicalized module and a dictionary mapping expression IDs to
their inferred types (as produced by `Solve.runWithExprVars`). Returns a
`TypedCanonical.Module` where every expression is paired with its type.

values.name: toTypedExpr
values.comment:
Convert a canonical expression to a typed expression using the type map.

This is the key function for accessing types of subexpressions during
optimization. When the optimizer encounters a `Can.Expr` child (e.g., in
`Can.Lambda args body`), it uses this to wrap it with its type.

name: Compiler.AST.TypedOptimized

unions.name: Choice
unions.comment: Represents the action taken when a pattern match succeeds.

unions.name: Decider
unions.comment: A decision tree for pattern matching, optimized from the canonical AST.

unions.name: Def
unions.comment: A local definition, either a simple value or a tail-recursive function.

unions.name: Destructor
unions.comment: Destructuring pattern that extracts a value from a data structure.

unions.name: EffectsType
unions.comment: The type of effects manager (commands, subscriptions, or both).

unions.name: Expr
unions.comment: Typed optimized expression. Each variant carries its canonical type as the last argument.

unions.name: Global
unions.comment: A reference to a top-level definition in a module.

unions.name: GlobalGraph
unions.comment: A graph of all top-level definitions across multiple modules.

unions.name: LocalGraph
unions.comment: A graph of top-level definitions for a single module.

unions.name: Main
unions.comment: Information about the main entry point of an Elm program.

unions.name: Node
unions.comment: A node in the dependency graph representing a top-level definition.

unions.name: Path
unions.comment: A path describing how to navigate into a data structure for destructuring.

aliases.name: Annotations
aliases.comment: Annotations dictionary - maps definition names to their type schemes

aliases.name: LocalGraphData
aliases.comment: Data structure for a single module's dependency graph.
aliases.type: { main : Maybe.Maybe Compiler.AST.TypedOptimized.Main, nodes : Data.Map.Dict (List.List String.String) Compiler.AST.TypedOptimized.Global Compiler.AST.TypedOptimized.Node, fields : Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Basics.Int, annotations : Compiler.AST.TypedOptimized.Annotations }

values.name: addGlobalGraph
values.comment: Merge two global graphs by unioning their nodes, fields, and annotations.

values.name: addLocalGraph
values.comment: Add a local graph's definitions to a global graph.

values.name: compareGlobal
values.comment: Compare two global references for ordering.

values.name: emptyGlobalGraph
values.comment: Create an empty global graph (alias for `empty`).

values.name: emptyLocalGraph
values.comment: Create an empty local graph with no definitions.

values.name: globalGraphDecoder
values.comment: Decode a global graph from binary format.

values.name: globalGraphEncoder
values.comment: Encode a global graph to binary format.

values.name: localGraphDecoder
values.comment: Decode a local graph from binary format.

values.name: localGraphEncoder
values.comment: Encode a local graph to binary format.

values.name: toComparableGlobal
values.comment: Convert a global reference to a comparable key for use in dictionaries.

values.name: toKernelGlobal
values.comment: Create a global reference to a kernel function.

values.name: typeOf
values.comment: Extract the canonical type from any expression.

name: Compiler.AST.Utils.Binop

unions.name: Associativity
unions.comment:
Associativity determines how operators of the same precedence are grouped.
Left: `a + b + c` is `(a + b) + c`
Non: `a == b == c` is an error
Right: `a :: b :: c` is `a :: (b :: c)`

aliases.name: Precedence
aliases.comment: Operator precedence level, with higher numbers binding more tightly.

values.name: associativityDecoder
values.comment: Decode associativity from binary format.

values.name: associativityEncoder
values.comment: Encode associativity to binary format as an unsigned 8-bit integer.

values.name: precedenceDecoder
values.comment: Decode precedence from binary format.

values.name: precedenceEncoder
values.comment: Encode precedence to binary format.

name: Compiler.AST.Utils.Shader

unions.name: Source
unions.comment: Escaped GLSL shader source code, ready for embedding in JavaScript.

unions.name: Type
unions.comment: GLSL types supported in shaders.

unions.name: Types
unions.comment: Type information for shader inputs: attributes, uniforms, and varyings.

values.name: fromString
values.comment: Create shader source from a raw GLSL string, escaping special characters for JavaScript.

values.name: sourceDecoder
values.comment: Decode shader source from binary format.

values.name: sourceEncoder
values.comment: Encode shader source to binary format.

values.name: toJsStringBuilder
values.comment: Extract the escaped shader source as a string for JavaScript embedding.

values.name: typesDecoder
values.comment: Decode shader type information from binary format.

values.name: typesEncoder
values.comment: Encode shader type information to binary format.

values.name: unescape
values.comment: Reverse the escaping process, converting escaped sequences back to their original characters.

name: Compiler.AST.Utils.Type
comment:
Utilities for manipulating and normalizing canonical type representations.

This module provides functions to work with type aliases and function types in the
canonical AST. The primary operations are dealiasing (expanding type aliases with
their concrete definitions) and delambda (flattening nested function types).


# Type Alias Expansion

@docs dealias, deepDealias, iteratedDealias


# Function Type Utilities

@docs delambda

values.name: dealias
values.comment:
Expand a type alias by substituting its type parameters with concrete types.
Takes a list of (parameter name, concrete type) pairs and the alias definition to expand.

values.name: deepDealias
values.comment: Recursively expand all type aliases in a type, replacing them with their concrete definitions.

values.name: delambda
values.comment:
Flatten a function type into a list of its argument types followed by the return type.
For example, `(a -> b -> c)` becomes `[a, b, c]`.

values.name: iteratedDealias
values.comment:
Expand type aliases at the top level only, iterating until no more aliases remain.
Does not recurse into nested types.

name: Compiler.Canonicalize.Effects
comment:
Canonicalization of effect managers, ports, and their type constraints.

This module handles the canonicalization of Elm's effects system, including:

  - Port declarations and their payload validation
  - Effect manager declarations (Cmd, Sub, and Fx managers)
  - Verification that effect types are valid and properly declared

@docs canonicalize, checkPayload

values.name: canonicalize
values.comment:
Canonicalize effect declarations, including ports and effect managers.

Validates that:

  - Port types are either `Cmd msg` or `Sub msg` with valid payloads
  - Effect managers declare all required functions (init, onEffects, onSelfMsg, cmdMap/subMap)
  - Effect types reference declared union types
values.type: Compiler.Parse.SyntaxVersion.SyntaxVersion -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Env -> List.List (Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located Compiler.AST.Source.Value) -> Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name union -> Compiler.AST.Source.Effects -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Effects.EResult i w Compiler.AST.Canonical.Effects

values.name: checkPayload
values.comment:
Verify that a type can be used as a port payload.

Valid port payloads include:

  - Primitive types (Int, Float, Bool, String)
  - Json.Encode.Value
  - Lists, Arrays, and Maybes containing valid payloads
  - Tuples and records containing valid payloads
  - Bytes.Bytes

Invalid payloads include functions, type variables, and extensible records.

name: Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment

unions.name: Binop
unions.comment: Wrapper type for binary operator information.

unions.name: Ctor
unions.comment:
Represents a constructor: either a record constructor or a union type variant.
Record constructors are special constructors for extensible records.

unions.name: Info
unions.comment:
Information about a name: either specific to one module or ambiguous across multiple.
When the same name is exposed by multiple imports, it becomes ambiguous.

unions.name: Type
unions.comment:
Represents a type definition: either a type alias or a union (custom) type.
The Int tracks the number of type parameters.

unions.name: Var
unions.comment:
Represents a variable in scope: either local, top-level, or imported from another module.
Foreigns tracks when multiple modules expose the same variable name (ambiguous imports).

aliases.name: BinopData
aliases.comment:
Complete information about a binary operator including its precedence,
associativity, and the function it desugars to.
aliases.type: { op : Compiler.Data.Name.Name, home : System.TypeCheck.IO.Canonical, name : Compiler.Data.Name.Name, annotation : Compiler.AST.Canonical.Annotation, associativity : Compiler.AST.Utils.Binop.Associativity, precedence : Compiler.AST.Utils.Binop.Precedence }

aliases.name: EResult
aliases.comment: Result type for environment operations that may produce canonicalization errors.

aliases.name: Env
aliases.comment:
The canonicalization environment tracks all names available in the current scope.
It maintains both exposed (unqualified) and qualified imports, along with local variables.
aliases.type: { home : System.TypeCheck.IO.Canonical, vars : Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Var, types : Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Exposed Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Type, ctors : Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Exposed Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Ctor, binops : Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Exposed Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Binop, q_vars : Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Qualified Compiler.AST.Canonical.Annotation, q_types : Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Qualified Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Type, q_ctors : Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Qualified Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Ctor }

aliases.name: Exposed
aliases.comment:
A map of exposed (unqualified) names to their definitions.
Multiple modules can expose the same name, leading to ambiguity.

aliases.name: Qualified
aliases.comment:
A two-level map for qualified names: module prefix -> name -> definition.
Allows referencing names via `Module.name` syntax.
aliases.type: Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name (Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name (Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Info a))

values.name: addLocals
values.comment:
Add local variable bindings to the environment, checking for shadowing.
Returns an error if any new local shadows an existing local or top-level binding.
Foreign bindings can be shadowed without error.
values.type: Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Region -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Env -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.EResult i w Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Env

values.name: findBinop
values.comment:
Look up a binary operator by its symbol in the environment.
Returns an error if the operator is not found or is ambiguous.
values.type: Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Region -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Env -> Compiler.Data.Name.Name -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.EResult i w Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Binop

values.name: findCtor
values.comment:
Look up an unqualified constructor name in the environment.
Returns an error if the constructor is not found or is ambiguous.
values.type: Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Region -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Env -> Compiler.Data.Name.Name -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.EResult i w Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Ctor

values.name: findCtorQual
values.comment:
Look up a qualified constructor name (e.g., `Maybe.Just`) in the environment.
Returns an error if the module or constructor is not found, or if the constructor is ambiguous.
values.type: Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Region -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Env -> Compiler.Data.Name.Name -> Compiler.Data.Name.Name -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.EResult i w Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Ctor

values.name: findType
values.comment:
Look up an unqualified type name in the environment.
Returns an error if the type is not found or is ambiguous.
values.type: Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Region -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Env -> Compiler.Data.Name.Name -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.EResult i w Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Type

values.name: findTypeQual
values.comment:
Look up a qualified type name (e.g., `Dict.Dict`) in the environment.
Returns an error if the module or type is not found, or if the type is ambiguous.
values.type: Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Region -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Env -> Compiler.Data.Name.Name -> Compiler.Data.Name.Name -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.EResult i w Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Type

values.name: mergeInfo
values.comment:
Merge two Info values, detecting when the same name comes from different modules.
Results in an Ambiguous info if modules differ.
values.type: Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Info a -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Info a -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Info a

name: Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Dups
comment:
Utilities for detecting duplicate names in declarations.

This module provides a generic duplicate-tracking system used throughout
canonicalization to detect and report duplicate:

  - Type names
  - Constructor names
  - Field names in records
  - Variable names
  - Type parameters

@docs Info, ToError, Tracker, checkFields, checkLocatedFields, detect, insert, none, one, union, unions

unions.name: Info
unions.comment: Information about a name occurrence, including its source region and associated value.

aliases.name: ToError
aliases.comment: Function that constructs a duplicate name error from name and two conflicting regions.

aliases.name: Tracker
aliases.comment: Tracks potential duplicate names, storing one or more occurrences of each name.

values.name: checkFields
values.comment:
Check for duplicate field names in a list of fields.

Returns an error if any field name appears more than once, otherwise returns
a dictionary mapping field names to their values.
values.type: List.List ( Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located Compiler.Data.Name.Name, a ) -> Compiler.Reporting.Result.RResult i w Compiler.Reporting.Error.Canonicalize.Error (Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name a)

values.name: checkLocatedFields
values.comment:
Check for duplicate field names in a list of fields, returning located names.

Used when processing record types and patterns where field location information
needs to be preserved.
values.type: List.List ( Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located Compiler.Data.Name.Name, a ) -> Compiler.Reporting.Result.RResult i w Compiler.Reporting.Error.Canonicalize.Error (Data.Map.Dict String.String (Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located Compiler.Data.Name.Name) a)

values.name: detect
values.comment:
Detect duplicate names in a tracker and convert to a dictionary.

Returns an error if any name appears more than once, otherwise returns a dictionary
with one entry per unique name.
values.type: Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Dups.ToError -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Dups.Tracker a -> Compiler.Reporting.Result.RResult i w Compiler.Reporting.Error.Canonicalize.Error (Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name a)

values.name: insert
values.comment:
Insert a name occurrence into a tracker.

If the name already exists, both occurrences are tracked for duplicate detection.

values.name: none
values.comment: Create an empty tracker with no names.

values.name: one
values.comment: Create a tracker with a single name occurrence.

values.name: union
values.comment: Combine two trackers, merging occurrences of the same name.

values.name: unions
values.comment: Combine a list of trackers into a single tracker.

name: Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Foreign
comment:
Build the initial canonicalization environment from imported modules.

This module processes import declarations and builds up the environment containing:

  - Imported values, types, and constructors
  - Qualified and unqualified name mappings
  - Binary operators from imported modules

@docs FResult, createInitialEnv

aliases.name: FResult
aliases.comment: Result type for foreign environment operations that can fail with canonicalization errors.

values.name: createInitialEnv
values.comment:
Create the initial canonicalization environment from a list of imports.

Processes each import declaration to build the environment with:

  - Exposed values, types, and constructors
  - Qualified names for module-prefixed access
  - Binary operators
  - Special handling for kernel imports in kernel packages
values.type: System.TypeCheck.IO.Canonical -> Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Elm.ModuleName.Raw Compiler.Elm.Interface.Interface -> List.List Compiler.AST.Source.Import -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Foreign.FResult i w Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Env

name: Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Local
comment:
Add locally-defined declarations to the canonicalization environment.

This module processes declarations within the current module to add:

  - Top-level value declarations
  - Type aliases and union types
  - Constructors for unions and record type aliases
  - Validation for duplicate names and cyclic type aliases

@docs LResult, add

aliases.name: LResult
aliases.comment: Result type for local environment operations that can fail with canonicalization errors.

values.name: add
values.comment:
Add all local declarations from a module to the environment.

Processes the module's declarations in order:

1.  Types (unions and aliases) - validates names and detects cycles
2.  Values (top-level declarations and ports)
3.  Constructors (from unions and record aliases)

Returns the updated environment along with canonicalized unions and aliases.

name: Compiler.Canonicalize.Expression
comment:
Canonicalize Elm expressions from source AST to canonical AST.

This module handles the transformation of all expression forms including literals,
variables, function applications, let bindings, case expressions, records, and more.
It tracks free variable usage to detect unused bindings and recursive definitions,
performs binary operator precedence resolution, and validates pattern bindings.


# Results and Tracking

@docs EResult, FreeLocals, Uses, IdState


# Canonicalization

@docs canonicalizeWithIds, gatherTypedArgsWithIds


# Validation

@docs verifyBindingsWithIds

unions.name: Uses
unions.comment:
Tracks how many times a variable is used, distinguishing direct from delayed usage.

Direct usage occurs when a variable is referenced in an immediately-evaluated context.
Delayed usage occurs when a variable is captured in a lambda or other delayed context.
This distinction helps detect problematic recursive definitions.

aliases.name: EResult
aliases.comment:
Result type for expression canonicalization that tracks free variable usage.

The info parameter `i` is typically `FreeLocals` during expression canonicalization,
allowing the result to accumulate information about which local variables are referenced.

aliases.name: FreeLocals
aliases.comment:
Dictionary tracking which local variables are used and how they are used.

Maps variable names to their usage counts (direct vs delayed). Used to detect
unused bindings and recursive definitions during canonicalization.

aliases.name: IdState
aliases.comment:
State for tracking expression IDs during canonicalization.
Re-exported from Compiler.Canonicalize.Ids for convenience.

values.name: canonicalizeWithIds
values.comment:
Transform a source expression with ID state threading.

Like canonicalize but also threads an IdState through to assign unique IDs
to each expression. Returns both the canonical expression and the updated state.
values.type: Compiler.Parse.SyntaxVersion.SyntaxVersion -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Env -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Expression.IdState -> Compiler.AST.Source.Expr -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Expression.EResult Compiler.Canonicalize.Expression.FreeLocals (List.List Compiler.Reporting.Warning.Warning) ( Compiler.AST.Canonical.Expr, Compiler.Canonicalize.Expression.IdState )

values.name: gatherTypedArgsWithIds
values.comment:
Match function argument patterns against a type signature, producing typed arguments.

This function processes a type-annotated function definition by pairing each argument
pattern with its corresponding type from the signature. It:

  - Iterates through the function type, extracting argument types from nested lambdas
  - Canonicalizes each argument pattern with IdState threading
  - Pairs each pattern with its type annotation
  - Returns the list of typed argument patterns, the final return type, and updated IdState
  - Reports an error if there are more patterns than types in the signature

This is used when processing function definitions with explicit type annotations.
values.type: Compiler.Parse.SyntaxVersion.SyntaxVersion -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Env -> Compiler.Data.Name.Name -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Expression.IdState -> List.List Compiler.AST.Source.Pattern -> Compiler.AST.Canonical.Type -> Compiler.Data.Index.ZeroBased -> List.List ( Compiler.AST.Canonical.Pattern, Compiler.AST.Canonical.Type ) -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Expression.EResult Compiler.Canonicalize.Pattern.DupsDict w ( ( List.List ( Compiler.AST.Canonical.Pattern, Compiler.AST.Canonical.Type ), Compiler.AST.Canonical.Type ), Compiler.Canonicalize.Expression.IdState )

values.name: verifyBindingsWithIds
values.comment:
Like verifyBindings but handles a result containing ( expr, IdState ).

The info type parameter allows this to be used in different contexts:

  - With info = () when wrapped with directUsageWithIds/delayedUsageWithIds
  - With info = FreeLocals when used directly in addDefNodesWithIds
values.type: Compiler.Reporting.Warning.Context -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Pattern.Bindings -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Expression.EResult Compiler.Canonicalize.Expression.FreeLocals (List.List Compiler.Reporting.Warning.Warning) ( expr, Compiler.Canonicalize.Expression.IdState ) -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Expression.EResult info (List.List Compiler.Reporting.Warning.Warning) ( ( expr, Compiler.Canonicalize.Expression.IdState ), Compiler.Canonicalize.Expression.FreeLocals )

name: Compiler.Canonicalize.Ids
comment:
Shared ID allocation for canonical AST nodes.

This module provides a unified ID space for both expressions and patterns
in the canonical AST. All canonical nodes (expressions and patterns) share
a single incrementing counter to ensure unique IDs across the entire module.


# State

@docs IdState, initialIdState


# Allocation

@docs allocId

aliases.name: IdState
aliases.comment: State for tracking the next available ID.

values.name: allocId
values.comment: Allocate a new ID and return the updated state.

values.name: initialIdState
values.comment: Initial ID state starting at 0.

name: Compiler.Canonicalize.Module
comment:
Canonicalize an entire Elm module, transforming it from source AST to canonical AST.

This module orchestrates the canonicalization of all module components including
value declarations, type definitions, infix operators, exports, and effects. It performs
cycle detection for both type inference and runtime termination analysis.


# Results

@docs MResult


# Canonicalization

@docs canonicalize

aliases.name: MResult
aliases.comment: Type alias for canonicalization results that can accumulate errors and warnings.

values.name: canonicalize
values.comment:
Canonicalize an entire source module into a canonical module.

Transforms a source AST module into a canonical AST module by:

  - Creating the initial environment from imported interfaces
  - Adding local declarations to the environment
  - Canonicalizing all value declarations with cycle detection
  - Canonicalizing effects (ports, managers)
  - Canonicalizing and validating exports
values.type: Compiler.Elm.Package.Name -> Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Elm.ModuleName.Raw Compiler.Elm.Interface.Interface -> Compiler.AST.Source.Module -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Module.MResult i (List.List Compiler.Reporting.Warning.Warning) Compiler.AST.Canonical.Module

name: Compiler.Canonicalize.Pattern
comment:
Canonicalize Elm patterns from source AST to canonical AST.

This module transforms pattern expressions used in function arguments, let bindings,
and case branches. It validates constructor applications, checks for duplicate
bindings within patterns, and tracks all variables bound by each pattern for use
in scope analysis.


# Results and Bindings

@docs PResult, Bindings, DupsDict


# Canonicalization

@docs canonicalizeWithIds, traverseWithIds


# Validation

@docs verify, verifyWithIds

aliases.name: Bindings
aliases.comment:
Dictionary mapping variable names to their canonical names and source regions.

Tracks all variables bound by a pattern for use in scope analysis and error reporting.

aliases.name: DupsDict
aliases.comment:
Tracker for detecting duplicate variable bindings within patterns.

Records each variable name and its source region to enable duplicate detection
and helpful error messages showing both binding locations.

aliases.name: PResult
aliases.comment:
Result type for pattern canonicalization operations.

A specialized result type that threads duplicate tracking information and
canonicalization errors through the pattern analysis pipeline.

values.name: canonicalizeWithIds
values.comment:
Transform a source pattern with ID state threading.

Like canonicalize but also threads an IdState through to assign unique IDs
to each pattern. Returns both the canonical pattern and the updated state.
values.type: Compiler.Parse.SyntaxVersion.SyntaxVersion -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Env -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Ids.IdState -> Compiler.AST.Source.Pattern -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Pattern.PResult Compiler.Canonicalize.Pattern.DupsDict w ( Compiler.AST.Canonical.Pattern, Compiler.Canonicalize.Ids.IdState )

values.name: traverseWithIds
values.comment:
Traverse a list of patterns, threading IdState through each canonicalization.

This is used when canonicalizing multiple patterns that should share the same
ID space, such as lambda arguments or case patterns.
values.type: Compiler.Parse.SyntaxVersion.SyntaxVersion -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Env -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Ids.IdState -> List.List Compiler.AST.Source.Pattern -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Pattern.PResult Compiler.Canonicalize.Pattern.DupsDict w ( List.List Compiler.AST.Canonical.Pattern, Compiler.Canonicalize.Ids.IdState )

values.name: verify
values.comment:
Verify that a pattern has no duplicate bindings and extract all variable bindings.

Takes a canonicalized pattern result and checks for duplicate variable names within
the pattern. Returns both the canonicalized pattern and a dictionary of all bindings
if successful, or errors if duplicates are found.
values.type: Compiler.Reporting.Error.Canonicalize.DuplicatePatternContext -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Pattern.PResult Compiler.Canonicalize.Pattern.DupsDict w a -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Pattern.PResult i w ( a, Compiler.Canonicalize.Pattern.Bindings )

values.name: verifyWithIds
values.comment:
Verify patterns that were canonicalized with ID threading.

This is similar to `verify` but also extracts the IdState from the result tuple.
Use this when patterns were canonicalized with `canonicalizeWithIds`.
values.type: Compiler.Reporting.Error.Canonicalize.DuplicatePatternContext -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Pattern.PResult Compiler.Canonicalize.Pattern.DupsDict w ( a, Compiler.Canonicalize.Ids.IdState ) -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Pattern.PResult i w ( a, Compiler.Canonicalize.Pattern.Bindings, Compiler.Canonicalize.Ids.IdState )

name: Compiler.Canonicalize.Type
comment:
Canonicalize Elm type annotations from source AST to canonical AST.

This module transforms type expressions, validating type constructors, checking
arity of type applications, resolving qualified type names, and collecting free
type variables for use in polymorphic type schemes (Forall quantification).


# Results

@docs CResult


# Canonicalization

@docs canonicalize, toAnnotation

aliases.name: CResult
aliases.comment:
Result type for canonicalization operations.

Wraps the reporting result type with canonicalization-specific error information.

values.name: canonicalize
values.comment:
Canonicalize a source type expression into a canonical type.

Transforms type variables, type constructors, function types, records, tuples,
and units from source AST to canonical AST. Validates type constructor existence,
checks arity of type applications, resolves qualified type names, and handles
syntax version differences (e.g., tuple size restrictions in Elm vs Guida).
values.type: Compiler.Parse.SyntaxVersion.SyntaxVersion -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Env -> Compiler.AST.Source.Type -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Type.CResult i w Compiler.AST.Canonical.Type

values.name: toAnnotation
values.comment:
Convert a source type to a canonical type annotation.

Canonicalizes the given source type and wraps it in a Forall quantifier with all
free type variables collected. This creates a polymorphic type scheme suitable
for top-level type annotations.
values.type: Compiler.Parse.SyntaxVersion.SyntaxVersion -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Environment.Env -> Compiler.AST.Source.Type -> Compiler.Canonicalize.Type.CResult i w Compiler.AST.Canonical.Annotation

name: Compiler.Compile
comment:
Orchestrates the full compilation pipeline from source to optimized artifacts.

This module provides the main entry points for compiling Elm modules. It coordinates
the complete transformation from parsed source code through canonicalization, type
checking, pattern match verification, and optimization.

The compilation pipeline consists of four phases:

1.  **Canonicalization** - Resolves all names to their home modules
2.  **Type Checking** - Infers and verifies types via constraint solving
3.  **Nitpicking** - Verifies pattern match exhaustiveness
4.  **Optimization** - Produces efficient intermediate representation


# Compilation

@docs compile, compileTyped


# Artifacts

@docs Artifacts, TypedArtifacts, TypedArtifactsData

unions.name: Artifacts
unions.comment:
Compilation artifacts produced by the standard compilation pipeline.

Contains the canonical AST, type annotations for all definitions, and the
optimized local graph suitable for JavaScript code generation.

unions.name: TypedArtifacts
unions.comment: Wrapper for typed compilation artifacts.

aliases.name: TypedArtifactsData
aliases.comment:
Extended compilation artifacts with typed optimization for MLIR backend.

In addition to standard artifacts, includes a typed optimization graph that
preserves full type information throughout the optimization process. This
enables type-directed optimizations and direct lowering to MLIR.
aliases.type: { canonical : Compiler.AST.Canonical.Module, annotations : Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Compiler.AST.Canonical.Annotation, objects : Compiler.AST.Optimized.LocalGraph, typedObjects : Compiler.AST.TypedOptimized.LocalGraph }

values.name: compile
values.comment:
Compiles an Elm module through the complete pipeline.

Executes all compilation phases in sequence:

1.  Canonicalization - resolves names and imports
2.  Type checking - infers and verifies types
3.  Pattern match analysis - ensures exhaustiveness
4.  Optimization - produces efficient intermediate representation

Returns artifacts suitable for JavaScript code generation.
values.type: Compiler.Elm.Package.Name -> Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Elm.ModuleName.Raw Compiler.Elm.Interface.Interface -> Compiler.AST.Source.Module -> Task.Task Basics.Never (Result.Result Compiler.Reporting.Error.Error Compiler.Compile.Artifacts)

values.name: compileTyped
values.comment:
Compiles an Elm module with typed optimization for native code generation.

Performs all standard compilation phases plus typed optimization, producing:

  - `Opt.LocalGraph` - Standard optimized IR for JavaScript backend
  - `TOpt.LocalGraph` - Typed optimized IR with preserved type information

The typed optimization phase preserves type information needed for monomorphization
and direct lowering to MLIR/LLVM.
values.type: Compiler.Elm.Package.Name -> Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Elm.ModuleName.Raw Compiler.Elm.Interface.Interface -> Compiler.AST.Source.Module -> Task.Task Basics.Never (Result.Result Compiler.Reporting.Error.Error Compiler.Compile.TypedArtifacts)

name: Compiler.Data.Bag
comment:
An efficient data structure for accumulating elements with constant-time append operations.

A Bag is a binary tree structure that allows O(1) appending of bags and O(n) conversion to a list.
This is useful for collecting results during tree traversals without paying list concatenation costs.

@docs Bag, append, empty, one, toList

unions.name: Bag
unions.comment: A bag data structure that can be empty, contain a single element, or combine two bags.

values.name: append
values.comment: Appends two bags together in O(1) time by creating a Two node that combines them.
values.type: Compiler.Data.Bag.Bag a -> Compiler.Data.Bag.Bag a -> Compiler.Data.Bag.Bag a

values.name: empty
values.comment: Creates an empty bag.

values.name: one
values.comment: Creates a bag containing a single element.

values.name: toList
values.comment: Converts a bag to a list by flattening the tree structure.

name: Compiler.Data.Index

unions.name: VerifiedList
unions.comment:
Result of an indexed zip operation that verifies list lengths match.
LengthMatch contains the successfully zipped list when lengths are equal.
LengthMismatch contains the actual lengths of both lists when they differ.

unions.name: ZeroBased
unions.comment:
A type-safe wrapper for zero-based indices.
Distinguishes indices from arbitrary integers to prevent confusion.

values.name: first
values.comment: The first index (0 in zero-based indexing).

values.name: indexedMap
values.comment:
Map over a list with zero-based indices provided to the mapping function.
The function receives both the index and the element at that position.
values.type: (Compiler.Data.Index.ZeroBased -> a -> b) -> List.List a -> List.List b

values.name: indexedZipWith
values.comment:
Zip two lists with a function that receives the zero-based index and both elements.
Returns LengthMatch with the result if both lists have the same length, or
LengthMismatch with both list lengths if they differ.
values.type: (Compiler.Data.Index.ZeroBased -> a -> b -> c) -> List.List a -> List.List b -> Compiler.Data.Index.VerifiedList c

values.name: next
values.comment: Get the next index after the given one (increment by 1).

values.name: second
values.comment: The second index (1 in zero-based indexing).

values.name: third
values.comment: The third index (2 in zero-based indexing).

values.name: toHuman
values.comment:
Convert a zero-based index to a human-readable integer (1-indexed).
Returns the index plus one, suitable for display to users.

values.name: toMachine
values.comment:
Convert a zero-based index to a machine integer (0-indexed).
Returns the raw integer value suitable for array/list indexing.

values.name: zeroBasedDecoder
values.comment:
Decode a zero-based index from bytes.
Reads an integer and wraps it in the ZeroBased type.

values.name: zeroBasedEncoder
values.comment: Encode a zero-based index to bytes as an integer.

name: Compiler.Data.Map.Utils

values.name: any
values.comment: Checks if any value in the dictionary satisfies the given predicate.
values.type: (v -> Basics.Bool) -> Data.Map.Dict c k v -> Basics.Bool

values.name: fromKeys
values.comment: Creates a dictionary from a list of keys by applying a function to each key to generate its value.
values.type: (comparable -> v) -> List.List comparable -> Data.Map.Dict comparable comparable v

values.name: fromKeysA

A string-based name used throughout the compiler for identifiers, module names, and type names.
This is a simple type alias to String for clarity and type safety.

Create a unique name from multiple names by prefixing the first name with "\_M$".

values.comment:
Create a type variable name with an index suffix. If index is 0, returns the name unchanged.
If the name ends with a digit, adds an underscore before the index (e.g., "a2" + 3 -> "a2\_3").

values.comment:
Generate a type variable name from a scheme index (0 -> "a", 1 -> "b", ..., 26 -> "a26", etc.).
Uses lowercase letters with numeric suffixes for indices beyond 25.

name: Compiler.Data.NonEmptyList

unions.comment: A non-empty list containing a head element and a (possibly empty) tail.

values.name: cons
values.comment: Add an element to the end of a non-empty list.

values.name: foldr
values.comment: Reduce a non-empty list from the right, combining elements with a function.
values.type: (a -> b -> b) -> b -> Compiler.Data.NonEmptyList.Nonempty a -> b

values.name: map
values.comment: Apply a function to every element in a non-empty list.

values.name: singleton
values.comment: Create a non-empty list containing a single element.

values.comment: Sort a non-empty list by a function that produces a comparable value for each element.

values.name: toList
values.comment: Convert a non-empty list to a standard list.

name: Compiler.Data.OneOrMore
comment:
A binary tree structure that guarantees at least one element.

Unlike NonEmptyList which is a linear structure, OneOrMore represents a binary
tree where each node can contain either a single value or two subtrees. This
structure is useful for representing hierarchical data with guaranteed non-emptiness.


# Type

@docs OneOrMore


# Construction

@docs one, more


# Transformations


# Extraction

@docs destruct, getFirstTwo

unions.name: OneOrMore
unions.comment:
A binary tree structure that guarantees at least one element.

Can be either:

  - `One a`: A leaf node containing a single value
  - `More (OneOrMore a) (OneOrMore a)`: A branch node containing two subtrees

values.name: destruct
values.comment:
Flatten the OneOrMore structure by applying a function to the leftmost element
and a list of remaining elements (traversed left-to-right, depth-first).
values.type: (a -> List.List a -> b) -> Compiler.Data.OneOrMore.OneOrMore a -> b

values.name: getFirstTwo
values.comment:
Extract the first element from each of two OneOrMore structures, returning them
as a tuple. Traverses leftmost path to find the first element in each tree.
values.type: Compiler.Data.OneOrMore.OneOrMore a -> Compiler.Data.OneOrMore.OneOrMore a -> ( a, a )

values.name: more
values.comment: Combine two OneOrMore structures into a single binary tree node.
values.type: Compiler.Data.OneOrMore.OneOrMore a -> Compiler.Data.OneOrMore.OneOrMore a -> Compiler.Data.OneOrMore.OneOrMore a

values.name: one
values.comment: Create a OneOrMore structure containing a single element.

name: Compiler.Elm.Compiler.Imports
comment:
Default imports for all Elm modules.

Defines the implicit imports that are automatically available in every Elm module
without explicit import statements. Includes core types like List, Maybe, Result,
and the Basics module functions.


# Defaults

@docs defaults

values.name: defaults
values.comment:
Returns the list of default imports automatically available in every Elm module.
Includes Basics (open), core types like List, Maybe, Result, and platform types.

unions.name: Alias

unions.comment: Debug metadata containing a message type and all transitively referenced type aliases and unions.

unions.comment:
Simplified type representation for documentation and debug metadata.
Represents Elm types including functions, variables, type constructors, records, unit, and tuples.

Collection of all types (unions and aliases) available from a set of modules.

name: Compiler.Elm.Constraint

unions.name: Constraint
unions.comment:
Represents a version constraint as a range with lower and upper bounds.
Each bound can be inclusive (<=) or exclusive (<).

unions.name: Error
unions.comment:
Represents errors that can occur when parsing constraint strings.
BadFormat indicates a syntax error at the given row and column.
InvalidRange indicates the lower bound is not less than the upper bound.

values.name: anything
values.comment: Creates a constraint that accepts any valid version (1.0.0 through max version).

values.name: decoder
values.comment:
Decodes a constraint from a JSON string.
Returns an Error if the format is invalid or the range is malformed.

values.name: defaultElm
values.comment:
Returns the default Elm version constraint.
For major version 1+, constrains until the next major version.
For major version 0, constrains until the next minor version.

values.name: encode
values.comment: Encodes a constraint as a JSON string value.

values.name: exactly
values.comment: Creates a constraint that matches exactly one specific version.

values.name: goodElm
values.comment: Checks whether the current Elm compiler version satisfies the constraint.

values.name: intersect
values.comment:
Computes the intersection of two constraints.
Returns Nothing if the constraints do not overlap, otherwise returns
a new constraint representing the overlapping range.

values.name: lowerBound
values.comment: Extracts the lower bound version from a constraint.

values.name: satisfies
values.comment:
Checks whether a given version satisfies the constraint.
Returns True if the version falls within the constraint's range.

values.name: toChars
values.comment:
Converts a constraint to its string representation.
Format: "lower <= v < upper" or similar based on operators.

values.name: untilNextMajor
values.comment:
Creates a constraint from the given version up to (but not including) the next major version.
Example: untilNextMajor 1.2.3 creates constraint "1.2.3 <= v < 2.0.0"

values.name: untilNextMinor
values.comment:
Creates a constraint from the given version up to (but not including) the next minor version.
Example: untilNextMinor 1.2.3 creates constraint "1.2.3 <= v < 1.3.0"
