values.comment: Extracts documentation from a canonical AST module, validating that all exported items have comments and type annotations.

values.name: jsonEncoder
values.comment: Encodes Documentation to a standard Json.Encode.Value for external consumption.

name: Compiler.Elm.Interface

unions.name: Alias
unions.comment:
Represents a type alias's visibility in the interface. Public aliases are exported,
while private aliases are only available within the defining module.

unions.name: Binop
unions.comment: Wrapper type for binary operator data.

unions.name: DependencyInterface
unions.comment:
Represents how a module's interface is exposed to dependencies. Public interfaces
expose the full API, while private interfaces only expose type definitions without values.

unions.name: Interface
unions.comment:
Wrapper type for module interface data, representing the complete public API
surface of a compiled Elm module.

unions.name: Union
unions.comment:
Represents a union type's visibility in the interface. Open unions export all
constructors, closed unions hide constructors, and private unions are not exported.

aliases.name: BinopData
aliases.comment:
Record containing all information about a binary operator, including its name,
type annotation, associativity (left/right), and precedence level.
aliases.type: { name : Compiler.Data.Name.Name, annotation : Compiler.AST.Canonical.Annotation, associativity : Compiler.AST.Utils.Binop.Associativity, precedence : Compiler.AST.Utils.Binop.Precedence }

aliases.name: InterfaceData
aliases.comment:
Record containing the complete interface data for a module, including its package
home, exported values with their type annotations, union types, type aliases, and binary operators.
aliases.type: { home : Compiler.Elm.Package.Name, values : Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Compiler.AST.Canonical.Annotation, unions : Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Compiler.Elm.Interface.Union, aliases : Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Compiler.Elm.Interface.Alias, binops : Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Compiler.Elm.Interface.Binop }

values.name: dependencyInterfaceDecoder
values.comment:
Decodes a dependency interface from binary format, reconstructing either a public
or private interface based on the encoded type tag.

values.name: dependencyInterfaceEncoder
values.comment:
Encodes a dependency interface to binary format, handling both public and private
interface variants with appropriate type tags.

values.name: extractAlias
values.comment:
Extracts the underlying canonical type alias from any alias visibility wrapper,
regardless of whether it is public or private.

values.name: extractUnion
values.comment:
Extracts the underlying canonical union type from any union visibility wrapper,
regardless of whether it is open, closed, or private.

values.name: fromModule
values.comment:
Constructs an interface from a canonical module, extracting only the exported values,
types, aliases, and operators based on the module's export list.
values.type: Compiler.Elm.Package.Name -> Compiler.AST.Canonical.Module -> Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Compiler.AST.Canonical.Annotation -> Compiler.Elm.Interface.Interface

values.name: interfaceDecoder
values.comment:
Decodes an interface from binary format, reconstructing the full interface structure
from serialized bytes.

values.name: interfaceEncoder
values.comment: Encodes an interface to binary format for serialization to disk or network transmission.

values.name: private
values.comment:
Creates a private dependency interface, exposing only type definitions (unions and aliases)
without values or operators. Used for dependency cycles where only types are needed.

values.name: privatize
values.comment:
Converts a dependency interface to its private form, keeping only type definitions.
If already private, returns the interface unchanged.

values.name: public
values.comment:
Creates a public dependency interface, exposing the full module API including all
exported values, types, and operators.

values.name: toPublicAlias
values.comment:
Converts a type alias to its public representation. Returns Just for public aliases
and Nothing for private aliases.

values.name: toPublicUnion
values.comment:
Converts a union type to its public representation. Open unions expose all constructors,
closed unions expose the type but hide constructors, and private unions are not exposed.

name: Compiler.Elm.Kernel

unions.name: Chunk
unions.comment:
Represents a parsed chunk of kernel JavaScript code.

Kernel code is parsed into chunks that represent different types of content:

  - JS: Raw JavaScript code
  - ElmVar: Reference to an Elm variable from another module
  - JsVar: Reference to a JavaScript variable from a kernel module
  - ElmField: Reference to an Elm record field (\_\_$fieldName)
  - JsField: Reference to a JavaScript field by index
  - JsEnum: Reference to an enumeration value by index
  - Debug: Conditional compilation marker for debug mode
  - Prod: Conditional compilation marker for production mode

unions.name: Content
unions.comment:
Represents the parsed content of a kernel module file.

Contains the list of imports from the kernel module header comment and the parsed
chunks representing the JavaScript code body.

aliases.name: Foreigns
aliases.comment:
Maps module names to their package names.

Used to resolve imported Elm modules to their canonical package locations when
processing kernel module imports.

values.name: chunkDecoder
values.comment:
Decode a Chunk from bytes during deserialization.

Reads the tag byte to determine the chunk type, then decodes the corresponding data.
Used for loading cached kernel module artifacts.

values.name: chunkEncoder
values.comment:
Encode a Chunk to bytes for serialization.

Each chunk type is encoded with a tag byte followed by its data. Used for caching
parsed kernel modules in artifact files.

values.name: countFields
values.comment:
Count the number of times each Elm field is referenced in a list of chunks.

Returns a dictionary mapping field names to their occurrence counts. Only ElmField
chunks are counted; all other chunk types are ignored.
values.type: List.List Compiler.Elm.Kernel.Chunk -> Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Basics.Int

values.name: fromByteString
values.comment:
Parse a kernel module file from a string.

Expects kernel files to start with a comment block containing imports, followed by
JavaScript code with special tags (\_\_x patterns) that reference Elm and JS variables,
fields, and conditional compilation markers. Returns Nothing if parsing fails.

name: Compiler.Elm.Licenses

unions.name: License
unions.comment: Represents a validated OSI-approved SPDX license identifier.

values.name: bsd3
values.comment: The BSD 3-Clause license, commonly used in Elm packages.

values.name: decoder
values.comment:
Decodes and validates a license string, providing suggestions if the license is not recognized.
The error callback receives a list of suggested valid license identifiers.

values.name: encode
values.comment: Encodes a license as a JSON string containing its SPDX identifier.

name: Compiler.Elm.Magnitude
comment:
Semantic versioning magnitude types.

Represents the three levels of version changes in semantic versioning:
PATCH for bug fixes, MINOR for backward-compatible additions, and MAJOR
for breaking changes.


# Types

@docs Magnitude


# Operations

@docs compare, toChars

unions.name: Magnitude
unions.comment: Represents the severity level of a version change in semantic versioning.

values.name: compare
values.comment: Compares two magnitude values, ordering them from least severe (PATCH) to most severe (MAJOR).

values.name: toChars
values.comment: Converts a magnitude value to its string representation (e.g., PATCH -> "PATCH").

name: Compiler.Elm.ModuleName

aliases.name: Raw
aliases.comment:
A raw module name represented as a dotted identifier string.
Examples: "List", "Dict.Extra", "Html.Attributes"

values.name: array
values.comment: Canonical name for the Array module from elm/core.

values.name: basics
values.comment: Canonical name for the Basics module from elm/core.

values.name: bytes
values.comment: Canonical name for the Bytes module from elm/bytes.

values.name: canonicalDecoder
values.comment: Decode a canonical module name from binary format, including package name and module name.

values.name: canonicalEncoder
values.comment: Encode a canonical module name to binary format, including package name and module name.

values.name: char
values.comment: Canonical name for the Char module from elm/core.

values.name: cmd
values.comment: Canonical name for the Platform.Cmd module from elm/core.

values.name: compareCanonical
values.comment: Compare two canonical module names, first by module name then by package name.

values.name: debug
values.comment: Canonical name for the Debug module from elm/core.

values.name: decoder
values.comment: Decode a raw module name from a JSON string, validating it matches module name syntax.

values.name: dict
values.comment: Canonical name for the Dict module from elm/core.

values.name: encode
values.comment: Encode a raw module name as a JSON string.

values.name: jsonDecode
values.comment: Canonical name for the Json.Decode module from elm/json.

values.name: jsonEncode
values.comment: Canonical name for the Json.Encode module from elm/json.

values.name: list
values.comment: Canonical name for the List module from elm/core.

values.name: matrix4
values.comment: Canonical name for the Math.Matrix4 module from elm-explorations/linear-algebra.

values.name: maybe
values.comment: Canonical name for the Maybe module from elm/core.

values.name: platform
values.comment: Canonical name for the Platform module from elm/core.

values.name: rawDecoder
values.comment: Decode a raw module name from binary format as a string.

values.name: rawEncoder
values.comment: Encode a raw module name to binary format as a string.

values.name: result
values.comment: Canonical name for the Result module from elm/core.

values.name: string
values.comment: Canonical name for the String module from elm/core.

values.name: sub
values.comment: Canonical name for the Platform.Sub module from elm/core.

values.name: texture
values.comment: Canonical name for the WebGL.Texture module from elm-explorations/webgl.

values.name: toComparableCanonical
values.comment:
Convert a canonical module name to a comparable list of strings [author, project, name].
Useful for sorting and comparison in data structures.

values.name: toFilePath
values.comment:
Convert a raw module name to a file path by replacing dots with slashes.
Example: "Html.Attributes" becomes "Html/Attributes"

values.name: toHyphenPath
values.comment:
Convert a raw module name to a hyphenated path by replacing dots with hyphens.
Example: "Html.Attributes" becomes "Html-Attributes"

values.name: tuple
values.comment: Canonical name for the Tuple module from elm/core.

values.name: vector2
values.comment: Canonical name for the Math.Vector2 module from elm-explorations/linear-algebra.

values.name: vector3
values.comment: Canonical name for the Math.Vector3 module from elm-explorations/linear-algebra.

values.name: vector4
values.comment: Canonical name for the Math.Vector4 module from elm-explorations/linear-algebra.

values.name: virtualDom
values.comment: Canonical name for the VirtualDom module from elm/virtual-dom.

values.name: webgl
values.comment: Canonical name for the WebGL module from elm-explorations/webgl.

name: Compiler.Elm.Package

aliases.name: Author
aliases.comment: Represents the author portion of a package name (e.g., "elm" in "elm/core").

aliases.name: Name
aliases.comment:
Represents a package name in the form of (Author, Project).
This has been simplified from `Name Author Project` as part of the work for
`System.TypeCheck.IO`.

aliases.name: Project
aliases.comment: Represents the project portion of a package name (e.g., "core" in "elm/core").

values.name: browser
values.comment: The "elm/browser" package name.

values.name: bytes
values.comment: The "elm/bytes" package name.

values.name: compareName
values.comment: Compare two package names lexicographically, first by author then by project.

values.name: core
values.comment: The "elm/core" package name.

values.name: decoder
values.comment: JSON decoder for package names. Expects a string in "author/project" format.

values.name: dummyName
values.comment: A dummy package name used as a placeholder ("author/project").

values.name: encode
values.comment: Encode a package name as a JSON string value.

values.name: html
values.comment: The "elm/html" package name.

values.name: isKernel
values.comment: Check if a package is a kernel package (authored by "elm" or "elm-explorations").

values.name: json
values.comment: The "elm/json" package name.

values.name: kernel
values.comment: The "elm/kernel" package name.

values.name: keyDecoder
values.comment:
JSON key decoder for package names used in JSON objects.
Accepts a function to create error values from row and column positions.

values.name: linearAlgebra
values.comment: The "elm-explorations/linear-algebra" package name.

values.name: nameDecoder
values.comment: Binary decoder for package names. Decodes two consecutive strings as (author, project).

values.name: nameEncoder
values.comment: Binary encoder for package names. Encodes author and project as consecutive strings.

values.name: nearbyNames
values.comment:
Find up to 4 package names from the given list that are most similar to the target name.
Uses Levenshtein distance to measure similarity, with special handling for "elm" and
"elm-explorations" authors (treated as distance 0).

values.name: parser
values.comment:
Parser for package names in "author/project" format.
Enforces naming rules: author starts with alphanumeric, project starts with lowercase,
both can contain dashes (but not consecutive), and must be under 256 characters.

values.name: random
values.comment: The "elm/random" package name.

values.name: suggestions
values.comment:
A dictionary mapping common module names to the packages that contain them.
Used to suggest which package to install when a module is missing.

values.name: test
values.comment: The "elm-explorations/test" package name.

values.name: time
values.comment: The "elm/time" package name.

values.name: toChars
values.comment: Convert a package name to a character string in the form "author/project".

values.name: toJsonString
values.comment: Convert a package name to a JSON-compatible string in the form "author/project".

values.name: toString
values.comment: Convert a package name to its string representation in the form "author/project".

values.name: toUrl
values.comment: Convert a package name to a URL-friendly string in the form "author/project".

values.name: virtualDom
values.comment: The "elm/virtual-dom" package name.

values.name: webgl
values.comment: The "elm-explorations/webgl" package name.

name: Compiler.Elm.String
comment:
String escape sequence handling for Elm source code.

Processes string chunks with escape sequences and unicode code points,
converting them into properly escaped JavaScript string literals.


# Types

@docs Chunk


# Operations

@docs fromChunks

unions.name: Chunk
unions.comment:
Represents a portion of a string literal: a slice of the original source,
an escape sequence, or a unicode code point.

values.name: fromChunks
values.comment:
Converts a list of string chunks into a properly escaped JavaScript string literal.
Handles unicode code points and escape sequences.

name: Compiler.Elm.Version

unions.name: Version
unions.comment: Represents a semantic version with major, minor, and patch components.

values.name: bumpMajor
values.comment:
Increment the major version number by 1 and reset minor and patch to 0 (e.g., 1.2.3 becomes 2.0.0).
Used for backwards-incompatible API changes.

values.name: bumpMinor
values.comment:
Increment the minor version number by 1 and reset patch to 0 (e.g., 1.2.3 becomes 1.3.0).
Used for backwards-compatible new features.

values.name: bumpPatch
values.comment:
Increment the patch version number by 1 (e.g., 1.2.3 becomes 1.2.4).
Used for backwards-compatible bug fixes.

values.name: compare
values.comment:
Compare two versions following semantic versioning rules.
Returns LT if the first version is less than the second, GT if greater, EQ if equal.

values.name: compiler
values.comment: The version of this compiler implementation. Currently returns 1.0.0.

values.name: decoder
values.comment:
Decode a Version from a JSON string using the custom parser.
Returns a decoder that produces error positions on parse failure.

values.name: elmCompiler
values.comment: The version of the Elm compiler this implementation targets: 0.19.1.

values.name: encode
values.comment: Encode a Version to a JSON string value in the format "major.minor.patch".

values.name: major
values.comment: Extract the major version number from a Version.

values.name: max
values.comment: Return the larger of two versions according to semantic versioning order.

values.name: maxVersion
values.comment: The maximum representable version (2147483647.0.0), using the maximum 32-bit signed integer.

values.name: min
values.comment: Return the smaller of two versions according to semantic versioning order.

values.name: one
values.comment: Version 1.0.0, commonly used as the initial package version.

values.name: parser
values.comment:
Parse a semantic version string in the format "major.minor.patch".
Each component must be a valid non-negative integer. Leading zeros are only allowed for "0".

values.name: toChars
values.comment: Convert a Version to its string representation in the format "major.minor.patch".

values.name: toComparable
values.comment: Convert a Version to a comparable tuple for use in sorting and comparison operations.

values.name: versionDecoder
values.comment: Decode a Version from a binary format expecting three consecutive integers (major, minor, patch).

values.name: versionEncoder
values.comment: Encode a Version to a binary format as three consecutive integers (major, minor, patch).

name: Compiler.Generate.CodeGen
comment:
Backend interface definitions for code generation.

This module defines the abstract interface that all compiler backends must implement.
It supports three different backend types based on the level of type information needed:

1.  CodeGen - Standard backends working with optimized AST (JavaScript)
2.  TypedCodeGen - Backends needing full type information (MLIR with type-directed optimizations)
3.  MonoCodeGen - Backends working with fully monomorphized IR (MLIR production)


# Output Types

@docs Output, outputToString


# Source Maps

@docs SourceMaps


# Main Entry Points

@docs Mains


# Backend Interfaces

@docs CodeGen, MonoCodeGen

unions.name: Output
unions.comment: Output from code generation - either a string or binary data.

unions.name: SourceMaps
unions.comment: Configuration for source map generation - either disabled or enabled with module source content.

aliases.name: CodeGen
aliases.comment: The CodeGen interface that standard backends must implement for full program generation, REPL evaluation, and browser REPL endpoints.
aliases.type: { generate : { sourceMaps : Compiler.Generate.CodeGen.SourceMaps, leadingLines : Basics.Int, mode : Compiler.Generate.Mode.Mode, graph : Compiler.AST.Optimized.GlobalGraph, mains : Compiler.Generate.CodeGen.Mains } -> Compiler.Generate.CodeGen.Output, generateForRepl : { ansi : Basics.Bool, localizer : Compiler.Reporting.Render.Type.Localizer.Localizer, graph : Compiler.AST.Optimized.GlobalGraph, home : System.TypeCheck.IO.Canonical, name : Compiler.Data.Name.Name, annotation : Compiler.AST.Canonical.Annotation } -> Compiler.Generate.CodeGen.Output, generateForReplEndpoint : { localizer : Compiler.Reporting.Render.Type.Localizer.Localizer, graph : Compiler.AST.Optimized.GlobalGraph, home : System.TypeCheck.IO.Canonical, maybeName : Maybe.Maybe Compiler.Data.Name.Name, annotation : Compiler.AST.Canonical.Annotation } -> Compiler.Generate.CodeGen.Output }

aliases.name: Mains
aliases.comment: Map from module names to their main entry points for standard optimized AST.

aliases.name: MonoCodeGen
aliases.comment: Backend interface for code generators that work with fully monomorphized IR where all polymorphism has been resolved to concrete types.
aliases.type: { generate : { sourceMaps : Compiler.Generate.CodeGen.SourceMaps, leadingLines : Basics.Int, mode : Compiler.Generate.Mode.Mode, graph : Compiler.AST.Monomorphized.MonoGraph } -> Compiler.Generate.CodeGen.Output }

values.name: outputToString
values.comment: Extract the string content from an Output value.

name: Compiler.Generate.CodeGen.JavaScript
comment:
JavaScript code generation backend.

This module provides the JavaScript backend implementation that conforms to the CodeGen
interface. It generates optimized JavaScript code from the optimized AST, with support
for full program compilation, REPL evaluation, and browser-based REPL endpoints.

The backend delegates to the Compiler.Generate.JavaScript module for the actual code
generation logic.


# Backend

@docs backend

values.name: backend
values.comment: The JavaScript backend implementation that generates optimized JavaScript from the standard optimized AST.

name: Compiler.Generate.CodeGen.MLIR
comment:
MLIR code generation backend for the Monomorphized IR.

This backend generates MLIR from fully specialized, monomorphic code.
All polymorphism has been resolved and layout information is embedded
in the types.


# Backend

@docs backend

values.name: backend
values.comment: The MLIR backend that generates MLIR code from fully monomorphized IR with all polymorphism resolved.

name: Compiler.Generate.Html
comment:
HTML template generation for standalone Elm programs.

This module provides functions to wrap generated JavaScript in a complete HTML document
that can be opened directly in a browser. The template includes basic styling, error
handling, and initialization logic.


# Template Generation

@docs sandwich, leadingLines

values.name: leadingLines
values.comment: Number of leading lines in the HTML template before the JavaScript code begins. Used for accurate source map generation.

values.name: sandwich
values.comment: Wrap generated JavaScript code in a complete HTML document that includes error handling and initialization logic.

name: Compiler.Generate.JavaScript
comment:
JavaScript code generation for the Elm compiler.

This module is the entry point for generating JavaScript from optimized Elm AST.
It takes an optimized global graph and produces executable JavaScript code, handling:

  - Module code generation with dependency resolution
  - REPL evaluation with type printing
  - Source map generation for debugging
  - Kernel JavaScript integration


# Code Generation

@docs generate, generateForRepl, generateForReplEndpoint

values.name: generate
values.comment:
Generates JavaScript code from an optimized global graph.

Takes the optimized AST and produces executable JavaScript wrapped in an IIFE,
including all dependencies, kernel functions, and main exports.
values.type: Compiler.Generate.CodeGen.SourceMaps -> Basics.Int -> Compiler.Generate.Mode.Mode -> Compiler.AST.Optimized.GlobalGraph -> Compiler.Generate.CodeGen.Mains -> String.String

values.name: generateForRepl
values.comment:
Generates JavaScript for REPL evaluation with type display.

Produces code that evaluates an expression and prints its value and type
to the console, formatted for terminal display with optional ANSI colors.
values.type: Basics.Bool -> Compiler.Reporting.Render.Type.Localizer.Localizer -> Compiler.AST.Optimized.GlobalGraph -> System.TypeCheck.IO.Canonical -> Compiler.Data.Name.Name -> Compiler.AST.Canonical.Annotation -> String.String

values.name: generateForReplEndpoint
values.comment:
Generates JavaScript for REPL evaluation in a web worker.

Similar to generateForRepl but outputs via postMessage for use in browser
environments. Returns a message object with name, value, and type fields.
values.type: Compiler.Reporting.Render.Type.Localizer.Localizer -> Compiler.AST.Optimized.GlobalGraph -> System.TypeCheck.IO.Canonical -> Maybe.Maybe Compiler.Data.Name.Name -> Compiler.AST.Canonical.Annotation -> String.String

name: Compiler.Generate.JavaScript.Builder

unions.name: Builder
unions.comment:
Opaque builder type for accumulating JavaScript output.

Wraps BuilderData to provide an opaque interface for building JavaScript code
with source map support.

unions.name: Case
unions.comment:
Switch statement case or default clause.

Represents a single case in a switch statement, with an expression to match
or a default case that handles all other values.

unions.name: Expr
unions.comment:
JavaScript expression AST node.

Represents all JavaScript expression forms including literals (strings, numbers, booleans),
arrays, objects, function calls, property access, operators, and function expressions.
Includes both tracked variants (with source positions for source map generation) and
untracked variants for performance.

unions.name: InfixOp
unions.comment:
JavaScript infix (binary) operators.

Includes arithmetic, comparison, logical, and bitwise operators.

unions.name: LValue
unions.comment:
Left-hand side value in an assignment expression.

Can be either a simple variable reference or an array/object index expression.

unions.name: Mapping
unions.comment:
Source map mapping entry.

Wraps MappingData to provide an opaque type for source map entries.

unions.name: PrefixOp
unions.comment:
JavaScript prefix (unary) operators.

Includes logical not, arithmetic negation, and bitwise complement.

unions.name: Stmt
unions.comment:
JavaScript statement AST node.

Represents all JavaScript statement forms including blocks, control flow (if, switch, while,
try-catch), variable declarations, function declarations, and expression statements.

aliases.name: BuilderData
aliases.comment:
Internal state for the JavaScript code builder.

Tracks the generated JavaScript output, current line and column position for
source map generation, and accumulated kernel dependencies.
aliases.type: { revKernels : List.List String.String, revBuilders : String.String, currentLine : Basics.Int, currentCol : Basics.Int, mappings : List.List Compiler.Generate.JavaScript.Builder.Mapping }

aliases.name: MappingData
aliases.comment:
Source map mapping entry data.

Records a correspondence between a location in the generated JavaScript and
a location in the original Elm source, optionally including the original name
before mangling.
aliases.type: { srcLine : Basics.Int, srcCol : Basics.Int, srcModule : System.TypeCheck.IO.Canonical, srcName : Maybe.Maybe Compiler.Generate.JavaScript.Name.Name, genLine : Basics.Int, genCol : Basics.Int }

values.name: addKernel
values.comment:
Register a kernel module dependency.

Tracks kernel modules that need to be included in the generated output.
Kernel dependencies are accumulated and can be retrieved later.

values.name: emptyBuilder
values.comment:
Create an empty builder starting at the specified line number.

Used to initialize a builder for generating JavaScript code, typically starting
at line 1 unless prepending to existing output.

values.name: exprToBuilder
values.comment:
Convert a JavaScript expression to builder output.

Takes an expression AST node and appends its formatted JavaScript representation
to the builder, handling operator precedence and parenthesization.

values.name: stmtToBuilder
values.comment:
Convert a JavaScript statement to builder output.

Takes a statement AST node and appends its formatted JavaScript representation
to the builder, handling indentation and line breaks.

name: Compiler.Generate.JavaScript.Expression
comment:
JavaScript expression generation from optimized Elm AST.

This module converts optimized Elm expressions into JavaScript code, handling
function calls, pattern matching, record operations, and various optimization
passes. It generates either pure expressions or statement blocks depending on
control flow requirements.


# Code Generation

@docs generate, Code


# Code Conversion

@docs codeToExpr, codeToStmtList


# Specialized Generators

@docs generateCtor, generateField, generateMain, generateTailDef

unions.name: Code
unions.comment: Generated JavaScript code, either a single expression or a block of statements.

values.name: codeToExpr
values.comment: Convert generated code to a JavaScript expression, wrapping statement blocks in IIFEs if necessary.

values.name: codeToStmtList
values.comment: Convert generated code to a list of JavaScript statements, unwrapping simple expressions into return statements.

values.name: generate
values.comment: Generate JavaScript code from an optimized Elm expression. Returns either a pure expression or statement block depending on control flow.

values.name: generateCtor
values.comment: Generate JavaScript code for a constructor function with the specified arity.

values.name: generateField
values.comment: Convert an Elm field name to a JavaScript property name, applying production-mode shortening if enabled.

values.name: generateMain
values.comment: Generate the main entry point for an Elm program, handling both static and dynamic initialization.

values.name: generateTailDef
values.comment: Generate a tail-recursive function definition wrapped in a while-true loop with labeled break.
values.type: Compiler.Generate.Mode.Mode -> System.TypeCheck.IO.Canonical -> Compiler.Data.Name.Name -> List.List (Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located Compiler.Data.Name.Name) -> Compiler.AST.Optimized.Expr -> Compiler.Generate.JavaScript.Expression.Code

name: Compiler.Generate.JavaScript.Functions
comment:
JavaScript runtime function wrappers for Elm functions.

This module provides the JavaScript code that implements Elm's curried function calling
convention. It includes wrapper functions (F2-F9) that create curried versions of
multi-argument JavaScript functions, and application functions (A2-A9) that efficiently
call both wrapped and unwrapped functions.

The F wrappers store the arity and original function, enabling efficient partial
application and full application at call sites. The A functions check the arity
at runtime to decide whether to call the optimized multi-argument form or the
curried form.


# Runtime Code

@docs functions

values.name: functions
values.comment: JavaScript runtime code for function wrapping and application. Provides F2-F9 wrappers for creating curried functions and A2-A9 helpers for efficient function calls.

name: Compiler.Generate.JavaScript.Name

aliases.name: Name

Convert a globally-qualified Elm name to a human-readable JavaScript name.

Convert an integer to a compact JavaScript name using ASCII encoding.
Avoids JavaScript and Elm reserved words through a renaming scheme.

values.name: fromKernel

Generate a name for a kernel function (built-in JavaScript implementation).
Kernel names use underscore prefix and separator to namespace them separately.

values.name: fromLocal

Convert a local Elm name to a JavaScript name, prefixing with underscore
if it conflicts with JavaScript or Elm reserved words.

values.name: fromLocalHumanReadable

Convert a local Elm name to a human-readable JavaScript name without mangling.

values.name: makeA

values.name: makeF

values.name: makeLabel

Generate a labeled name with an index suffix (e.g., loop$0, branch$1).
Used for loop labels and branching constructs in generated code.

values.name: makeTemp
values.comment:
Generate a temporary variable name with $temp$ prefix.
Used for intermediate values during code generation.

name: Compiler.Generate.JavaScript.SourceMap
comment:
Source map generation for JavaScript output.

This module generates source maps conforming to the Source Map Revision 3 specification,
enabling browser developer tools to map generated JavaScript back to original Elm source
code. The source maps are generated as base64-encoded data URLs for embedding directly
in the generated JavaScript.

The implementation uses Variable Length Quantity (VLQ) encoding to efficiently represent
the mappings between generated and source positions. It tracks position deltas to minimize
the encoded size.


# Source Map Generation

@docs generate

values.name: generate
values.comment: Generate a source map as a base64-encoded data URL that maps generated JavaScript positions back to original Elm source positions.
values.type: Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> Data.Map.Dict (List.List String.String) System.TypeCheck.IO.Canonical String.String -> List.List Compiler.Generate.JavaScript.Builder.Mapping -> String.String

name: Compiler.Generate.Mode
comment:
Code generation modes for the Elm compiler.

This module defines the different compilation modes (development, production, debug)
and handles production-mode optimizations like field name shortening. The mode affects
code generation strategies, including whether to include debug metadata, preserve
human-readable names, and apply space-saving transformations.


# Mode

@docs Mode, isDebug


# Field Name Optimization

@docs ShortFieldNames, shortenFieldNames

unions.name: Mode
unions.comment: Compilation mode determining code generation strategy - development mode with optional debug metadata or production mode with optimizations.

aliases.name: ShortFieldNames
aliases.comment: Map from original field names to shortened JavaScript property names for production mode size optimization.

values.name: isDebug
values.comment: Check if the compilation mode includes debug metadata for time-travel debugging.

values.name: shortenFieldNames
values.comment: Analyze record field usage frequencies and assign short JavaScript names to frequently-used fields for code size reduction.

name: Compiler.Generate.Monomorphize
comment:
This module transforms a TypedOptimized.GlobalGraph into a Monomorphized.MonoGraph
by specializing all polymorphic functions to their concrete type instantiations.

The monomorphization algorithm works as follows:

1.  Find the entry point (main function).
2.  Use a worklist to process each (Global, MonoType, Maybe LambdaId) specialization.
3.  For each work item, specialize the TOpt.Node into a MonoNode by:
    a. Unifying the polymorphic type with the concrete type to get a substitution.
    b. Applying the substitution to all types in the expression.
    c. Discovering new specializations needed and adding them to the worklist.
4.  Continue until the worklist is empty.


# Monomorphization

@docs monomorphize

values.name: monomorphize
values.comment:
Transform a typed optimized graph using a custom entry point name.

This is useful for testing when the entry point is not named "main".

name: Compiler.Json.String
comment:
String extraction utilities for JSON processing in the Elm compiler.

This module provides functions to extract strings from various compiler data structures,
including parser snippets and names. It handles proper escaping when extracting strings
from comments, ensuring newlines, quotes, and backslashes are properly escaped for JSON output.


# String Extraction

@docs fromSnippet, fromName, fromComment

values.name: fromComment
values.comment: Extract a string from a comment snippet, properly escaping newlines, quotes, and backslashes.

values.name: fromName
values.comment: Extract a string from a Name.

values.name: fromSnippet
values.comment: Extract a string from a parser snippet.

name: Compiler.Nitpick.Debug
comment:
Debug usage detection for optimized Elm code.

This module traverses the optimized AST to determine whether any Debug functions
(like Debug.log, Debug.todo) are referenced in the code. This is used to enforce
the prohibition of Debug calls in production builds and to guide code generation
optimizations.


# Detection

@docs hasDebugUses

values.name: hasDebugUses
values.comment:
Check whether the optimized code contains any references to Debug functions.
Returns true if any Debug.log, Debug.todo, or other Debug functions are found.

name: Compiler.Nitpick.PatternMatches
comment:
Pattern match exhaustiveness and redundancy checker for Elm.

This module implements Luc Maranget's algorithm for detecting incomplete and redundant
patterns in case expressions, function arguments, and let destructuring. It simplifies
canonical patterns into a normalized form, then analyzes them to find missing cases
(non-exhaustive matches) and unreachable branches (redundant patterns).

The algorithm comes from "Warnings for Pattern Matching" by Luc Maranget:
<http://moscova.inria.fr/~maranget/papers/warn/warn.pdf>


# Pattern Representation

@docs Pattern, Literal


# Error Reporting

@docs Error, Context


# Checking

@docs check


# Serialization

@docs errorEncoder, errorDecoder

unions.name: Context
unions.comment:
Context where a pattern matching error occurred.

  - `BadArg` means the error is in a function argument pattern
  - `BadDestruct` means the error is in a let-destructuring pattern
  - `BadCase` means the error is in a case expression

unions.name: Error
unions.comment:
Pattern matching error detected during exhaustiveness checking.

  - `Incomplete` indicates missing cases in pattern matching, with the region,
    context where the error occurred, and example patterns that are not covered
  - `Redundant` indicates an unreachable pattern, with the overall match region,
    the redundant pattern's region, and its 1-based index in the pattern list

unions.name: Literal
unions.comment:
Literal value that can appear in patterns.

Represents primitive values that can be matched exactly:

  - `Chr` for character literals
  - `Str` for string literals
  - `Int` for integer literals

unions.name: Pattern
unions.comment:
Simplified pattern representation used by the exhaustiveness checker.

Canonical patterns are normalized into this simpler form for analysis:

  - `Anything` matches any value (wildcards, variables, records)
  - `Literal` matches exact primitive values (Int, Char, String)
  - `Ctor` matches custom type constructors with their arguments

values.name: check
values.comment:
Check a canonical module for pattern matching errors.

Traverses all declarations, expressions, and patterns in the module to find:

  - Non-exhaustive pattern matches (missing cases)
  - Redundant patterns (unreachable branches)

Returns `Ok ()` if all patterns are valid, or `Err` with a list of errors found.

values.name: errorDecoder
values.comment: Decode a pattern matching error from bytes after deserialization.

values.name: errorEncoder
values.comment: Encode a pattern matching error to bytes for caching or serialization.

name: Compiler.Optimize.Erased.Case
comment:
Optimizes case expressions using decision trees.

This module bridges the gap between decision tree compilation and the optimized
AST. It takes pattern-matched branches and converts them into an efficient
case expression with:

  - A decision tree that determines which branch to execute
  - Inline choices for branches that are only reached from one path
  - Jump labels for branches that can be reached from multiple paths

The optimization reduces code duplication by sharing branch implementations
when the same code would be reached through different pattern match paths.


# Optimization

@docs optimize

values.name: optimize
values.comment:
Optimize a case expression into a decision tree.
Takes a temporary variable name, the root variable being matched, and the pattern-matched branches.
Returns an optimized Case expression with decision tree and inline/jump choices.
values.type: Compiler.Data.Name.Name -> Compiler.Data.Name.Name -> List.List ( Compiler.AST.Canonical.Pattern, Compiler.AST.Optimized.Expr ) -> Compiler.AST.Optimized.Expr

name: Compiler.Optimize.Erased.DecisionTree
comment:
Compiles pattern matching into efficient decision trees.

This module implements the algorithm described in "When Do Match-Compilation
Heuristics Matter?" by Kevin Scott and Norman Ramsey. It converts a simple list
of patterns into an optimized decision tree that minimizes the number of runtime
tests needed to determine which branch to execute.

The algorithm uses heuristics similar to SML/NJ:

  - Minimize the number of branches with default behavior
  - Minimize the branching factor at each decision point


# Core Types

@docs DecisionTree, Test, Path


# Compilation

@docs compile


# Binary Encoding

@docs pathEncoder, pathDecoder, testEncoder, testDecoder

unions.name: DecisionTree
unions.comment:
A decision tree representation for efficient pattern matching.

  - `Match Int`: A leaf node indicating successful match with the branch index
  - `Decision Path (List (Test, DecisionTree)) (Maybe DecisionTree)`: A decision node that tests a value at the given path, with edges for each test outcome and an optional fallback for unmatched cases

unions.name: Path
unions.comment:
A path describing how to access a value within a matched pattern.

  - `Index`: Access the nth field of a tuple or constructor arguments
  - `Unbox`: Unwrap a single-constructor custom type to access its contents
  - `Empty`: The root path (the matched value itself)

unions.name: Test
unions.comment:
A runtime test to determine which branch to take in a decision tree.

  - `IsCtor`: Tests if a value is a specific custom type constructor
  - `IsCons`: Tests if a list is non-empty (has cons cell)
  - `IsNil`: Tests if a list is empty
  - `IsTuple`: Tests if a value is a tuple
  - `IsInt`: Tests if a value equals a specific integer
  - `IsChr`: Tests if a value equals a specific character
  - `IsStr`: Tests if a value equals a specific string
  - `IsBool`: Tests if a value equals a specific boolean

values.name: compile
values.comment:
Users of this module will mainly interact with this function. It takes
some normal branches and gives out a decision tree that has "labels" at all
the leafs and a dictionary that maps these "labels" to the code that should
run.

If 2 or more leaves point to the same label, we need to do some tricks in JS to
make that work nicely. When is JS getting goto?! ;) That is outside the scope
of this module though.

values.name: pathDecoder
values.comment: Decode a Path from bytes.

values.name: pathEncoder
values.comment: Encode a Path to bytes for serialization.

values.name: testDecoder
values.comment: Decode a Test from bytes.

values.name: testEncoder
values.comment: Encode a Test to bytes for serialization.

name: Compiler.Optimize.Erased.Expression
comment:
Optimizes canonical expressions into optimized expressions.

This module transforms the canonical AST produced by type checking into an
optimized representation suitable for code generation. Key optimizations include:

  - Inlining constructors and simple operations
  - Tracking global dependencies and field usage
  - Pattern destructuring for efficient field access
  - Tail call detection and optimization for recursive functions


# Core Types

@docs Cycle


# Optimization

@docs optimize, optimizePotentialTailCall


# Helpers

@docs destructArgs

aliases.name: Cycle
aliases.comment:
Set of names that participate in a recursive definition cycle.
Used to identify which variables need special cycle-breaking handling during optimization.

values.name: destructArgs
values.comment:
Converts a list of function argument patterns into argument names and destructuring operations.

For each pattern in the argument list, this generates a simple variable name and creates
Destructor operations to extract any nested values. This allows function arguments to use
complex patterns (like tuples or records) while maintaining efficient compiled code.

Returns a tuple of:

  - List of argument names (one per pattern)
  - List of destructors to extract nested values from those arguments
values.type: List.List Compiler.AST.Canonical.Pattern -> Compiler.Optimize.Erased.Names.Tracker ( List.List (Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located Compiler.Data.Name.Name), List.List Compiler.AST.Optimized.Destructor )

values.name: optimize
values.comment:
Transforms a canonical expression into an optimized expression.

This is the main entry point for expression optimization. It recursively processes
the canonical AST, performing optimizations such as:

  - Tracking global variable and constructor usage
  - Registering field accesses for record optimization
  - Converting pattern matches to efficient destructuring operations
  - Detecting and preserving tail call opportunities

The function works within the Names.Tracker monad to collect dependency information
while transforming the expression tree.

values.name: optimizePotentialTailCall
values.comment:
Analyzes a function definition for tail call optimization opportunities.

Given a function's region, name, arguments, and body expression, this function:

1.  Destructures the argument patterns into simple names
2.  Analyzes the body for tail-recursive calls to the same function
3.  Converts tail calls into TailCall nodes for efficient compilation
4.  Returns either a TailDef (if tail calls found) or regular Def

This enables recursive functions to be compiled as loops when they are tail-recursive,
avoiding stack overflow and improving performance.
values.type: Compiler.Optimize.Erased.Expression.Cycle -> Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Region -> Compiler.Data.Name.Name -> List.List Compiler.AST.Canonical.Pattern -> Compiler.AST.Canonical.Expr -> Compiler.Optimize.Erased.Names.Tracker Compiler.AST.Optimized.Def

name: Compiler.Optimize.Erased.Module
comment:
Optimization phase entry point that transforms canonical AST into optimized representation.

This module serves as the main interface for the optimization phase of the Elm compiler.
It takes a canonicalized module (after type checking) and produces an optimized LocalGraph
suitable for code generation. The optimization process:

1.  Converts type aliases into constructor functions
2.  Registers union type constructors
3.  Handles effect managers (Cmd/Sub/Fx)
4.  Processes port declarations with encoder/decoder generation
5.  Optimizes value declarations and recursive definition groups

The optimization tracks dependencies between globals and field access patterns to enable
dead code elimination and efficient code generation.


# Optimization

@docs optimize


# Types

@docs Annotations, MResult

aliases.name: Annotations
aliases.comment: Maps definition names to their type annotations from the canonical phase.

aliases.name: MResult
aliases.comment: Result type for module optimization, carrying errors and warnings.

values.name: optimize
values.comment:
Optimize a canonical module to produce an optimized local graph.

Processes all declarations, union types, type aliases, and effects in the module.
Returns a LocalGraph containing optimized nodes and field access counts.

name: Compiler.Optimize.Erased.Names

unions.name: Tracker
unions.comment:
State monad that tracks name generation and dependencies during optimization.

Threads through three pieces of state:

  - A unique ID counter for generating fresh variable names
  - A set of global dependencies (functions, constructors, kernels)
  - A dictionary tracking field name usage counts

values.name: andThen
values.comment:
Chain two Tracker computations together, threading state from first to second.

Equivalent to `>>=` (bind) in Haskell. The callback receives the result of the first
computation and can use it to produce a second computation. Dependencies and state
accumulate across both computations.
values.type: (a -> Compiler.Optimize.Erased.Names.Tracker b) -> Compiler.Optimize.Erased.Names.Tracker a -> Compiler.Optimize.Erased.Names.Tracker b

values.name: generate
values.comment:
Generate a fresh, unique variable name.

The generated name is guaranteed to not conflict with any other names in the module.
Names are generated sequentially as _v0, _v1, \_v2, etc.

values.name: map
values.comment:
Transform the result value of a Tracker computation using the given function.

Preserves all tracked dependencies and state while applying the function to the result.

values.name: mapTraverse
values.comment:
Apply a Tracker-producing function to each value in a dictionary, accumulating results.

Like traverse but for dictionaries. Sequences computations across all dictionary entries,
threading state through each step. Returns a Tracker containing a dictionary with
transformed values and all accumulated dependencies.
values.type: (k -> comparable) -> (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> (a -> Compiler.Optimize.Erased.Names.Tracker b) -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k a -> Compiler.Optimize.Erased.Names.Tracker (Data.Map.Dict comparable k b)

values.name: pure
values.comment:
Lift a pure value into a Tracker computation without tracking any dependencies.

Equivalent to `return` in Haskell's monad notation. The value is wrapped in a Tracker
that doesn't modify the state or add any dependencies.

values.name: registerCtor
values.comment:
Register a dependency on a type constructor and return an optimized expression.

Handles three cases based on constructor options:

  - Normal: Regular constructor, returns VarGlobal
  - Enum: Zero-argument constructor, returns VarEnum (or Bool for True/False in Basics)
  - Unbox: Single-argument constructor, returns VarBox and registers identity dependency
values.type: Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Region -> System.TypeCheck.IO.Canonical -> Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located Compiler.Data.Name.Name -> Compiler.Data.Index.ZeroBased -> Compiler.AST.Canonical.CtorOpts -> Compiler.Optimize.Erased.Names.Tracker Compiler.AST.Optimized.Expr

values.name: registerDebug
values.comment:
Register a dependency on a Debug module function and return a debug variable reference.

Debug functions are special-cased to support conditional compilation and removal
in production builds. The home module is tracked for context.

values.name: registerField
values.comment:
Register usage of a record field name and return the given value.

Increments the usage count for the field name, which is used by the code generator
to determine optimal field name mangling strategies.

values.name: registerFieldDict
values.comment:
Register usage of multiple record fields from a dictionary and return the given value.

Takes a dictionary where keys are field names and increments the usage count for each.
The dictionary values are ignored - only the keys (field names) matter.
values.type: Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name v -> a -> Compiler.Optimize.Erased.Names.Tracker a

values.name: registerFieldList
values.comment:
Register usage of multiple record fields from a list and return the given value.

Increments the usage count for each field name in the list. Useful when processing
field accesses or record patterns.

values.name: registerGlobal
values.comment:
Register a dependency on a global function or value and return a reference to it.

Creates a VarGlobal expression referencing the function/value and adds it to the
dependency set so the code generator knows to import it.

values.name: registerKernel
values.comment:
Register a dependency on a kernel function and return the given value.

Kernel functions are JavaScript primitives exposed to Elm code.
Registering them ensures they are included in the final output.

values.name: run
values.comment:
Execute a Tracker computation, returning collected dependencies, fields, and the result value.

Returns a tuple of:

  - Global dependencies (functions, constructors, kernels used)
  - Field names and their usage counts
  - The computed result value
values.type: Compiler.Optimize.Erased.Names.Tracker a -> ( Data.Set.EverySet (List.List String.String) Compiler.AST.Optimized.Global, Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Basics.Int, a )

values.name: traverse
values.comment:
Apply a Tracker-producing function to each element of a list, accumulating results.

Sequences the Tracker computations from left to right, threading state through each step.
Returns a Tracker containing the list of all results with all dependencies accumulated.
values.type: (a -> Compiler.Optimize.Erased.Names.Tracker b) -> List.List a -> Compiler.Optimize.Erased.Names.Tracker (List.List b)

name: Compiler.Optimize.Erased.Port
comment:
Generates JSON encoders and decoders for port types.

This module analyzes Elm types used in port definitions and automatically
generates the necessary encoder and decoder expressions for converting between
Elm values and JavaScript JSON values. It handles:

  - Primitives (Int, Float, Bool, String)
  - Collections (List, Array, Maybe)
  - Tuples
  - Records
  - Special types (Bytes, Json.Encode.Value)

The generated code ensures type-safe communication across the Elm/JavaScript
boundary without requiring manual encoder/decoder implementations.


# Encoders

@docs toEncoder


# Decoders

@docs toDecoder, toFlagsDecoder

values.name: toDecoder
values.comment:
Generate a JSON decoder for the given Elm type.
Produces an optimized expression that converts JSON to Elm values for incoming ports.

values.name: toEncoder
values.comment:
Generate a JSON encoder function for the given Elm type.
Produces an optimized expression that converts Elm values to JSON for outgoing ports.

values.name: toFlagsDecoder
values.comment:
Generate a JSON decoder for program flags.
Handles the special case where Unit flags decode to a successful Unit value.

name: Compiler.Optimize.Typed.Case
comment:
Optimizes typed case expressions using decision trees.

This module bridges the gap between decision tree compilation and the typed optimized
AST. It takes pattern-matched branches and converts them into an efficient
case expression with:

  - A decision tree that determines which branch to execute
  - Inline choices for branches that are only reached from one path
  - Jump labels for branches that can be reached from multiple paths

The optimization reduces code duplication by sharing branch implementations
when the same code would be reached through different pattern match paths.


# Optimization

@docs optimize

values.name: optimize
values.comment:
Optimize a typed case expression into a decision tree.
Takes a temporary variable name, the root variable being matched, the pattern-matched branches,
and the result type. Returns an optimized Case expression with decision tree and inline/jump choices.
values.type: Compiler.Data.Name.Name -> Compiler.Data.Name.Name -> List.List ( Compiler.AST.Canonical.Pattern, Compiler.AST.TypedOptimized.Expr ) -> Compiler.AST.Canonical.Type -> Compiler.AST.TypedOptimized.Expr

name: Compiler.Optimize.Typed.Expression

aliases.name: Annotations
aliases.comment:
Dictionary mapping variable names to their type annotations.

Used to look up types during optimization for kernel function typing
and other type-directed optimizations.

aliases.name: Cycle
aliases.comment: Set of names that participate in a recursive definition cycle.

values.name: destructArgs
values.comment: Converts a list of function argument patterns into argument names with types and destructuring operations.
values.type: Compiler.Optimize.Typed.Expression.Annotations -> List.List Compiler.AST.Canonical.Pattern -> Compiler.Optimize.Typed.Names.Tracker ( List.List ( Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located Compiler.Data.Name.Name, Compiler.AST.Canonical.Type ), List.List Compiler.AST.TypedOptimized.Destructor )

values.name: optimize
values.comment:
Transforms a TypedCanonical expression into a TypedOptimized expression.

Takes the kernel type environment, annotations, expression types map, cycle names,
and a typed expression. The type of the expression is taken from the TypedCanonical node.

The `exprTypes` parameter is needed to convert subexpressions (which are still
`Can.Expr`) back to `TCan.Expr` when recursing.

values.name: optimizePotentialTailCall
values.comment: Optimize a potentially tail-recursive definition.
values.type: Compiler.Optimize.Typed.KernelTypes.KernelTypeEnv -> Compiler.Optimize.Typed.Expression.Annotations -> Compiler.AST.TypedCanonical.ExprTypes -> Compiler.Optimize.Typed.Expression.Cycle -> Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Region -> Compiler.Data.Name.Name -> List.List Compiler.AST.Canonical.Pattern -> Compiler.AST.TypedCanonical.Expr -> Compiler.AST.Canonical.Type -> Compiler.Optimize.Typed.Names.Tracker Compiler.AST.TypedOptimized.Def

name: Compiler.Optimize.Typed.KernelTypes
comment:
Kernel function type environment for typed optimization.

This module provides the kernel type environment and helper utilities.
The environment maps kernel function (home, name) pairs to their canonical types.

Construction of the kernel environment is handled by the PostSolve phase,
which uses alias seeding and usage-based inference over the canonical AST.


# Type

@docs KernelTypeEnv


# Lookup

@docs lookup, hasEntry


# Helpers for PostSolve

@docs insertFirstUsage, buildFunctionType

aliases.name: KernelTypeEnv
aliases.comment: Environment mapping kernel function (home, name) pairs to their types.
aliases.type: Data.Map.Dict ( String.String, String.String ) ( Compiler.Data.Name.Name, Compiler.Data.Name.Name ) Compiler.AST.Canonical.Type

values.name: buildFunctionType
values.comment: Build a function type from argument types and result type.

values.name: hasEntry
values.comment: Check if an entry exists for a kernel.

values.name: insertFirstUsage
values.comment: Insert a kernel type only if no entry exists (first-usage-wins).

values.name: lookup
values.comment: Look up a kernel type by (home, name).

name: Compiler.Optimize.Typed.Module
comment:
Typed module optimization.

Converts a canonical module to a TypedOptimized.LocalGraph, preserving full
type information on every expression.


# Types

@docs Annotations, MResult


# Optimization

@docs optimizeTyped

aliases.name: Annotations
aliases.comment: Type annotations for top-level definitions, mapping names to their canonical type annotations.

aliases.name: MResult
aliases.comment: Result type for module optimization operations that can produce errors or warnings.

values.name: optimizeTyped
values.comment:
Optimize a TypedCanonical module to a typed optimized local graph.

This is the main entry point for typed optimization. It takes a TypedCanonical
module (where every expression already has its type), the expression type map
for converting subexpressions, and produces a TypedOptimized.LocalGraph.

The kernelEnv is computed by the PostSolve phase and passed in from the caller.
values.type: Compiler.Optimize.Typed.Module.Annotations -> Compiler.AST.TypedCanonical.ExprTypes -> Compiler.Optimize.Typed.KernelTypes.KernelTypeEnv -> Compiler.AST.TypedCanonical.Module -> Compiler.Optimize.Typed.Module.MResult i (List.List Compiler.Reporting.Warning.Warning) Compiler.AST.TypedOptimized.LocalGraph

name: Compiler.Optimize.Typed.Names

unions.name: Tracker
unions.comment:
State monad that tracks name generation, dependencies, and local types during optimization.

Threads through four pieces of state:

  - A unique ID counter for generating fresh variable names
  - A set of global dependencies (functions, constructors, kernels)
  - A dictionary tracking field name usage counts
  - A dictionary of local variable types (Name -> Can.Type)

values.name: andThen
values.comment:
Chain two Tracker computations together, threading state from first to second.

Equivalent to `>>=` (bind) in Haskell. The callback receives the result of the first
computation and can use it to produce a second computation. Dependencies and state
accumulate across both computations.
values.type: (a -> Compiler.Optimize.Typed.Names.Tracker b) -> Compiler.Optimize.Typed.Names.Tracker a -> Compiler.Optimize.Typed.Names.Tracker b

values.name: generate
values.comment:
Generate a fresh, unique variable name.

The generated name is guaranteed to not conflict with any other names in the module.
Names are generated sequentially as _v0, _v1, \_v2, etc.

values.name: lookupLocalType
values.comment:
Look up the type of a local variable.

Returns the type if the variable is in scope, or crashes with an error message
if the variable is not found. This should not happen in well-typed code.

values.name: map
values.comment:
Transform the result value of a Tracker computation using the given function.

Preserves all tracked dependencies and state while applying the function to the result.

values.name: pure
values.comment:
Lift a pure value into a Tracker computation without tracking any dependencies.

Equivalent to `return` in Haskell's monad notation. The value is wrapped in a Tracker
that doesn't modify the state or add any dependencies.

values.name: registerCtor
values.comment:
Register a dependency on a type constructor and return an optimized expression.

Handles three cases based on constructor options:

  - Normal: Regular constructor, returns VarGlobal
  - Enum: Zero-argument constructor, returns VarEnum (or Bool for True/False in Basics)
  - Unbox: Single-argument constructor, returns VarBox and registers identity dependency
values.type: Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Region -> System.TypeCheck.IO.Canonical -> Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located Compiler.Data.Name.Name -> Compiler.Data.Index.ZeroBased -> Compiler.AST.Canonical.CtorOpts -> Compiler.AST.Canonical.Type -> Compiler.Optimize.Typed.Names.Tracker Compiler.AST.TypedOptimized.Expr

values.name: registerDebug
values.comment:
Register a dependency on a Debug module function and return a debug variable reference.

Debug functions are special-cased to support conditional compilation and removal
in production builds. The home module is tracked for context.
values.type: Compiler.Data.Name.Name -> System.TypeCheck.IO.Canonical -> Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Region -> Compiler.AST.Canonical.Type -> Compiler.Optimize.Typed.Names.Tracker Compiler.AST.TypedOptimized.Expr

values.name: registerField
values.comment:
Register usage of a record field name and return the given value.

Increments the usage count for the field name, which is used by the code generator
to determine optimal field name mangling strategies.

values.name: registerFieldDict
values.comment:
Register usage of multiple record fields from a dictionary and return the given value.

Takes a dictionary where keys are field names and increments the usage count for each.
The dictionary values are ignored - only the keys (field names) matter.
values.type: Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name v -> a -> Compiler.Optimize.Typed.Names.Tracker a

values.name: registerFieldList
values.comment:
Register usage of multiple record fields from a list and return the given value.

Increments the usage count for each field name in the list. Useful when processing
field accesses or record patterns.

values.name: registerGlobal
values.comment:
Register a dependency on a global function or value and return a reference to it.

Creates a VarGlobal expression referencing the function/value and adds it to the
dependency set so the code generator knows to import it.
values.type: Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Region -> System.TypeCheck.IO.Canonical -> Compiler.Data.Name.Name -> Compiler.AST.Canonical.Type -> Compiler.Optimize.Typed.Names.Tracker Compiler.AST.TypedOptimized.Expr

values.name: registerKernel
values.comment:
Register a dependency on a kernel function and return the given value.

Kernel functions are JavaScript primitives exposed to Elm code.
Registering them ensures they are included in the final output.

values.name: run
values.comment:
Execute a Tracker computation, returning collected dependencies, fields, and the result value.

Returns a tuple of:

  - Global dependencies (functions, constructors, kernels used)
  - Field names and their usage counts
  - The computed result value
values.type: Compiler.Optimize.Typed.Names.Tracker a -> ( Data.Set.EverySet (List.List String.String) Compiler.AST.TypedOptimized.Global, Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Basics.Int, a )

values.name: traverse
values.comment:
Apply a Tracker-producing function to each element of a list, accumulating results.

Sequences the Tracker computations from left to right, threading state through each step.
Returns a Tracker containing the list of all results with all dependencies accumulated.
values.type: (a -> Compiler.Optimize.Typed.Names.Tracker b) -> List.List a -> Compiler.Optimize.Typed.Names.Tracker (List.List b)

values.name: withVarTypes
values.comment:
Extend the local type environment with multiple bindings for the duration of a computation.

All bindings are visible within the provided Tracker computation and are automatically
removed when that computation completes.
values.type: List.List ( Compiler.Data.Name.Name, Compiler.AST.Canonical.Type ) -> Compiler.Optimize.Typed.Names.Tracker a -> Compiler.Optimize.Typed.Names.Tracker a

name: Compiler.Optimize.Typed.Port

values.name: toDecoder
values.comment: Generate a typed JSON decoder for the given Elm type.

values.name: toEncoder
values.comment: Generate a typed JSON encoder function for the given Elm type.

values.name: toFlagsDecoder
values.comment: Generate a typed JSON decoder for program flags.

name: Compiler.Parse.Declaration
comment:
Parser for top-level declarations in Elm modules.

This module handles parsing of value definitions, type aliases, custom type
declarations, and port declarations. It supports doc comments, type annotations,
and function definitions with pattern arguments.


# Declaration Types

@docs Decl


# Parsing Declarations

@docs declaration, infix_

unions.name: Decl
unions.comment: Represents a top-level declaration in an Elm module.

values.name: declaration
values.comment:
Parses a top-level declaration including doc comments, type annotations, and definitions.
Handles value declarations, type aliases, custom types, and port declarations.

values.name: infix_
values.comment:
Parses an infix operator declaration with associativity, precedence, and function binding.
Always consumes input up to a fresh line.

name: Compiler.Parse.Expression
comment:
Expression parser for Elm source code.

This module parses Elm expressions into the Source AST, handling:

  - Literals (strings, numbers, characters)
  - Variables and qualified names
  - Operators and operator sections
  - Function application and lambdas
  - Control flow (if/then/else, case/of, let/in)
  - Data structures (lists, tuples, records)
  - Record access and update syntax


# Parsing

@docs expression, record

values.name: expression
values.comment:
Parse a complete Elm expression including operators, function application,
and special forms like if/case/let/lambda. Returns the parsed expression with
associated comments and position information.

values.name: record
values.comment:
Parse record expressions including record literals and record update syntax.
Handles both empty records and records with fields.

name: Compiler.Parse.Keyword

values.name: alias_
values.comment: Parses the 'alias' keyword for type alias declarations.

values.name: as_
values.comment: Parses the 'as' keyword for module aliasing in import declarations.

values.name: case_
values.comment: Parses the 'case' keyword for pattern matching expressions.

values.name: command_
values.comment: Parses the 'command' keyword for effect module declarations.

values.name: effect_
values.comment: Parses the 'effect' keyword for effect module declarations.

values.name: else_
values.comment: Parses the 'else' keyword for conditional expressions.

values.name: exposing_
values.comment: Parses the 'exposing' keyword for exposing lists in module and import declarations.

values.name: if_
values.comment: Parses the 'if' keyword for conditional expressions.

values.name: import_
values.comment: Parses the 'import' keyword for import declarations.

values.name: in_
values.comment: Parses the 'in' keyword for let expressions.

values.name: infix_
values.comment: Parses the 'infix' keyword for infix operator declarations.

values.name: k4
values.comment: Helper for parsing a 4-character keyword. Ensures the keyword is not followed by identifier characters.
values.type: Char.Char -> Char.Char -> Char.Char -> Char.Char -> (Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Row -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col -> x) -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x ()

values.name: k5
values.comment: Helper for parsing a 5-character keyword. Ensures the keyword is not followed by identifier characters.
values.type: Char.Char -> Char.Char -> Char.Char -> Char.Char -> Char.Char -> (Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Row -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col -> x) -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x ()

values.name: left_
values.comment: Parses the 'left' keyword for left-associative operators.

values.name: let_
values.comment: Parses the 'let' keyword for let expressions.

values.name: module_
values.comment: Parses the 'module' keyword for module declarations.

values.name: non_
values.comment: Parses the 'non' keyword for non-associative operators.

values.name: of_
values.comment: Parses the 'of' keyword for pattern matching expressions.

values.name: port_
values.comment: Parses the 'port' keyword for port declarations.

values.name: right_
values.comment: Parses the 'right' keyword for right-associative operators.

values.name: subscription_
values.comment: Parses the 'subscription' keyword for effect module declarations.

values.name: then_
values.comment: Parses the 'then' keyword for conditional expressions.

values.name: type_
values.comment: Parses the 'type' keyword for type declarations.

values.name: where_
values.comment: Parses the 'where' keyword for effect module declarations.

name: Compiler.Parse.Module
comment:
Parse Elm module declarations including headers, imports, and declarations.

This module provides the entry point for parsing entire Elm modules. It handles:

  - Module headers (normal, port, and effect modules)
  - Import declarations
  - Module documentation comments
  - Top-level declarations


# Parsing Entry Point

@docs fromByteString


# Module Structure

@docs Module, Header, ProjectType, Effects


# Parsers

@docs chompModule, chompImports, chompImport


# Utilities

@docs isKernel

unions.name: Effects
unions.comment:
The kind of effects a module can have.

  - `NoEffects` - A normal module with no special effects
  - `Ports` - A port module that can define ports for JavaScript interop
  - `Manager` - An effect manager (kernel code only) that defines commands/subscriptions

unions.name: ProjectType
unions.comment:
Whether we're compiling a package or application.

This affects whether ports and effect modules are allowed, and which
default imports are included.

aliases.name: Header
aliases.comment:
Module header information including name, exports, and effect type.

Contains all the information from the module declaration line,
including surrounding comments and the module documentation comment.
aliases.type: { name : Compiler.AST.Source.C2 (Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located Compiler.Data.Name.Name), effects : Compiler.Parse.Module.Effects, exports : Compiler.AST.Source.C2 (Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located Compiler.AST.Source.Exposing), docs : Result.Result Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Region Compiler.AST.Source.Comment }

aliases.name: Module
aliases.comment:
Intermediate representation of a parsed module before validation.

This structure preserves all comments and formatting information during
parsing. It's later converted to `Src.Module` after validation in `checkModule`.
aliases.type: { initialComments : Compiler.AST.Source.FComments, header : Maybe.Maybe Compiler.Parse.Module.Header, imports : Compiler.AST.Source.C1 (List.List (Compiler.AST.Source.C1 Compiler.AST.Source.Import)), infixes : List.List (Compiler.AST.Source.C1 (Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located Compiler.AST.Source.Infix)), decls : List.List (Compiler.AST.Source.C2 Compiler.Parse.Declaration.Decl) }

values.name: chompImport
values.comment:
Parse a single import declaration.

Parses the import keyword, module name, optional alias, and optional
exposing list. Handles all comment preservation around each component.

values.name: chompImports
values.comment:
Parse a sequence of import declarations.

Repeatedly parses import statements until none remain. Takes a list of
default imports to prepend (empty for core, standard defaults otherwise).

values.name: chompModule
values.comment:
Parse the structure of an Elm module.

Parses header (if present), imports, infixes (for kernel code), and
declarations. Handles different project types by conditionally including
default imports and allowing/disallowing certain features.

values.name: fromByteString
values.comment:
Parse a complete Elm module from source text.

Takes a syntax version, project type, and source string. Returns either
a parsed module or a syntax error. This is the main entry point for module parsing.

values.name: isKernel
values.comment:
Check if the project type represents a kernel package.

Only kernel packages (like elm/core internals) are allowed to define
effect modules and use kernel JavaScript code.

name: Compiler.Parse.Number

unions.name: Number
unions.comment: Represents a parsed numeric literal with its value and source text.

unions.name: Outcome
unions.comment: Represents the result of parsing a number: error, integer, or float.

values.name: chompHex
values.comment:
Parses hexadecimal digits and returns the position and parsed integer value.
Used for parsing hex literals (0x...) and Unicode escape sequences.

values.name: number
values.comment:
Parses a numeric literal (integer, float, hexadecimal, or binary).
Supports underscores for readability in Guida syntax mode.
values.type: Compiler.Parse.SyntaxVersion.SyntaxVersion -> (Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Row -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col -> x) -> (Compiler.Reporting.Error.Syntax.Number -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Row -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col -> x) -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x Compiler.Parse.Number.Number

values.name: precedence
values.comment: Parses a single-digit precedence value (0-9) for infix operator declarations.

name: Compiler.Parse.Pattern
comment:
Parser for pattern matching constructs in Elm.

This module handles parsing of patterns used in case expressions, function
arguments, and let bindings. It supports wildcards, variables, literals,
constructors with arguments, tuples, lists, records, and cons patterns.


# Parsing Patterns

@docs term, expression

values.name: expression
values.comment:
Parse a complete pattern expression including cons (::) operators and aliases.

This handles complex patterns like:

  - Cons patterns: x :: xs
  - Chained cons: x :: y :: rest
  - Aliased patterns: (x :: xs) as list
  - Constructor applications: Just x, Nothing
  - Nested patterns with all operators

Returns a pattern with surrounding comments and the ending position.
This is the main entry point for parsing patterns in case expressions and function arguments.

values.name: term
values.comment:
Parse a single pattern term without considering operators like cons (::) or aliases.

This parses atomic patterns such as:

  - Records: { x, y }
  - Tuples: (a, b) or parenthesized patterns (x)
  - Lists: [a, b, c]
  - Wildcards: \_
  - Variables: x
  - Constructors: Nothing, Just x, List.map
  - Literals: 42, "hello", 'a'

This is the entry point for parsing simple patterns that don't involve infix operators.

name: Compiler.Parse.Primitives

unions.name: PStep
unions.comment:
Parser step result type with four variants for different parse outcomes.

  - `Cok`: Consumed input, parse OK (success after consuming input)
  - `Eok`: Empty OK (success without consuming input)
  - `Cerr`: Consumed input, error (failure after consuming input)
  - `Eerr`: Empty error (failure without consuming input)

The distinction between consumed/empty is crucial for backtracking behavior.

unions.name: Parser
unions.comment:
The core parser type that transforms a State into a parse result.

Parsers consume input and produce either success (with a value) or failure
(with an error). The `x` type parameter represents the error type, and `a`
represents the success value type.

unions.name: Snippet
unions.comment:
A snippet represents a slice of a source file with position information.

This allows parsing a substring of a file while maintaining accurate row/column
positions relative to the original file. Useful for incremental parsing or
parsing embedded code fragments.

  - `fptr`: The source string (file pointer/content)
  - `offset`: Starting byte position in the source
  - `length`: Number of bytes in the snippet
  - `offRow`: Starting row number in the original file
  - `offCol`: Starting column number in the original file
cases: { fptr : String.String, offset : Basics.Int, length : Basics.Int, offRow : Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Row, offCol : Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col }

unions.name: State
unions.comment: Parser state wrapper type.

unions.name: Step
unions.comment:
Control flow type for the `loop` combinator.

  - `Loop state`: Continue looping with updated state
  - `Done a`: Exit loop with final result

aliases.name: Col
aliases.comment: Column number type alias for position tracking (1-indexed).

aliases.name: Row
aliases.comment: Row (line) number type alias for position tracking (1-indexed).

aliases.name: StateData
aliases.comment:
The internal parser state data including source, position, and location.

  - `src`: The source string being parsed
  - `pos`: Current byte position in the source
  - `end`: End position (length of source or snippet)
  - `indent`: Current indentation level for layout-sensitive parsing
  - `row`: Current row (line) number (1-indexed)
  - `col`: Current column number (1-indexed)
aliases.type: { src : String.String, pos : Basics.Int, end : Basics.Int, indent : Basics.Int, row : Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Row, col : Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col }

values.name: addEnd
values.comment:
Create a located value from a start position, a value, and the current position.

Uses the provided start position and the current parser position as the end,
wrapping the value in a `Located` annotation. Does not consume any input.

values.name: addLocation
values.comment:
Annotate a parser's result with its source location.

Captures the start position before parsing and the end position after parsing,
wrapping the result in a `Located` value with the complete region.
values.type: Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x a -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x (Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located a)

values.name: andThen
values.comment:
Sequence two parsers, using the result of the first to determine the second.

This is the monadic bind operation. The callback function receives the result
of the first parser and returns the second parser to run. Properly handles
consumption tracking: if the first parser consumed input (Cok), the second
parser's Eok is promoted to Cok.
values.type: (a -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x b) -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x a -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x b

values.name: fromByteString
values.comment:
Run a parser on a complete source string.

Returns `Ok` with the parsed value if successful and the entire input is consumed.
Returns `Err` if parsing fails or if there is unconsumed input remaining (using
the provided error constructor for the latter case).
values.type: Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x a -> (Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Row -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col -> x) -> String.String -> Result.Result x a

values.name: fromSnippet
values.comment:
Run a parser on a snippet of source code with position tracking.
