Similar to `fromByteString`, but parses only a portion of a source string
(defined by offset and length) while maintaining correct position information
relative to the original source file.
values.type: Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x a -> (Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Row -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col -> x) -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Snippet -> Result.Result x a

values.name: getCharWidth
values.comment:
Get the width of a character in UTF-16 code units.

Returns 2 for characters outside the Basic Multilingual Plane (code point > 0xFFFF),
which require surrogate pairs in UTF-16, and 1 for all other characters.

values.name: getPosition
values.comment:
Get the current parser position without consuming any input.

Returns a `Position` containing the current row and column numbers.

values.name: inContext
values.comment:
Parse with contextual error information added to failures.

Runs a start parser to establish context, then runs the main parser. If the
main parser fails, the error is wrapped with context information from the start
position. This helps provide better error messages by showing where a syntactic
construct began.
values.type: (x -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Row -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col -> y) -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser y start -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x a -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser y a

values.name: isWord
values.comment:
Check if the character at a given position matches an expected character.

Returns `True` if position is within bounds and the character matches,
`False` otherwise. This is a safe alternative to direct character comparison.

values.name: loop
values.comment:
Repeatedly apply a parser-producing function until it returns `Done`.

A general-purpose looping combinator for parsers. The callback receives the
current state and returns a parser that produces either `Loop` (to continue
with new state) or `Done` (to finish with a result). Properly tracks input
consumption across iterations.
values.type: (state -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x (Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Step state a)) -> state -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x a

values.name: map
values.comment:
Transform the result of a parser by applying a function to the success value.

This is the functor map operation for parsers. It does not affect error values
or consume any additional input.
values.type: (a -> b) -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x a -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x b

values.name: oneOf
values.comment:
Try a list of parsers in order, succeeding with the first one that succeeds.

If all parsers fail without consuming input (Eerr), returns an error created by
the provided error constructor at the current position. If any parser consumes
input, that result is returned immediately (no backtracking after consumption).
values.type: (Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Row -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col -> x) -> List.List (Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x a) -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x a

values.name: oneOfWithFallback
values.comment:
Try a list of parsers, returning a fallback value if all fail without consuming input.

Similar to `oneOf`, but instead of failing when all parsers fail, returns the
provided fallback value. This is useful for optional syntax constructs.
values.type: List.List (Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x a) -> a -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x a

values.name: pure
values.comment:
Create a parser that always succeeds with the given value without consuming input.

This is the monadic return/pure operation. It produces an Eok result.

values.name: snippetDecoder
values.comment:
Decode a Snippet from bytes.

Decodes the fields in the same order as `snippetEncoder` (fptr, offset, length,
offRow, offCol) to reconstruct the Snippet.

values.name: snippetEncoder
values.comment:
Encode a Snippet to bytes for serialization.

Encodes all fields (fptr, offset, length, offRow, offCol) in sequence for
storage or transmission.

values.name: specialize
values.comment:
Transform parser errors by applying a context-adding function.

Similar to `inContext` but without a separate start parser. Captures the current
position and uses it to add context to any errors produced by the parser.
values.type: (x -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Row -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col -> y) -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x a -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser y a

values.name: unsafeIndex
values.comment:
Get a character at a specific index in a string without bounds checking.

This function is unsafe because it crashes if the index is out of bounds.
It should only be used when the index is known to be valid.

values.name: withBacksetIndent
values.comment:
Run a parser with the indentation level set back by a specified offset.

Sets the indent level to the current column minus the backset amount, then
restores the previous indent level after parsing. Used for handling indentation
in cases like `case` expressions where branches may be indented relative to
a previous token.
values.type: Basics.Int -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x a -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x a

values.name: withIndent
values.comment:
Run a parser with the indentation level set to the current column.

Sets the indent level to the current column before running the parser, then
restores the previous indent level afterward. This is used for indentation-
sensitive syntax like `let` blocks where nested definitions must align.
values.type: Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x a -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x a

values.name: word1
values.comment:
Parse a single specific character.

Succeeds if the next character matches the expected character, consuming it.
Fails with an Eerr if the character doesn't match or if at end of input.

values.name: word2
values.comment:
Parse a sequence of two specific characters.

Succeeds if the next two characters match the expected sequence, consuming both.
Fails with an Eerr if either character doesn't match or if there aren't enough
characters remaining.
values.type: Char.Char -> Char.Char -> (Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Row -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col -> x) -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x ()

name: Compiler.Parse.Shader
comment:
Parser for GLSL shader blocks in Elm.

This module handles parsing of shader literals marked with [glsl|...|] syntax.
It extracts the GLSL source code, parses it to identify shader inputs
(attributes, uniforms, varyings), and validates the shader syntax.


# Shader Parsing

@docs shader

values.name: shader
values.comment:
Parses a GLSL shader block enclosed in [glsl|...|].
Extracts shader types (attributes, uniforms, varyings) from the GLSL source.

name: Compiler.Parse.Space

aliases.name: Parser
aliases.comment:
A parser that returns a value along with its ending position.
Used throughout the space parsing system to track source locations.

values.name: checkAligned
values.comment:
Checks that the current column is aligned with the expected indentation level.
Must be called after chomp. Fails if the column doesn't match the indent exactly.

values.name: checkFreshLine
values.comment:
Checks that we're at the start of a new line (column 1).
Must be called after chomp. Fails if we're not at the beginning of a line.

values.name: checkIndent
values.comment:
Checks that the current column is properly indented relative to the context.
Must be called after chomp. Fails if indentation is insufficient.

values.name: chomp
values.comment:
Consumes whitespace and comments, returning any comments found.
Handles spaces, newlines, line comments (--), and block comments.
values.type: (Compiler.Reporting.Error.Syntax.Space -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Row -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col -> x) -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x Compiler.AST.Source.FComments

values.name: chompAndCheckIndent
values.comment:
Consumes whitespace and comments, then checks indentation in one operation.
More efficient than calling chomp followed by checkIndent separately.
values.type: (Compiler.Reporting.Error.Syntax.Space -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Row -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col -> x) -> (Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Row -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col -> x) -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x Compiler.AST.Source.FComments

values.name: docComment
values.comment:
Parses a documentation comment (the kind that starts with open-brace, dash, pipe).
Documentation comments are used to document exposed functions and types.
values.type: (Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> x) -> (Compiler.Reporting.Error.Syntax.Space -> Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> x) -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x Compiler.AST.Source.Comment

name: Compiler.Parse.String
comment:
Parser for string and character literals in Elm.

This module handles parsing of single-quoted character literals and double-quoted
string literals (both single-line and multi-line). It processes escape sequences
including Unicode escapes and validates string format rules.


# Parsing Strings

@docs string, character

values.name: character
values.comment:
Parses a single-quoted character literal like 'a' or '\\n'.
Handles escape sequences and Unicode escapes. Rejects multi-character and empty literals.
values.type: Compiler.Parse.SyntaxVersion.SyntaxVersion -> (Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Row -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col -> x) -> (Compiler.Reporting.Error.Syntax.Char -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Row -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col -> x) -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x String.String

values.name: string
values.comment:
Parses a string literal (single-line or multi-line).
Returns the string content and a boolean indicating if it's a multi-line string (""").
Handles escape sequences including Unicode escapes.
values.type: Compiler.Parse.SyntaxVersion.SyntaxVersion -> (Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Row -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col -> x) -> (Compiler.Reporting.Error.Syntax.String_ -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Row -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col -> x) -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x ( String.String, Basics.Bool )

name: Compiler.Parse.Symbol

unions.name: BadOperator
unions.comment: Represents operators that are reserved and cannot be used as custom infix operators.

values.name: badOperatorDecoder
values.comment: Decodes a BadOperator from bytes for deserialization.

values.name: badOperatorEncoder
values.comment: Encodes a BadOperator to bytes for serialization.

values.name: binopCharSet
values.comment:
The set of characters that can appear in binary operators.
Includes: + - / \* = . < > : & | ^ ? % !

values.name: operator
values.comment:
Parses an infix operator composed of symbolic characters.
Rejects reserved operators like '.', '|', '->', '=', and ':'.
values.type: (Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Row -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col -> x) -> (Compiler.Parse.Symbol.BadOperator -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Row -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col -> x) -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Parser x Compiler.Data.Name.Name

name: Compiler.Parse.SyntaxVersion
comment:
Compiler.Parse.SyntaxVersion

@docs SyntaxVersion, decoder, encoder, fileSyntaxVersion

unions.name: SyntaxVersion
unions.comment:
The `SyntaxVersion` type is used to specify which syntax version to work
with. It provides options to differentiate between the "legacy" Elm syntax,
which the Guida language builds upon, and the new Guida-specific syntax.

This type is useful when building parsers that need to distinguish between
the two syntactic styles and adapt behavior accordingly.

values.name: decoder
values.comment: Decodes a SyntaxVersion from bytes for deserialization.

values.name: encoder
values.comment: Encodes a SyntaxVersion to bytes for serialization.

values.name: fileSyntaxVersion
values.comment:
Returns the syntax version based on a filepath.
Files ending in .elm use Elm syntax, all others use Guida syntax.

name: Compiler.Parse.Type
comment:
Parser for type annotations and type declarations in Elm.

This module parses type expressions including type variables, type constructors,
function types, tuple types, record types, and extensible record types. It also
handles parsing type constructor variants for custom type declarations.


# Type Expressions

@docs expression


# Custom Type Variants

@docs variant

values.name: expression
values.comment:
Parse a type expression including function types, type applications, and type terms.

Handles parsing of complete type expressions such as:

  - Simple types: `Int`, `String`, `Maybe a`
  - Function types: `Int -> String`, `a -> b -> c`
  - Type applications: `List Int`, `Dict String Value`
  - Tuples: `(Int, String)`, `(a, b, c)`
  - Records: `{ x : Int, y : Int }`, `{ a | x : Int }`

Returns a tuple containing the parsed type with comments and the end position.

values.name: variant
values.comment:
Parse a custom type variant declaration.

Parses variant constructors in custom type definitions, handling:

  - Constructor name (must be uppercase)
  - Optional type arguments

Examples:

  - `Nothing` (no arguments)
  - `Just a` (one argument)
  - `Node a (Tree a) (Tree a)` (multiple arguments)

Returns the variant constructor name and its type arguments with associated comments.
values.type: Compiler.AST.Source.FComments -> Compiler.Parse.Space.Parser Compiler.Reporting.Error.Syntax.CustomType (Compiler.AST.Source.C2Eol ( Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located Compiler.Data.Name.Name, List.List (Compiler.AST.Source.C1 Compiler.AST.Source.Type) ))

name: Compiler.Parse.Variable

unions.name: Upper
unions.comment: Represents an upper-case name that may be qualified with a module prefix.

values.name: chompInnerChars
values.comment:
Consumes the inner characters of an identifier (letters, digits, underscores).
Used after consuming the initial character of a variable name.
values.type: String.String -> Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col -> ( Basics.Int, Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col )

values.name: chompLower
values.comment:
Consumes a lower-case identifier including any trailing inner characters (letters, digits, underscores).
Returns the new position and column after consuming the identifier.
values.type: String.String -> Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col -> ( Basics.Int, Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col )

values.name: chompUpper
values.comment:
Consumes an upper-case identifier including any trailing inner characters (letters, digits, underscores).
Returns the new position and column after consuming the identifier.
values.type: String.String -> Basics.Int -> Basics.Int -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col -> ( Basics.Int, Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col )

values.name: foreignAlpha
values.comment:
Parses a qualified or unqualified variable reference (upper or lower case).
Returns a Var or VarQual expression node. Examples: 'x', 'map', 'List.map', 'Maybe.Just'.

values.name: foreignUpper
values.comment:
Parses an upper-case identifier that may be qualified with a module prefix.
Examples: 'Just', 'Maybe.Just', 'Html.Attributes.class'.

values.name: getInnerWidth
values.comment:
Returns the byte width of an inner identifier character (letter, digit, or underscore).
Returns 0 if the character is not a valid inner character.

values.name: getInnerWidthHelp
values.comment:
Helper for getInnerWidth that determines byte width based on the character.
Handles ASCII alphanumeric characters, underscores, and multi-byte UTF-8 letters.

values.name: getUpperWidth
values.comment:
Returns the byte width of an upper-case starting character (1-4 bytes for UTF-8).
Returns 0 if the character is not upper-case.

values.name: isDot
values.comment: Checks if the character at the given position is a dot (.).

values.name: isReservedWord
values.comment: Checks whether a name is a reserved keyword that cannot be used as a variable name.

values.name: lower
values.comment:
Parses a lower-case identifier (variable or function name) that starts with a-z.
Rejects reserved keywords like 'if', 'then', 'case', etc.

values.name: moduleName
values.comment:
Parses a module name like 'Html', 'Json.Decode', or 'Data.Map.Internal'.
Module names consist of one or more upper-case identifiers separated by dots.

values.name: upper
values.comment: Parses an upper-case identifier (type name or constructor) that starts with A-Z.

name: Compiler.Reporting.Annotation

unions.name: Located
unions.comment:
A value annotated with its source location.

Wraps any value with information about where it appears in the source code,
enabling precise error reporting.

unions.name: Position
unions.comment:
A single position in source code, represented as row and column numbers.

Both row and column are 1-indexed (first character is at row 1, column 1).

unions.name: Region
unions.comment:
A contiguous span in source code, from a start position to an end position.

Represents the location of a syntactic construct in the source file.

values.name: at
values.comment:
Create a located value from start and end positions.

Constructs a region from the two positions and wraps the value with it.

values.name: compareLocated
values.comment:
Compare two located values based on their underlying values, ignoring location.

Useful for sorting or equality checks where location is irrelevant.

values.name: isMultiline
values.comment:
Check if a region spans multiple lines.

Returns True if the start and end positions are on different rows.

values.name: locatedDecoder
values.comment:
Decode a located value from bytes using a custom decoder for the value.

Decodes the region first, then the value using the provided decoder,
and combines them into a Located value.

values.name: locatedEncoder
values.comment:
Encode a located value to bytes using a custom encoder for the value.

Encodes the region first, then the wrapped value using the provided encoder.

values.name: merge
values.comment:
Create a located value spanning the combined region of two other located values.

Takes the start position from the first located value and the end position
from the second, wrapping a new value with this merged region.
values.type: Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located a -> Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located b -> c -> Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located c

values.name: mergeRegions
values.comment:
Combine two regions into one spanning from the start of the first to the end of the second.

Useful for representing the location of a construct that encompasses multiple sub-parts.

values.name: one
values.comment:
A zero-width region at position (1, 1).

Represents the very beginning of a source file.

values.name: regionDecoder
values.comment:
Decode a region from bytes.

Expects the bytes to contain a start position followed by an end position.

values.name: regionEncoder
values.comment:
Encode a region to bytes for serialization.

Encodes both the start and end positions sequentially.

values.name: toRegion
values.comment: Extract the region from a located value, discarding the value itself.

values.name: toValue
values.comment: Extract the value from a located wrapper, discarding location information.

values.name: traverse
values.comment:
Apply an IO-producing function to a located value, preserving its location.

This is a monadic map operation that transforms the value while keeping
the region annotation intact.
values.type: (a -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO b) -> Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located a -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO (Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Located b)

values.name: zero
values.comment:
A zero-width region at position (0, 0).

Used for synthetic or compiler-generated constructs with no source location.

name: Compiler.Reporting.Doc

unions.name: Color
unions.comment: Terminal color values, with lowercase for dull colors and uppercase for vivid colors.

aliases.name: Doc
aliases.comment: The core document type for pretty-printing.

values.name: a
values.comment: Append two documents with a space in between.

values.name: align
values.comment: Align a document by adding appropriate indentation to all subsequent lines.

values.name: append
values.comment: Concatenate two documents without any space between them.

values.name: args
values.comment: Format a count of arguments with proper singular/plural handling (e.g., "1 argument" or "2 arguments").

values.name: black
values.comment: Apply black color to a document.

values.name: blue
values.comment: Apply vivid blue color to a document.

values.name: cat
values.comment: Concatenate documents horizontally without spaces, adding line breaks only when necessary.

values.name: commaSep
values.comment:
Format a list with commas and a conjunction (e.g., "a, b, and c").
The first argument is the conjunction, the second is a styling function,
and the third is the list of documents to format.
values.type: Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc -> (Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc -> Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc) -> List.List Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc -> List.List Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc

values.name: cyan
values.comment: Apply vivid cyan color to a document.

values.name: cycle
values.comment:
Create a visual cycle diagram showing circular dependencies between names.
The diagram is indented by the specified amount and shows arrows connecting the names in a cycle.

values.name: dullcyan
values.comment: Apply dull cyan color to a document.

values.name: dullred
values.comment: Apply dull red color to a document.

values.name: dullyellow
values.comment: Apply dull yellow color to a document.

values.name: empty
values.comment: An empty document.

values.name: encode
values.comment: Encode a Doc to JSON, preserving styling information (bold, underline, colors).

values.name: fancyLink
values.comment:
Create a link with an underlined word, formatted docs before, a URL to a file,
and formatted docs after.
values.type: String.String -> List.List Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc -> String.String -> List.List Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc -> Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc

values.name: fill
values.comment: Fill a document to the specified width by adding spaces on the right.

values.name: fillSep
values.comment: Concatenate documents with spaces, adding line breaks when they don't fit on one line.

values.name: fromChars
values.comment: Convert a string to a Doc.

values.name: fromInt
values.comment: Convert an integer to a Doc.

values.name: fromName
values.comment: Convert a Name to a Doc.

values.name: fromPackage
values.comment: Convert a package name to a Doc.

values.name: fromVersion
values.comment: Convert a version to a Doc.

values.name: green
values.comment: Apply vivid green color to a document.

values.name: hang
values.comment: Hang a document by indenting all lines except the first by the specified amount.

values.name: hcat
values.comment: Concatenate documents horizontally without any separation.

values.name: hsep
values.comment: Concatenate documents horizontally with spaces between them.

values.name: indent
values.comment: Indent a document by the specified number of spaces.

values.name: intToOrdinal
values.comment: Convert an integer to an ordinal string (e.g., "1st", "2nd", "3rd", "4th").

values.name: link
values.comment: Create a link with an underlined word, text before, a URL to a file, and text after.

values.name: makeLink
values.comment: Create a full link URL in angle brackets pointing to elm-lang.org documentation.

values.name: makeNakedLink
values.comment: Create a bare link URL (without angle brackets) pointing to elm-lang.org documentation.

values.name: ordinal
values.comment: Convert a zero-based index to an ordinal string (e.g., "1st", "2nd", "3rd", "4th").

values.name: plus
values.comment: Append two documents with a space in between (synonym for `a`).

values.name: red
values.comment: Apply vivid red color to a document.

values.name: reflow
values.comment: Reflow a paragraph of text, breaking it into words and filling lines optimally.

values.name: reflowLink
values.comment: Create a reflowed text block with an embedded link.

values.name: sep
values.comment: Concatenate documents with spaces, or with line breaks if they don't fit.

values.name: stack
values.comment: Stack documents vertically with blank lines between them.

values.name: toAnsi
values.comment: Render a Doc to ANSI output with color support for the given handle.

values.name: toFancyHint
values.comment: Create a hint from a list of formatted document chunks.

values.name: toFancyNote
values.comment: Create a note from a list of formatted document chunks.

values.name: toLine
values.comment: Render a Doc to a single line string without any line breaks.

values.name: toSimpleHint
values.comment: Create a hint from a simple string message.

values.name: toSimpleNote
values.comment: Create a note from a simple string message.

values.name: toString
values.comment: Render a Doc to a plain string without any ANSI color codes.

values.name: vcat
values.comment: Concatenate documents vertically.

values.name: yellow
values.comment: Apply vivid yellow color to a document.

name: Compiler.Reporting.Error

unions.name: Error
unions.comment:
Errors that can occur during different phases of compilation.

Each variant corresponds to a specific compiler phase:

  - `BadSyntax` - Parse errors in source code
  - `BadImports` - Problems resolving imports
  - `BadNames` - Name resolution and canonicalization errors
  - `BadTypes` - Type checking errors
  - `BadMains` - Invalid main function definitions
  - `BadPatterns` - Non-exhaustive pattern matches
  - `BadDocs` - Malformed documentation comments

aliases.name: Module
aliases.comment:
Represents a module that failed to compile, along with its error context.

Contains the module name, file path, modification time, source code, and the
specific error that occurred during compilation.
aliases.type: { name : Compiler.Elm.ModuleName.Raw, absolutePath : String.String, modificationTime : Builder.File.Time, source : String.String, error : Compiler.Reporting.Error.Error }

values.name: moduleDecoder
values.comment: Decode a module's error from binary cache.

values.name: moduleEncoder
values.comment: Encode a module's error for binary caching.

values.name: reportToJson
values.comment:
Convert a single report to JSON format.

Produces a JSON object with the error title, source region (line/column positions),
and formatted message. Used by `toJson` to serialize individual error reports.

values.name: toDoc
values.comment:
Format compilation errors for terminal display.

Sorts modules by modification time and formats each error with a visual
separator. Returns a Doc that can be rendered with color to the terminal.

values.name: toJson
values.comment:
Serialize a module's errors to JSON format.

Produces a JSON object with the module path, name, and a list of problems.
Used by editor integrations and build tools to consume compiler errors.

name: Compiler.Reporting.Error.Canonicalize

unions.name: BadArityContext
unions.comment: Context for arity mismatch errors, indicating whether the error occurred in a type or pattern.

unions.name: DuplicatePatternContext
unions.comment: Context indicating where duplicate pattern variables were found.

unions.name: Error
unions.comment: Represents errors that occur during canonicalization of Elm source code.

unions.name: InvalidPayload
unions.comment: Describes why a port's payload type is invalid for communication with JavaScript.

unions.name: PortProblem
unions.comment: Describes structural problems with port type signatures.

unions.name: VarKind
unions.comment: Categorizes the kind of name that was not found or exported incorrectly.

aliases.name: PossibleNames
aliases.comment: Collection of locally defined names and qualified names available in scope for suggesting alternatives.
aliases.type: { locals : Data.Set.EverySet String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name, quals : Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name (Data.Set.EverySet String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name) }

values.name: errorDecoder
values.comment: Deserialize a canonicalization error from bytes.

values.name: errorEncoder
values.comment: Serialize a canonicalization error to bytes for caching or transmission.

values.name: invalidPayloadDecoder
values.comment: Deserialize an invalid port payload type from bytes.

values.name: invalidPayloadEncoder
values.comment: Serialize an invalid port payload type to bytes.

values.name: toReport
values.comment:
Convert a canonicalization error into a user-friendly error report with
source code snippets and helpful suggestions.

name: Compiler.Reporting.Error.Docs

unions.name: DefProblem
unions.comment: Represents missing documentation or type annotations on definitions.

unions.name: Error
unions.comment: Represents various errors that can occur during documentation validation.

unions.name: NameProblem
unions.comment: Represents mismatches between names in documentation and the exposing list.

unions.name: SyntaxProblem
unions.comment: Represents syntax errors found while parsing documentation comments.

values.name: errorDecoder
values.comment: Deserialize a documentation error from bytes.

values.name: errorEncoder
values.comment: Serialize a documentation error to bytes for caching or transmission.

values.name: toReports
values.comment:
Convert documentation validation errors into one or more user-friendly error
reports, helping package authors write proper documentation comments.

name: Compiler.Reporting.Error.Import

unions.name: Error
unions.comment: Represents an import error with location and problem details.

unions.name: Problem
unions.comment: Specific import problems: module not found or ambiguous due to multiple definitions.

aliases.name: ErrorProps
aliases.comment: Properties that describe an import error, including location, module name, and the specific problem.
aliases.type: { region : Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Region, name : Compiler.Elm.ModuleName.Raw, unimportedModules : Data.Set.EverySet String.String Compiler.Elm.ModuleName.Raw, problem : Compiler.Reporting.Error.Import.Problem }

values.name: errorDecoder
values.comment: Deserialize an import error from bytes.

values.name: errorEncoder
values.comment: Serialize an import error to bytes for caching or transmission.

values.name: problemDecoder
values.comment: Deserialize an import problem from bytes.

values.name: problemEncoder
values.comment: Serialize an import problem to bytes.

values.name: toReport
values.comment:
Convert an import error into a user-friendly error report with suggestions
for fixing module not found, ambiguous imports, or name conflict issues.

name: Compiler.Reporting.Error.Json
comment:
Error reporting for JSON parsing and decoding failures.

This module converts low-level JSON parse errors and decode problems into
user-friendly error reports with source code snippets and helpful suggestions.


# Error Reporting

@docs toReport


# Error Context

@docs Context, FailureToReport, Reason

unions.name: Context
unions.comment:
Tracks the path through the JSON structure to the error location.
Used to provide context like "in the dependencies field" or "in the 3rd array element."

unions.name: FailureToReport
unions.comment:
A function that converts custom decode failures into error reports.
This allows different JSON decoders to provide specialized error messages.
cases: String.String -> Compiler.Reporting.Render.Code.Source -> Compiler.Reporting.Error.Json.Context -> Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Region -> x -> Builder.Reporting.Exit.Help.Report

unions.name: Reason
unions.comment:
The reason a JSON value is being parsed, used in error messages.
For example, "I need this because it contains package dependencies."

values.name: toReport
values.comment:
Convert a JSON parsing or decoding error into a user-friendly error report
with source code snippets highlighting the problematic JSON location.
values.type: String.String -> Compiler.Reporting.Error.Json.FailureToReport x -> Compiler.Json.Decode.Error x -> Compiler.Reporting.Error.Json.Reason -> Builder.Reporting.Exit.Help.Report

name: Compiler.Reporting.Error.Main

unions.name: Error
unions.comment: Represents errors that can occur with the main entry point of an Elm program.

values.name: errorDecoder
values.comment: Deserialize a main entry point error from bytes.

values.name: errorEncoder
values.comment: Serialize a main entry point error to bytes for caching or transmission.

values.name: toReport
values.comment:
Convert a main entry point error into a user-friendly error report,
explaining type compatibility issues, recursion problems, or invalid flags.

name: Compiler.Reporting.Error.Pattern
comment:
Error reporting for pattern matching problems.

This module converts pattern match analysis results into user-friendly error
reports, including errors for redundant patterns and incomplete case coverage.


# Reporting

@docs toReport

values.name: toReport
values.comment:
Convert a pattern matching error (redundant patterns or incomplete coverage)
into a user-friendly error report with examples of unhandled cases.

name: Compiler.Reporting.Error.Syntax

unions.name: Case
unions.comment: Errors in case expression syntax, including patterns, arrows, branches, and alignment.

unions.name: Char
unions.comment: Errors specific to character literal parsing, including escape sequences and delimiters.

unions.name: CustomType
unions.comment: Errors in custom type (union type) declarations, including variant names and arguments.

unions.name: Decl
unions.comment: Errors in top-level declarations including ports, type declarations, and definitions.

unions.name: DeclDef
unions.comment: Errors in function and value definition syntax, including type annotations, arguments, and bodies.

unions.name: DeclType
unions.comment: Errors in type declaration syntax, dispatching to type alias or custom type errors.

unions.name: Def
unions.comment: Errors in let-binding definition syntax, including type annotations, arguments, and bodies.

unions.name: Destruct
unions.comment: Errors in destructuring let-bindings, where patterns extract values from expressions.

unions.name: Error
unions.comment: Top-level syntax errors including module name issues, port violations, and parse errors.

unions.name: Escape
unions.comment: Errors in escape sequence parsing, including invalid unicode escapes and unknown sequences.

unions.name: Exposing
unions.comment: Errors related to module exposing lists, including values, operators, and type privacy.

unions.name: Expr
unions.comment: Errors in expression syntax, covering all expression forms from literals to complex structures.

unions.name: Func
unions.comment: Errors in anonymous function (lambda) syntax, including arguments, arrows, and body.

unions.name: If
unions.comment: Errors in if-then-else expression syntax, including condition, branches, and keywords.

unions.name: Let
unions.comment: Errors in let-in expression syntax, including definitions, destructuring, and alignment.

unions.name: List_
unions.comment: Errors in list expression syntax, including brackets and element expressions.

unions.name: Module
unions.comment: Errors that occur while parsing module declarations, imports, and top-level structure.

unions.name: Number
unions.comment: Errors in numeric literal parsing, including invalid formats, digits, and underscore placement.

unions.name: PList
unions.comment: Errors in list pattern syntax, including element patterns and brackets.

unions.name: PRecord
unions.comment: Errors in record pattern syntax, including field names and braces.

unions.name: PTuple
unions.comment: Errors in tuple pattern syntax, including element patterns and parentheses.

unions.name: Pattern
unions.comment: Errors in pattern syntax, covering all pattern forms including literals and structures.

unions.name: Port
unions.comment: Errors specific to port declaration syntax, including name and type annotation issues.

unions.name: Record
unions.comment: Errors in record expression syntax, including field names, equals signs, and values.

unions.name: Space
unions.comment: Errors related to whitespace and comments, such as tabs or unterminated multi-line comments.

unions.name: String_
unions.comment: Errors specific to string literal parsing, including unterminated strings and escape sequences.

unions.name: TRecord
unions.comment: Errors in record type syntax, including field names, colons, and field types.

unions.name: TTuple
unions.comment: Errors in tuple type syntax, including element types and parentheses.

unions.name: Tuple
unions.comment: Errors in tuple expression syntax, including element expressions and operators.

unions.name: Type
unions.comment: Errors in type annotation syntax, covering all type expression forms.

unions.name: TypeAlias
unions.comment: Errors in type alias declarations, including name, equals sign, and body type syntax.

values.name: errorDecoder
values.comment: Deserialize a syntax error from bytes.

values.name: errorEncoder
values.comment: Serialize a syntax error to bytes for caching or transmission.

values.name: spaceDecoder
values.comment: Deserialize a whitespace error from bytes.

values.name: spaceEncoder
values.comment: Serialize a whitespace error to bytes.

values.name: toReport
values.comment:
Convert a syntax error into a user-friendly error report with source code
snippets and suggestions for fixing parsing problems.

values.name: toSpaceReport
values.comment:
Convert a whitespace-related syntax error into a user-friendly error report,
handling issues with tabs, spaces, and indentation.

name: Compiler.Reporting.Error.Type

unions.name: Category
unions.comment: Category of expression that has a type error, used for generating appropriate error messages.

unions.name: Context
unions.comment: Context describing where in an expression a type expectation originated.

unions.name: Error
unions.comment: Represents type errors discovered during type checking and inference.

unions.name: Expected
unions.comment: Expected type for an expression, with optional context about where the expectation came from.

unions.name: MaybeName
unions.comment: Optional name for a function, constructor, or operator involved in a type error.

unions.name: PCategory
unions.comment: Category of pattern that has a type error, used for generating appropriate error messages.

unions.name: PContext
unions.comment: Context describing where in a pattern a type expectation originated.

unions.name: PExpected
unions.comment: Expected type for a pattern, with optional context about where the expectation came from.

unions.name: SubContext
unions.comment: Sub-context for type annotations, indicating where within an annotated definition the mismatch occurred.

values.name: errorDecoder
values.comment: Deserialize a type error from bytes.

values.name: errorEncoder
values.comment: Serialize a type error to bytes for caching or transmission.

values.name: ptypeReplace
values.comment:
Replace the type within a PExpected (pattern expected) value while preserving
the error context. Used during pattern type checking.

values.name: toReport
values.comment:
Convert a type error into a user-friendly error report with type diffs,
helpful hints, and suggestions for fixing type mismatches.

values.name: typeReplace
values.comment:
Replace the type within an Expected value while preserving the error context.
Used during type inference to substitute concrete types for type variables.

name: Compiler.Reporting.Render.Code

unions.name: Next
unions.comment:
Represents the next syntactic element at a position in the source code.
Used to provide context-aware error messages.

aliases.name: Source
aliases.comment:
Represents source code as a list of line numbers paired with their content.
Line numbers start at 1.

values.name: nextLineStartsWithCloseCurly
values.comment:
Checks if the next line starts with a closing curly brace `}`. Returns the
position if found.

values.name: nextLineStartsWithKeyword
values.comment:
Checks if the next line starts with the specified keyword. Returns the
position of the keyword if found.
values.type: String.String -> Compiler.Reporting.Render.Code.Source -> Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Row -> Maybe.Maybe ( Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Row, Compiler.Parse.Primitives.Col )

values.name: toPair
values.comment:
Renders two related code regions, either on a single line or as separate chunks.
Takes documentation for single-line and two-chunk cases respectively.
values.type: Compiler.Reporting.Render.Code.Source -> Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Region -> Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Region -> ( Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc, Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc ) -> ( Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc, Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc, Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc ) -> Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc

values.name: toSnippet
values.comment:
Renders a code snippet showing the specified region with optional highlighting
of a sub-region. Takes pre-hint and post-hint documentation to display before and
after the code snippet.
values.type: Compiler.Reporting.Render.Code.Source -> Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Region -> Maybe.Maybe Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Region -> ( Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc, Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc ) -> Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc

values.name: toSource
values.comment:
Converts raw source code text into a Source representation, splitting on
newlines and adding line numbers starting from 1.

values.name: whatIsNext
values.comment:
Analyzes the source code at the given row and column to determine what
syntactic element appears next (keyword, operator, identifier, etc.).

name: Compiler.Reporting.Render.Type

unions.name: Context
unions.comment:
Parenthesization context for type rendering. Determines whether parentheses
are needed around a type expression based on where it appears.

values.name: apply
values.comment:
Renders a type application (type constructor applied to arguments) with
proper formatting and context-aware parenthesization.

values.name: canToDoc
values.comment:
Converts a canonical type (after type checking and resolution) into a
formatted Doc for display, using the localizer to determine the best way to
display qualified type names.

values.name: lambda
values.comment:
Renders a function type with proper formatting and context-aware parenthesization.
Takes the first two arguments and any additional arguments, formatting them as
`arg1 -> arg2 -> ... -> result`.
values.type: Compiler.Reporting.Render.Type.Context -> Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc -> Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc -> List.List Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc -> Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc

values.name: record
values.comment:
Renders a record type with horizontal layout. Takes field name/type pairs
and an optional extension variable, formatting as `{ field : Type, ... }` or
`{ ext | field : Type, ... }`.
values.type: List.List ( Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc, Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc ) -> Maybe.Maybe Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc -> Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc

values.name: srcToDoc
values.comment:
Converts a source-level type (as parsed from user code) into a formatted
Doc for display in error messages and documentation.

values.name: tuple
values.comment:
Renders a tuple type with proper formatting. Takes at least two elements
(tuples in Elm have 2 or more elements) and formats them as `( a, b, ... )`.

values.name: vrecord
values.comment:
Renders a record type with vertical layout (each field on its own line).
Takes field name/type pairs and an optional extension variable.
values.type: List.List ( Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc, Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc ) -> Maybe.Maybe Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc -> Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc

values.name: vrecordSnippet
values.comment:
Renders a partial record type snippet with vertical layout, showing the first
field and indicating additional fields with `...`. Used for abbreviated error messages.
values.type: ( Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc, Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc ) -> List.List ( Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc, Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc ) -> Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc

name: Compiler.Reporting.Render.Type.Localizer

unions.name: Localizer
unions.comment:
Tracks import context to determine how to display qualified type names.
Encapsulates information about module imports, aliases, and exposed types.

values.name: empty
values.comment:
Creates an empty localizer with no import information. Type names will be
displayed fully qualified.

values.name: fromModule
values.comment:
Creates a localizer from a source module, extracting import information to
determine how types should be displayed based on the module's import statements.

values.name: fromNames
values.comment:
Creates a localizer from a dictionary of names, treating all as fully exposed.
Useful when all names are in scope without qualification.

values.name: localizerDecoder
values.comment: Decodes a Localizer from bytes for deserialization.

values.name: localizerEncoder
values.comment: Encodes a Localizer to bytes for serialization.

values.name: toChars
values.comment:
Converts a qualified type name to a String using the shortest unambiguous form
based on the import context (bare name, aliased name, or fully qualified).

values.name: toDoc
values.comment:
Converts a qualified type name to a Doc using the shortest unambiguous form
based on the import context (bare name, aliased name, or fully qualified).

name: Compiler.Reporting.Report
comment:
Core data structure for compiler error and warning reports.

A Report packages together all the information needed to display a helpful
compiler diagnostic message: a title, source location, suggested fixes, and
formatted documentation describing the issue.


# Types

@docs Report, ReportProps


# Construction

@docs report

unions.name: Report
unions.comment:
A compiler diagnostic report containing all information needed to display
a helpful error or warning message to the user.

aliases.name: ReportProps
aliases.comment:
The properties that make up a compiler report: a title summarizing the issue,
the source region where it occurred, suggested fixes, and detailed documentation.
aliases.type: { title : String.String, region : Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Region, suggestions : List.List String.String, doc : Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc }

values.name: report
values.comment:
Helper constructor for backward compatibility.
Allows existing code to continue using positional arguments:
`Report.report "title" region suggestions doc`
values.type: String.String -> Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Region -> List.List String.String -> Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc -> Compiler.Reporting.Report.Report

name: Compiler.Reporting.Result

unions.name: RResult
unions.comment:
A Result monad that threads compiler info and warnings through computations.

Wraps a function that takes current info and warnings state, and produces a step
result that may succeed with a value or fail with one or more errors.
cases: info -> warnings -> Compiler.Reporting.Result.RStep info warnings error a

unions.name: RStep
unions.comment:
Represents the result of a single step in an RResult computation.

Either succeeds with updated info, warnings, and a value, or fails with
accumulated errors.

unions.name: Step
unions.comment:
Represents a step in a loop computation.

Loop continues with new state, Done completes with final value.

values.name: andThen
values.comment:
Chain RResult computations sequentially.

If the first computation succeeds, its value is passed to the callback to
produce the next computation. Info and warnings thread through both steps.
values.type: (a -> Compiler.Reporting.Result.RResult i w x b) -> Compiler.Reporting.Result.RResult i w x a -> Compiler.Reporting.Result.RResult i w x b

values.name: apply
values.comment:
Apply a function wrapped in an RResult to a value wrapped in an RResult.

Runs both computations in sequence, threading info and warnings through both.
If both fail, errors are accumulated using OneOrMore.more.
values.type: Compiler.Reporting.Result.RResult i w x a -> Compiler.Reporting.Result.RResult i w x (a -> b) -> Compiler.Reporting.Result.RResult i w x b

values.name: indexedTraverse
values.comment:
Traverse a list with an index-aware function.

Applies the function to each element along with its zero-based index,
accumulating results while threading RResult state through the computation.
values.type: (Compiler.Data.Index.ZeroBased -> a -> Compiler.Reporting.Result.RResult i w error b) -> List.List a -> Compiler.Reporting.Result.RResult i w error (List.List b)

values.name: loop
values.comment:
Repeatedly apply a function to a state until it returns Done.

Allows implementing tail-recursive loops within the RResult monad while
threading info and warnings through each iteration.
values.type: (state -> Compiler.Reporting.Result.RResult i w e (Compiler.Reporting.Result.Step state a)) -> state -> Compiler.Reporting.Result.RResult i w e a

values.name: map
values.comment:
Transform the value inside a successful RResult.

If the computation failed, the error is preserved unchanged.
values.type: (a -> b) -> Compiler.Reporting.Result.RResult i w e a -> Compiler.Reporting.Result.RResult i w e b

values.name: mapTraverseWithKey
values.comment:
Traverse a dictionary with a key-aware function, building a new dictionary.

Applies the function to each key-value pair in the dictionary, threading RResult
state through each application. Uses loop for tail-recursive efficiency.
values.type: (k -> comparable) -> (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> (k -> a -> Compiler.Reporting.Result.RResult i w x b) -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k a -> Compiler.Reporting.Result.RResult i w x (Data.Map.Dict comparable k b)

values.name: ok
values.comment:
Create a successful RResult with a value.

Does not modify info or warnings state.
values.type: a -> Compiler.Reporting.Result.RResult i w e a

values.name: run
values.comment:
Execute an RResult computation with empty initial state.

Returns accumulated warnings and either a successful value or non-empty list of errors.
Warnings are returned in the order they were added.
values.type: Compiler.Reporting.Result.RResult () (List.List w) e a -> ( List.List w, Result.Result (Compiler.Data.OneOrMore.OneOrMore e) a )

values.name: throw
values.comment:
Create a failed RResult with a single error.

Terminates the computation with the given error.
values.type: e -> Compiler.Reporting.Result.RResult i w e a

values.name: traverse
values.comment:
Apply a function to each element of a list, accumulating results.

Threads info and warnings through each element in sequence. If any application
fails, errors are accumulated. Returns the list of results in original order.
values.type: (a -> Compiler.Reporting.Result.RResult i w x b) -> List.List a -> Compiler.Reporting.Result.RResult i w x (List.List b)

values.name: warn
values.comment:
Add a warning to the current computation without failing.

Warnings accumulate in the order they are added.

name: Compiler.Reporting.Suggest
comment:
Suggestion generation based on string similarity.

This module uses Levenshtein distance to generate helpful suggestions when
the compiler encounters an unknown name, helping users quickly identify typos
and similar alternatives.


# Suggestions

@docs sort, rank

values.name: rank
values.comment:
Rank candidates by their Levenshtein distance from the target string.

Returns a list of tuples where each tuple contains the edit distance and the original
candidate. The list is sorted by distance in ascending order, with the closest matches
first. Case is ignored when computing distance.
values.type: String.String -> (a -> String.String) -> List.List a -> List.List ( Basics.Int, a )

values.name: sort
values.comment:
Sort a list of candidates by their Levenshtein distance from the target string.

The candidates are sorted in ascending order of edit distance, so the most similar
strings appear first. Case is ignored when computing distance.
values.type: String.String -> (a -> String.String) -> List.List a -> List.List a

name: Compiler.Reporting.Warning
comment:
Compiler warnings for code quality issues.

This module defines warnings that the compiler can emit for code that is
syntactically correct but potentially problematic. Unlike errors, warnings
do not prevent compilation but help developers write cleaner, more
maintainable code.


# Types

@docs Warning, Context


# Reporting

unions.name: Context
unions.comment:
Describes the context where an unused variable was found.

The context affects how the warning message is phrased and what suggestions
are provided to the user.

unions.name: Warning
unions.comment:
Represents a compiler warning that indicates potentially problematic code.

Warnings are issued for code quality issues such as unused imports, unused
variables, or missing type annotations. Unlike errors, warnings do not prevent
compilation.

name: Compiler.Type.Constrain.Common

unions.name: Args
unions.comment:
Wrapper for argument constraint information, containing type variables,
the overall function type, result type, and pattern state from argument patterns.

unions.name: Info
unions.comment:
Internal type for accumulating information about recursive definitions.
Tracks type variables, constraints, and type headers for both rigid (typed)
and flexible (untyped) definitions.

unions.name: State
unions.comment:
State accumulated during pattern constraint generation.

Contains the header (variables introduced by the pattern with their types),
a list of flexible type variables created during constraint generation,
and constraints stored in reverse order for efficient appending.

unions.name: TypedArgs
unions.comment:
Information about typed function arguments including the full function type,
the result type, and the pattern state from argument patterns.

aliases.name: ArgsProps
aliases.comment:
Properties for constrained function arguments including:

  - vars: Type variables introduced for arguments and result
  - tipe: The full function type (arg1 -> arg2 -> ... -> result)
  - result: The result type of the function
  - state: Pattern matching state from argument patterns
aliases.type: { vars : List.List System.TypeCheck.IO.Variable, tipe : Compiler.Type.Type.Type, result : Compiler.Type.Type.Type, state : Compiler.Type.Constrain.Common.State }

aliases.name: Header
aliases.comment:
Header maps variable names to their types with source locations.

Records all variables introduced by a pattern, associating each name
with its inferred type and the region where it was bound.

aliases.name: RigidTypeVar
aliases.comment:
As we step past type annotations, the free type variables are added to
the "rigid type variables" dict. Allowing sharing of rigid variables
between nested type annotations.

So if you have a top-level type annotation like (func : a -> b) the RTV
dictionary will hold variables for `a` and `b`

values.name: addToHeaders
values.comment: Add a variable to the pattern headers.
values.type: Compiler.Reporting.Annotation.Region -> Compiler.Data.Name.Name -> Compiler.Reporting.Error.Type.PExpected Compiler.Type.Type.Type -> Compiler.Type.Constrain.Common.State -> Compiler.Type.Constrain.Common.State

values.name: emptyInfo
values.comment: Empty Info structure with no variables, constraints, or headers.

values.name: emptyState
values.comment:
Initial empty state for pattern constraint generation.

Contains no variable bindings, no type variables, and no constraints.

values.name: extractVarFromType
values.comment:
Try to extract a variable from a type.
Returns Just var if the type is VarN var, Nothing otherwise.

values.name: getAccessName
values.comment:
Extract the variable name from an expression being accessed (e.g., in record
access). Returns Nothing if the expression is not a simple variable reference.

values.name: getName
values.comment:
Extract the name from an expression for better error messages. Returns
FuncName, CtorName, OpName, or NoName depending on the expression form.

values.name: getType
values.comment: Extract the type from a pattern expectation.

values.name: glToType
values.comment:
Convert a GLSL/WebGL type to the corresponding Elm type (e.g., V2 becomes
vec2, Float becomes float).

values.name: makeArgs
values.comment:
Construct an Args value from its components: type variables, function type,
result type, and pattern state.
values.type: List.List System.TypeCheck.IO.Variable -> Compiler.Type.Type.Type -> Compiler.Type.Type.Type -> Compiler.Type.Constrain.Common.State -> Compiler.Type.Constrain.Common.Args

values.name: patternNeedsConstraint
values.comment:
Determine if a pattern needs a CPattern constraint.

Patterns that DON'T need constraints (matching `add` behavior):

  - PAnything: just returns state unchanged
  - PVar: just updates headers, no structural constraint
  - PAlias: delegates to inner pattern, no constraint for the alias itself

All other patterns need CPattern constraints to enforce their structure.

values.name: patternToCategory
values.comment:
Determine the appropriate PCategory for a pattern node.
This matches the categories used in the constraint generation.

values.name: toShaderRecord
values.comment:
Convert a dictionary of shader types to a record type. If the dictionary
is empty, returns the base record type; otherwise constructs a record with
the shader types mapped to Elm types.
values.type: Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Compiler.AST.Utils.Shader.Type -> Compiler.Type.Type.Type -> Compiler.Type.Type.Type

name: Compiler.Type.Constrain.Erased.Expression
comment:
Type constraint generation for expressions (Erased pathway).

This module walks through canonical expression AST nodes and generates type constraints
that will be solved during type inference. It handles all expression forms including
literals, variables, function calls, pattern matching, records, and more.

The constraint generation process creates relationships between types (e.g., "this
function argument must have the same type as this parameter") without immediately
solving them. The actual unification happens in a separate solving phase.


# Constraint Generation

@docs constrainDef, constrainRecursiveDefs

values.name: constrainDef
values.comment:
Generate constraints for a single definition in a let-expression. Handles
both unannotated definitions (where types are inferred) and typed definitions
(where explicit type annotations guide constraint generation).

Uses the stack-safe DSL internally.

values.name: constrainRecursiveDefs
values.comment:
Generate constraints for a group of mutually recursive definitions in a
let-rec expression. Handles both typed and untyped definitions, ensuring that
recursive references are properly constrained.

Uses the stack-safe DSL internally.

name: Compiler.Type.Constrain.Erased.Module
comment:
Generates type constraints for Elm modules during type checking (Erased pathway).

This is the entry point for constraint generation. It traverses the module's
declarations, effects (ports, managers), and builds a constraint tree that
the constraint solver will use to infer or verify types.


# Constraint Generation

@docs constrain

values.name: constrain
values.comment:
Generate type constraints for a canonical module.

Handles regular declarations, ports, and effect managers by traversing the
module's structure and producing a constraint tree that represents all type
relationships.

name: Compiler.Type.Constrain.Erased.Pattern
comment:
Type constraint generation for pattern matching (Erased pathway).

This module generates type constraints for patterns used in case expressions, function
arguments, let bindings, and destructuring assignments. Patterns introduce new variables
into scope and constrain their types based on the pattern structure (e.g., a list pattern
constrains the matched value to be a list).

Uses an internal PatternProg DSL for stack-safe constraint generation,
preventing stack overflow on deeply nested patterns.


# Constraint Generation

@docs add

values.name: add
values.comment:
Generate type constraints for a pattern.

Takes a pattern, an expected type, and the current state, and returns
updated state with new constraints and variable bindings. Handles all
pattern forms including literals, variables, tuples, lists, records,
and custom type constructors.

Uses the stack-safe PatternProg DSL internally.
values.type: Compiler.AST.Canonical.Pattern -> Compiler.Reporting.Error.Type.PExpected Compiler.Type.Type.Type -> Compiler.Type.Constrain.Common.State -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO Compiler.Type.Constrain.Common.State

name: Compiler.Type.Constrain.Erased.Program
comment:
Defunctionalized DSL for constraint generation (Erased pathway).

This module provides a stack-safe way to build type constraints by representing
constraint-generation steps as data rather than nested closures. The `Prog` type
captures operations like "allocate a flex var" as instructions that are
interpreted by a tail-recursive loop.

The key insight is that deeply nested `IO.andThen` chains grow the JavaScript
call stack proportionally to AST depth. By reifying these operations as data
and interpreting them with an explicit continuation stack, we maintain constant
stack depth regardless of AST complexity.


# Program Type

@docs Prog, Instr


# Monad Operations

@docs pure, map, andThen


# Running Programs

@docs run


# Smart Constructors

@docs opMkFlexVar, opMkFlexNumber
@docs opIO

unions.name: Instr
unions.comment:
One instruction in the constraint DSL.

Each instruction captures the operation to perform and a continuation
that receives the result and produces the next program.

  - `MkFlexVar k` - Allocate a fresh flex var, continue with `k var`
  - `MkFlexNumber k` - Allocate a fresh number var, continue with `k var`
  - `RunIO io` - Run an IO action, wrapping the result in Done

unions.name: Prog
unions.comment:
A program representing constraint-generation steps.

  - `Done a` - Computation complete with value `a`
  - `Step instr` - One instruction to execute, with continuation

values.name: andThen
values.comment: Sequence two programs, passing the result of the first to the second.

values.name: map
values.comment: Map a function over the program result.

values.name: opIO
values.comment:
Run an IO action that produces a value, then continue.
This is used to delegate to existing IO-based functions.

values.name: opMkFlexNumber
values.comment: Allocate a fresh flexible number type variable.

values.name: opMkFlexVar
values.comment: Allocate a fresh flexible type variable.

values.name: pure
values.comment: Lift a pure value into the program.

values.name: run
values.comment:
Run a constraint program to produce an IO Constraint.

Uses `IO.loop` to interpret the program with an explicit continuation stack,
ensuring constant stack depth regardless of program complexity.

name: Compiler.Type.Constrain.Typed.Expression

aliases.name: ExprIdState
aliases.comment: State for tracking node ID → Variable mappings during constraint generation.

values.name: constrainArgsWithIds
values.comment:
Generate constraints for a list of function argument patterns,
also tracking pattern IDs in the NodeIdState.

values.name: constrainDefWithIds
values.comment: Generate constraints for a definition, also tracking node IDs (expressions and patterns).

values.name: constrainRecursiveDefsWithIds
values.comment: Generate constraints for recursive definitions, also tracking node IDs (expressions and patterns).

values.name: constrainTypedArgsWithIds
values.comment:
Generate constraints for explicitly typed function arguments,
also tracking pattern IDs in the NodeIdState.
values.type: Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Compiler.Type.Type.Type -> Compiler.Data.Name.Name -> List.List ( Compiler.AST.Canonical.Pattern, Compiler.AST.Canonical.Type ) -> Compiler.AST.Canonical.Type -> Compiler.Type.Constrain.Typed.NodeIds.NodeIdState -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO ( Compiler.Type.Constrain.Common.TypedArgs, Compiler.Type.Constrain.Typed.NodeIds.NodeIdState )

values.name: constrainWithIds
values.comment:
Generate constraints for an expression, tracking expression ID → Variable mapping.

This records the expression's ID and the type variable created for it in the state,
allowing post-solving conversion of variables to types.

values.name: emptyExprIdState
values.comment: Initial empty state for node ID tracking.

name: Compiler.Type.Constrain.Typed.Module
comment:
Generates type constraints for Elm modules during type checking (Typed pathway).

This is the entry point for constraint generation with ID tracking. It traverses
the module's declarations, effects (ports, managers), and builds a constraint tree
while tracking node IDs to solver variables for later type retrieval.


# Constraint Generation with ID Tracking

@docs constrainWithIds

values.name: constrainWithIds
values.comment:
Generate type constraints for a canonical module, tracking node IDs.

Handles regular declarations, ports, and effect managers by traversing the
module's structure and producing a constraint tree. Also builds a mapping
from expression/pattern IDs to solver variables for later type retrieval.

name: Compiler.Type.Constrain.Typed.NodeIds
comment:
Unified node ID tracking for type constraint generation (Typed pathway).

This module provides a shared ID space for tracking solver variables
associated with canonical AST nodes (both expressions and patterns).

During constraint generation, each node is assigned a fresh type variable.
This module maintains the mapping from node IDs to those variables, enabling
the solver to later produce a mapping from node IDs to their inferred types.


# Types

@docs NodeVarMap, NodeIdState


# State

@docs emptyNodeIdState


# Recording

@docs recordNodeVar

aliases.name: NodeIdState
aliases.comment: State for tracking node ID to variable mappings during constraint generation.

aliases.name: NodeVarMap
aliases.comment:
Mapping from node IDs to solver variables.

Each key is the ID of either a canonical expression or pattern,
and the value is the solver variable representing its type.

values.name: emptyNodeIdState
values.comment: Initial empty node ID state.

values.name: recordNodeVar
values.comment:
Record a mapping from a node ID to its solver variable.

Negative IDs (used for placeholder nodes like synthesized patterns)
are skipped to avoid polluting the mapping.

name: Compiler.Type.Constrain.Typed.Pattern
comment:
Type constraint generation for pattern matching (Typed pathway).

This module generates type constraints for patterns while also tracking node IDs
to solver variables, enabling later retrieval of pattern types from the solver.

Uses an internal PatternProg DSL for stack-safe constraint generation,
preventing stack overflow on deeply nested patterns.


# Constraint Generation with ID Tracking

@docs addWithIds

values.name: addWithIds
values.comment:
Generate type constraints for a pattern, tracking node IDs.

Like the erased `add` but also tracks pattern node IDs to solver variables in the
NodeIdState, enabling later retrieval of pattern types from the solver.

IMPORTANT: This function matches `add` in constraint generation behavior.
For patterns that don't need constraints (PAnything, PVar, PAlias), we do NOT
add extra CPattern constraints or flex variables to the state. We only record
the pattern's type variable in NodeIds for later type retrieval.

Uses the stack-safe PatternProg DSL internally.
values.type: Compiler.AST.Canonical.Pattern -> Compiler.Reporting.Error.Type.PExpected Compiler.Type.Type.Type -> Compiler.Type.Constrain.Common.State -> Compiler.Type.Constrain.Typed.NodeIds.NodeIdState -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO ( Compiler.Type.Constrain.Common.State, Compiler.Type.Constrain.Typed.NodeIds.NodeIdState )

name: Compiler.Type.Constrain.Typed.Program

unions.name: InstrS
unions.comment:
One instruction in the stateful constraint DSL.

  - `MkFlexVarS k` - Allocate a fresh flex var, continue with `k var`
  - `MkFlexNumberS k` - Allocate a fresh number var, continue with `k var`
  - `GetStateS k` - Get current state, continue with `k state`
  - `ModifyStateS f k` - Modify state with function f, continue with `k ()`
  - `RunIOS io` - Run an IO action, wrapping the result

unions.name: ProgS
unions.comment:
A stateful program that threads state through constraint generation.

Used by constrainWithIds to track expression ID to type variable mappings.

  - `DoneS a` - Computation complete with value `a`
  - `StepS instr` - One instruction to execute, with continuation

values.name: andThenS
values.comment: Sequence two stateful programs, passing the result of the first to the second.
values.type: (a -> Compiler.Type.Constrain.Typed.Program.ProgS s b) -> Compiler.Type.Constrain.Typed.Program.ProgS s a -> Compiler.Type.Constrain.Typed.Program.ProgS s b

values.name: mapS
values.comment: Map a function over the stateful program result.

values.name: opGetS
values.comment: Get the current state.

values.name: opIOS
values.comment: Run an IO action that produces a value, then continue (stateful version).

values.name: opMkFlexNumberS
values.comment: Allocate a fresh flexible number type variable (stateful version).

values.name: opMkFlexVarS
values.comment: Allocate a fresh flexible type variable (stateful version).

values.name: opModifyS
values.comment: Modify the state with a function.

values.name: pureS
values.comment: Lift a pure value into the stateful program.

values.name: runS
values.comment:
Run a stateful constraint program to produce an IO (Constraint, state).

Uses `IO.loop` to interpret the program with an explicit continuation stack,
ensuring constant stack depth regardless of program complexity.
values.type: s -> Compiler.Type.Constrain.Typed.Program.ProgS s Compiler.Type.Type.Constraint -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO ( Compiler.Type.Type.Constraint, s )

name: Compiler.Type.Error

unions.name: Direction
unions.comment: Indicates whether a type is what we have or what we need in a type mismatch.

unions.name: Extension
unions.comment: Represents whether a record type is closed or has an extensible row variable.

unions.name: Problem
unions.comment:
Specific type mismatch problems that can be detected when comparing types.

These problems enable more helpful error messages by identifying common mistakes like
confusing Int and Float, missing record fields, or arity mismatches in function types.

unions.name: Super
unions.comment: Represents type variable constraints for numbers, comparable values, and appendable collections.

unions.name: Type
unions.comment:
Simplified type representation used for error reporting.

This type captures the structure of Elm types in a form that's optimized for generating
human-readable error messages, including function types, type variables, records, tuples,
and type aliases.

values.name: isChar
values.comment: Check if a canonical type name refers to the Char type.

values.name: isFloat
values.comment: Check if a canonical type name refers to the Float type.

values.name: isInt
values.comment: Check if a canonical type name refers to the Int type.

values.name: isList
values.comment: Check if a canonical type name refers to the List type.

values.name: isString
values.comment: Check if a canonical type name refers to the String type.

values.name: iteratedDealias
values.comment: Recursively remove all type aliases to reveal the underlying concrete type.

values.name: toComparison
values.comment: Compare two types and return formatted documentation for each along with a list of detected problems.
values.type: Compiler.Reporting.Render.Type.Localizer.Localizer -> Compiler.Type.Error.Type -> Compiler.Type.Error.Type -> ( Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc, Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc, List.List Compiler.Type.Error.Problem )

values.name: toDoc
values.comment: Convert a type to a formatted documentation representation for display in error messages.

values.name: typeDecoder
values.comment: Decode a Type value from bytes for deserialization.

values.name: typeEncoder
values.comment: Encode a Type value to bytes for serialization.

name: Compiler.Type.Instantiate
comment:
Instantiation of source-level type annotations into internal type representations.

This module converts type annotations from the canonical AST (source types) into the
internal type representation used during type inference. The conversion involves creating
fresh type variables and properly handling type aliases and polymorphism.

When a type annotation contains free type variables (like `a` in `List a`), those
variables are tracked in the FreeVars dictionary to ensure consistent mapping throughout
the type structure.


# Types

@docs FreeVars


# Conversion

@docs fromSrcType

aliases.name: FreeVars
aliases.comment:
A mapping from type variable names to their instantiated type representations.

When converting source types to internal types, free type variables (like `a` in `List a`)
need to be tracked to ensure all occurrences of the same variable name map to the same
internal type variable. This dictionary maintains that consistent mapping throughout the
conversion process.

values.name: fromSrcType
values.comment:
Convert a source-level type annotation into an internal type representation.

Takes a mapping of free type variables and a canonical source type, and produces
an internal type used during type inference. This handles:

  - Function types (lambdas)
  - Type variables (using the FreeVars mapping)
  - Named types with arguments (applications)
  - Type aliases (both filled and holey)
  - Tuples (pairs and triples)
  - Unit type
  - Record types with optional extension

The conversion may perform IO operations such as creating fresh type variables
for unbound names.

name: Compiler.Type.Occurs
comment:
Occurs check for detecting infinite types during type unification.

The occurs check prevents the creation of infinite types by detecting when a type
variable would occur within its own definition (e.g., `a = List a` where `a` appears
on both sides). This is essential for ensuring that type unification terminates and
produces valid, finite types.

During type inference, if we attempt to unify a type variable with a structure
containing that same variable, we have detected a type error that would create an
infinite type. This module performs that check by traversing type structures and
tracking which variables have been seen.


# Occurs Check

@docs occurs

values.name: occurs
values.comment:
Checks if a type variable occurs within its own definition, which would create an infinite type.

Returns True if a cycle is detected (the variable appears in its own structure),
False otherwise. This is used during type unification to prevent infinite types.

name: Compiler.Type.PostSolve
comment:
PostSolve phase for fixing Group B expression types and computing kernel types.

This phase runs after the type solver (`runWithIds`) and before `TypedCanonical.fromCanonical`.
It walks the canonical AST to:

1. Fix "missing" types for Group B expressions (those with unconstrained synthetic vars)
2. Compute kernel function types (`KernelTypeEnv`) via alias seeding and usage inference

The result is a fixed `nodeTypes` map where all expression IDs have meaningful types,
plus a `kernelEnv` for typed optimization.

@docs postSolve

values.name: postSolve
values.comment:
Run the post-solve phase on a canonical module.

Takes:

  - `annotations`: Top-level type annotations from type checking
  - `canonical`: The canonical module AST
  - `nodeTypes`: Expression/pattern types from the solver (Group B entries are unconstrained)

Returns:

  - `nodeTypes`: Fixed node types with Group B expressions properly typed
  - `kernelEnv`: Kernel function type environment for typed optimization
values.type: Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Compiler.AST.Canonical.Annotation -> Compiler.AST.Canonical.Module -> Compiler.Type.PostSolve.NodeTypes -> { nodeTypes : Compiler.Type.PostSolve.NodeTypes, kernelEnv : Compiler.Optimize.Typed.KernelTypes.KernelTypeEnv }

name: Compiler.Type.Solve
comment:
Constraint solver for Hindley-Milner type inference.

This module solves type constraints generated during type checking. It implements
Algorithm W with rank-based let-polymorphism, using pools to track variable scopes
and enable efficient generalization.

The solver works through constraints recursively:

1.  Converts types to unification variables
2.  Unifies actual types with expected types
3.  Manages variable ranks for generalization
4.  Detects infinite types via occurs check

Variables are organized into pools by rank. Higher ranks represent more deeply
nested scopes. During generalization, variables in young pools are either promoted
to older pools or generalized to `noRank` (making them polymorphic).


# Solving

@docs run, runWithIds

values.name: run
values.comment:
Solve a constraint tree and return either errors or type annotations.

Takes a constraint tree generated during type checking and solves it by
unifying types. Returns either a non-empty list of type errors or a
dictionary mapping names to their inferred type annotations.
values.type: Compiler.Type.Type.Constraint -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO (Result.Result (Compiler.Data.NonEmptyList.Nonempty Compiler.Reporting.Error.Type.Error) (Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Compiler.AST.Canonical.Annotation))

values.name: runWithIds
values.comment:
Solve constraints and return both annotations and per-node types.

Takes a constraint tree and a node variable map (mapping expression and pattern
IDs to their solver variables). Returns either errors or both the annotations
and a dictionary mapping node IDs to their inferred types.

Used for building the TypedCanonical AST.
values.type: Compiler.Type.Type.Constraint -> Data.Map.Dict Basics.Int Basics.Int System.TypeCheck.IO.Variable -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO (Result.Result (Compiler.Data.NonEmptyList.Nonempty Compiler.Reporting.Error.Type.Error) { annotations : Data.Map.Dict String.String Compiler.Data.Name.Name Compiler.AST.Canonical.Annotation, nodeTypes : Data.Map.Dict Basics.Int Basics.Int Compiler.AST.Canonical.Type })

name: Compiler.Type.Type

unions.name: Constraint
unions.comment:
Constraint tree representing type relationships for the solver.

Constraints form a tree structure that encodes all type requirements discovered
during constraint generation. The solver walks this tree to unify types and
detect type errors.

unions.name: Type
unions.comment:
Runtime type representation with unification variables.

Types during inference use Variables (union-find pointers) rather than the
named type variables seen in source code. This enables efficient unification
through union-find operations.

values.name: bool
values.comment: The Bool type.

values.name: char
values.comment: The Char type.

values.name: exists
values.comment:
Wraps a constraint with existentially quantified flex variables.

Creates a CLet constraint that introduces new flex variables that are local to
the given constraint, with no header bindings.

values.name: float
values.comment: The Float type.

values.name: funType
values.comment:
Constructs a function type from argument to result.

Represents the type `arg -> result` in Elm syntax.

values.name: int
values.comment: The Int type.

values.name: mat4
values.comment: The Mat4 WebGL type for 4x4 matrices.

values.name: mkFlexNumber
values.comment:
Creates a fresh unnamed flexible number variable.

Returns a new variable constrained to the Number supertype (Int or Float).
Used for numeric literals that could be either type.

values.name: mkFlexVar
values.comment:
Creates a fresh unnamed flexible type variable.

Returns a new variable that can unify with any type. Used during type inference
when the type is initially unknown.

values.name: nameToFlex
values.comment:
Creates a named flexible variable from a type variable name.

If the name corresponds to a supertype (number, comparable, appendable,
