compappend), creates a flexible supertype variable. Otherwise creates a regular
flexible variable.

values.name: nameToRigid
values.comment:
Creates a named rigid variable from a type variable name.

Rigid variables represent bound type variables from user annotations or let
polymorphism. They cannot unify with other rigid variables. If the name
corresponds to a supertype, creates a rigid supertype variable.

values.name: never
values.comment: The Never type, representing values that cannot exist.

values.name: nextMark
values.comment:
Generates the next mark value for a new traversal.

Each graph traversal should use a fresh mark value to distinguish visited nodes
from unvisited ones.

values.name: noMark
values.comment:
Default mark value for unmarked variables.

Marks are used during graph traversal to avoid revisiting nodes. This is the
initial mark value for fresh variables.

values.name: noRank
values.comment:
Rank value for unranked variables or error states.

Used to mark variables that haven't been assigned a rank yet or that are in an
error state.

values.name: outermostRank
values.comment:
Rank value for top-level type variables.

Variables at the outermost scope have rank 1, allowing the solver to detect
when generalization is safe.

values.name: string
values.comment: The String type.

values.name: texture
values.comment: The Texture WebGL type.

values.name: toAnnotation
values.comment:
Converts a type variable to a canonical type annotation.

Traverses the type structure to produce a user-readable type with properly
named type variables. Generates fresh names for unnamed variables and returns
a Forall quantifier listing all type variables found.

values.name: toCanType
values.comment:
Convert a solver Variable to a Can.Type.

Similar to toAnnotation but returns just the type without the Forall quantifier.
Used for building the TypedCanonical AST where each expression needs its type.

values.name: toErrorType
values.comment:
Converts a type variable to an error type for reporting.

Similar to toAnnotation but produces types in the error reporting format.
Includes special handling for infinite types (occurs check failures) by
detecting cycles during traversal.

values.name: unnamedFlexSuper
values.comment:
Content representing an unnamed flexible supertype variable.

Supertype variables are constrained to unify only with types matching the given
supertype (Number, Comparable, Appendable, or CompAppend).

values.name: unnamedFlexVar
values.comment:
Content representing an unnamed flexible variable.

Flexible variables can unify with any type and will be given generated names if
needed for error reporting.

values.name: vec2
values.comment: The Vec2 WebGL type for 2D vectors.

values.name: vec3
values.comment: The Vec3 WebGL type for 3D vectors.

values.name: vec4
values.comment: The Vec4 WebGL type for 4D vectors.

name: Compiler.Type.Unify
comment:
Type unification for Hindley-Milner type inference.

Unification finds a substitution that makes two types equal, or reports
that no such substitution exists. This module implements unification using
union-find data structures for efficient variable binding.


# Unification

@docs unify, Answer

unions.name: Answer
unions.comment:
Result of attempting to unify two type variables.

AnswerOk indicates successful unification and includes all newly created
variables. AnswerErr indicates a type mismatch and includes the conflicting
types for error reporting.

values.name: unify
values.comment:
Attempts to unify two type variables.

Finds a substitution that makes both variables represent the same type, or
returns an error with the conflicting types. Uses union-find to efficiently
merge equivalent variables and handles all type constructors including
functions, records, tuples, and type aliases.

name: Compiler.Type.UnionFind
comment:
Union-Find data structure for efficient type unification.

This module implements a union-find (disjoint-set) data structure optimized for type
inference. It allows efficient tracking of type variable equivalences and supports
path compression for fast lookups. The implementation uses mutable references (IORef)
to achieve efficient updates while maintaining a pure interface through the IO monad.

Union-find is critical for type inference performance, allowing near-constant-time
operations for unifying type variables and checking equivalence.


# Operations

@docs fresh, get, set, modify, union, equivalent, redundant

values.name: equivalent
values.comment:
Check if two union-find points are in the same equivalence class.
Returns True if they share the same representative element.

values.name: fresh
values.comment:
Create a fresh union-find point containing the given descriptor.
This initializes a new singleton set with weight 1.

values.name: get
values.comment:
Get the descriptor stored in a union-find point.
Follows links to find the representative element's descriptor.

values.name: modify
values.comment:
Modify the descriptor stored in a union-find point using a transformation function.
Follows links to modify the representative element's descriptor in place.

values.name: redundant
values.comment:
Check if a union-find point is redundant (i.e., it is a link to another point).
Returns True if the point has been merged into another equivalence class.

values.name: set
values.comment:
Set the descriptor stored in a union-find point.
Follows links to update the representative element's descriptor.

values.name: union
values.comment:
Unite two union-find points into the same equivalence class with a new descriptor.
Uses weighted union to keep the tree balanced - the lighter tree becomes a child of the heavier tree.
If the points are already equivalent, just updates the descriptor.

name: Control.Monad.State.Strict

unions.name: StateT
unions.comment:
newtype StateT s m a

A state transformer monad parameterized by:

s - The state.
m - The inner monad. (== IO)

The return function leaves the state unchanged, while >>= uses the final state of the first computation as the initial state of the second.

Ref.: <https://hackage.haskell.org/package/transformers-0.6.1.2/docs/Control-Monad-Trans-State-Lazy.html#t:StateT>

values.name: evalStateT
values.comment: Evaluates a state transformer computation with an initial state, returning only the result and discarding the final state.
values.type: Control.Monad.State.Strict.StateT s a -> s -> Task.Task Basics.Never a

values.name: liftIO
values.comment: Lifts a Task computation into the StateT monad transformer, leaving the state unchanged.
values.type: Task.Task Basics.Never a -> Control.Monad.State.Strict.StateT s a

values.name: put
values.comment: Stores the given REPL state to the underlying storage.

name: Control.Monad.State.TypeCheck.Strict

unions.name: StateT
unions.comment:
newtype StateT s m a

A state transformer monad parameterized by:

s - The state.
m - The inner monad. (== IO)

The return function leaves the state unchanged, while >>= uses the final state of the first computation as the initial state of the second.

Ref.: <https://hackage.haskell.org/package/transformers-0.6.1.2/docs/Control-Monad-Trans-State-Lazy.html#t:StateT>

values.name: andThen
values.comment: Chains StateT computations, passing the result of the first computation to a function that produces the second computation.
values.type: (a -> Control.Monad.State.TypeCheck.Strict.StateT s b) -> Control.Monad.State.TypeCheck.Strict.StateT s a -> Control.Monad.State.TypeCheck.Strict.StateT s b

values.name: apply
values.comment: Applies a function wrapped in StateT to a value wrapped in StateT, threading state through both computations.
values.type: Control.Monad.State.TypeCheck.Strict.StateT s a -> Control.Monad.State.TypeCheck.Strict.StateT s (a -> b) -> Control.Monad.State.TypeCheck.Strict.StateT s b

values.name: evalStateT
values.comment: Evaluates a state transformer computation with an initial state, returning only the result and discarding the final state.

values.name: gets
values.comment: Retrieves a projection of the current state by applying a function to it.

values.name: liftIO
values.comment: Lifts an IO computation into the StateT monad transformer, leaving the state unchanged.

values.name: map
values.comment: Maps a function over the result value of a StateT computation, leaving the state unchanged.

values.name: modify
values.comment: Modifies the current state by applying a transformation function to it.

values.name: pure
values.comment: Wraps a pure value in a StateT computation, leaving the state unchanged.

values.name: runStateT
values.comment: Runs a state transformer computation with an initial state, returning both the result and final state.
values.type: Control.Monad.State.TypeCheck.Strict.StateT s a -> s -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO ( a, s )

values.name: traverseList
values.comment: Applies a stateful computation to each element of a list, threading state through all computations and collecting results.
values.type: (a -> Control.Monad.State.TypeCheck.Strict.StateT s b) -> List.List a -> Control.Monad.State.TypeCheck.Strict.StateT s (List.List b)

values.name: traverseMap
values.comment: Applies a stateful computation to each value in a dictionary, threading state through all computations and collecting results.
values.type: (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> (k -> comparable) -> (a -> Control.Monad.State.TypeCheck.Strict.StateT s b) -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k a -> Control.Monad.State.TypeCheck.Strict.StateT s (Data.Map.Dict comparable k b)

values.name: traverseTuple
values.comment: Applies a stateful computation to the second element of a tuple, leaving the first element unchanged.
values.type: (b -> Control.Monad.State.TypeCheck.Strict.StateT s c) -> ( a, b ) -> Control.Monad.State.TypeCheck.Strict.StateT s ( a, c )

name: Data.IORef

unions.name: IORef
unions.comment: Mutable reference wrapping an index into a type-specific array in the IO state.

values.name: modifyIORefDescriptor
values.comment: Modify a Descriptor value in an IORef by applying a function.

values.name: modifyIORefMVector
values.comment: Modify a mutable vector (array) in an IORef by applying a function.
values.type: Data.IORef.IORef (Array.Array (Maybe.Maybe (List.List System.TypeCheck.IO.Variable))) -> (Array.Array (Maybe.Maybe (List.List System.TypeCheck.IO.Variable)) -> Array.Array (Maybe.Maybe (List.List System.TypeCheck.IO.Variable))) -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO ()

values.name: newIORefDescriptor

values.name: newIORefMVector

values.name: newIORefPointInfo

values.name: newIORefWeight

values.name: readIORefDescriptor

values.name: readIORefMVector

values.name: readIORefPointInfo

values.name: readIORefWeight

values.name: writeIORefDescriptor
values.comment: Write a Descriptor value to an IORef.

values.name: writeIORefMVector
values.comment: Write a mutable vector (array) to an IORef.

values.name: writeIORefPointInfo
values.comment: Write a PointInfo value to an IORef.

values.name: writeIORefWeight

name: Data.Map

unions.name: Dict
unions.comment:
A dictionary of keys and values. So a `Dict String User` is a dictionary
that lets you look up a `String` (such as user names) and find the associated
`User`.

    import Data.Map as Dict exposing (Dict)

    users : Dict String User
    users =
        Dict.fromList
            [ ( "Alice", User "Alice" 28 1.65 )
            , ( "Bob", User "Bob" 19 1.82 )
            , ( "Chuck", User "Chuck" 33 1.75 )
            ]

    type alias User =
        { name : String
        , age : Int
        , height : Float
        }

values.name: diff
values.comment: Keep a key-value pair when its key does not appear in the second dictionary.
values.type: Data.Map.Dict comparable k a -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k b -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k a

values.name: empty
values.comment:
Create an empty dictionary.

    isEmpty empty
    --> True

values.name: filter
values.comment: Keep only the key-value pairs that pass the given test.
values.type: (k -> v -> Basics.Bool) -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k v -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k v

values.name: foldl
values.comment:
Fold over the key-value pairs in a dictionary from most recently inserted
to least recently inserted.

    users : Dict String Int
    users =
        empty
            |> insert "Alice" 28
            |> insert "Bob" 19
            |> insert "Chuck" 33

    foldl (\name age result -> age :: result) [] users
    --> [28,19,33]
values.type: (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> (k -> v -> b -> b) -> b -> Data.Map.Dict c k v -> b

values.name: foldr
values.comment:
Fold over the key-value pairs in a dictionary from least recently inserted
to most recently insered.

    users : Dict String Int
    users =
        empty
            |> insert "Alice" 28
            |> insert "Bob" 19
            |> insert "Chuck" 33

    foldr (\name age result -> age :: result) [] users
    --> [33,19,28]
values.type: (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> (k -> v -> b -> b) -> b -> Data.Map.Dict c k v -> b

values.name: fromList
values.comment:
Convert an association list into a dictionary. The elements are inserted
from left to right. (If you want to insert the elements from right to left, you
can simply call `List.reverse` on the input before passing it to `fromList`.)
values.type: (k -> comparable) -> List.List ( k, v ) -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k v

values.name: get
values.comment:
Get the value associated with a key. If the key is not found, return
`Nothing`. This is useful when you are not sure if a key will be in the
dictionary.

    type Animal
        = Cat
        | Mouse

    animals : Dict String Animal
    animals = fromList [ ("Tom", Cat), ("Jerry", Mouse) ]

    get "Tom" animals
    --> Just Cat

    get "Jerry" animals
    --> Just Mouse

    get "Spike" animals
    --> Nothing
values.type: (k -> comparable) -> k -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k v -> Maybe.Maybe v

values.name: insert
values.comment:
Insert a key-value pair into a dictionary. Replaces value when there is
a collision.
values.type: (k -> comparable) -> k -> v -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k v -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k v

values.name: intersection
values.comment:
Keep a key-value pair when its key appears in the second dictionary.
Preference is given to values in the first dictionary.
values.type: (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k a -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k b -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k a

values.name: isEmpty
values.comment:
Determine if a dictionary is empty.

    isEmpty empty
    --> True

values.name: keys
values.comment:
Get all of the keys in a dictionary, in the order that they were inserted
with the most recently inserted key at the head of the list.

    keys (fromList [ ( 0, "Alice" ), ( 1, "Bob" ) ])
    --> [ 1, 0 ]
values.type: (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> Data.Map.Dict c k v -> List.List k

values.name: map
values.comment: Apply a function to all values in a dictionary.
values.type: (k -> a -> b) -> Data.Map.Dict c k a -> Data.Map.Dict c k b

values.name: member
values.comment: Determine if a key is in a dictionary.
values.type: (k -> comparable) -> k -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k v -> Basics.Bool

values.name: merge
values.comment:
The most general way of combining two dictionaries. You provide three
accumulators for when a given key appears:

1.  Only in the left dictionary.
2.  In both dictionaries.
3.  Only in the right dictionary.

You then traverse all the keys in the following order, building up whatever
you want:

1.  All the keys that appear only in the right dictionary from least
    recently inserted to most recently inserted.
2.  All the keys in the left dictionary from least recently inserted to most
    recently inserted (without regard to whether they appear only in the left
    dictionary or in both dictionaries).
values.type: (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> (k -> a -> result -> result) -> (k -> a -> b -> result -> result) -> (k -> b -> result -> result) -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k a -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k b -> result -> result

values.name: remove
values.comment:
Remove a key-value pair from a dictionary. If the key is not found,
no changes are made.
values.type: (k -> comparable) -> k -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k v -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k v

values.name: singleton
values.comment:
Create a dictionary with one key-value pair.

    singleton identity "key" 42
        |> get identity "key"
    --> Just 42
values.type: (k -> comparable) -> k -> v -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k v

values.name: size
values.comment:
Determine the number of key-value pairs in the dictionary.

    size (fromList [ ( "a", 1 ), ( "b", 2 ), ( "c", 3 ) ])
    --> 3

    size (insert 1 "b" (singleton 1 "a"))
    --> 1

values.name: toList
values.comment:
Convert a dictionary into an association list of key-value pairs, in the
order that they were inserted with the most recently inserted entry at the
head of the list.
values.type: (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> Data.Map.Dict c k v -> List.List ( k, v )

values.name: union
values.comment:
Combine two dictionaries. If there is a collision, preference is given
to the first dictionary.

If you are using this module as an ordered dictionary, the ordering of the
output dictionary will be all the entries of the first dictionary (from most
recently inserted to least recently inserted) followed by all the entries of
the second dictionary (from most recently inserted to least recently inserted).
values.type: Data.Map.Dict comparable k v -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k v -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k v

values.name: update
values.comment:
Update the value of a dictionary for a specific key with a given function.

If you are using this module as an ordered dictionary, please note that if you
are replacing the value of an existing entry, the entry will remain where it
is in the insertion order. (If you do want to change the insertion order,
consider using `get` in conjunction with `insert` instead.)
values.type: (k -> comparable) -> k -> (Maybe.Maybe v -> Maybe.Maybe v) -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k v -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k v

values.name: values
values.comment:
Get all of the values in a dictionary, in the order that they were inserted
with the most recently inserted value at the head of the list.

    values (fromList [ ( 0, "Alice" ), ( 1, "Bob" ) ])
    --> [ "Bob", "Alice" ]
values.type: (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> Data.Map.Dict c k v -> List.List v

name: Data.Set

unions.name: EverySet
unions.comment:
Represents a set of unique values. So `(Set Int)` is a set of integers and
`(Set String)` is a set of strings.

values.name: diff
values.comment:
Get the difference between the first set and the second. Keeps values
that do not appear in the second set.
values.type: Data.Set.EverySet comparable a -> Data.Set.EverySet comparable a -> Data.Set.EverySet comparable a

values.name: empty
values.comment: Create an empty set.

values.name: filter
values.comment: Create a new set consisting only of elements which satisfy a predicate.
values.type: (a -> Basics.Bool) -> Data.Set.EverySet comparable a -> Data.Set.EverySet comparable a

values.name: foldr
values.comment: Fold over the values in a set, in order from highest to lowest.
values.type: (a -> a -> Basics.Order) -> (a -> b -> b) -> b -> Data.Set.EverySet c a -> b

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Convert a list into a set, removing any duplicates.
values.type: (a -> comparable) -> List.List a -> Data.Set.EverySet comparable a

values.name: insert
values.comment: Insert a value into a set.
values.type: (a -> comparable) -> a -> Data.Set.EverySet comparable a -> Data.Set.EverySet comparable a

values.name: isEmpty
values.comment: Determine if a set is empty.

values.name: member
values.comment: Determine if a value is in a set.
values.type: (a -> comparable) -> a -> Data.Set.EverySet comparable a -> Basics.Bool

values.name: remove
values.comment: Remove a value from a set. If the value is not found, no changes are made.
values.type: (a -> comparable) -> a -> Data.Set.EverySet comparable a -> Data.Set.EverySet comparable a

values.name: size
values.comment: Determine the number of elements in a set.

values.name: toList
values.comment: Convert a set into a list, sorted from lowest to highest.
values.type: (a -> a -> Basics.Order) -> Data.Set.EverySet c a -> List.List a

values.name: union
values.comment: Get the union of two sets. Keep all values.
values.type: Data.Set.EverySet comparable a -> Data.Set.EverySet comparable a -> Data.Set.EverySet comparable a

name: Data.Vector
comment:
Utilities for working with mutable vectors in the IO monad during type checking.

This module provides operations on IORef-wrapped arrays used as mutable vectors in the type checker.
The "unsafe" prefix indicates operations that assume array indices exist or that values are present,
crashing if preconditions aren't met. This is acceptable in the type checker where these invariants
are maintained by the algorithm.


# Vector Operations

@docs unsafeLast, unsafeInit, unsafeFreeze


# Monadic Iteration

@docs forM_, imapM_

values.name: forM_
values.comment: Apply a monadic action to each element in the vector, discarding results (flipped argument order).
values.type: Data.IORef.IORef (Array.Array (Maybe.Maybe (List.List System.TypeCheck.IO.Variable))) -> (List.List System.TypeCheck.IO.Variable -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO b) -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO ()

values.name: imapM_
values.comment: Apply an indexed monadic action to each element in the vector, discarding results.
values.type: (Basics.Int -> List.List System.TypeCheck.IO.Variable -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO b) -> Data.IORef.IORef (Array.Array (Maybe.Maybe (List.List System.TypeCheck.IO.Variable))) -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO ()

values.name: unsafeFreeze
values.comment: Freeze a mutable vector into an immutable one (identity function, no actual freezing).

values.name: unsafeInit
values.comment: Return all elements except the last (identity function, preserving the reference).

values.name: unsafeLast
values.comment: Get the last element from a mutable vector, crashing if empty or invalid.

name: Data.Vector.Mutable
comment:
Mutable vector operations for type checker data structures.

This module provides operations on mutable vectors (IORef-wrapped arrays) used in the type checker.
Operations include creation, reading, writing, modification, and growing vectors.

@docs grow, length, modify, read, replicate, write

values.name: grow
values.comment: Grow a mutable vector by appending n Nothing elements, returning the same reference.

values.name: length
values.comment: Get the length of a mutable vector.

values.name: modify
values.comment: Modify an element at the given index in a mutable vector by applying a function.
values.type: Data.IORef.IORef (Array.Array (Maybe.Maybe (List.List System.TypeCheck.IO.Variable))) -> (List.List System.TypeCheck.IO.Variable -> List.List System.TypeCheck.IO.Variable) -> Basics.Int -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO ()

values.name: read
values.comment: Read an element at the given index from a mutable vector, crashing if not found or invalid.

values.name: replicate
values.comment: Create a new mutable vector with n copies of the given element.

values.name: write
values.comment: Write an element to the given index in a mutable vector.
values.type: Data.IORef.IORef (Array.Array (Maybe.Maybe (List.List System.TypeCheck.IO.Variable))) -> Basics.Int -> List.List System.TypeCheck.IO.Variable -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO ()

name: Prelude
comment:
Unsafe list operations that crash on empty lists, providing Haskell Prelude-style behavior.

This module provides partial functions that mirror Haskell's Prelude for list operations.
These functions are intentionally unsafe and will crash with descriptive error messages
when given empty lists, making them suitable for cases where the list is guaranteed to be non-empty.


# List Operations

@docs head, init, last

values.name: head
values.comment: Returns the first element of a list, crashing if the list is empty.

values.name: init
values.comment: Returns all elements except the last one, crashing if the list is empty.

values.name: last
values.comment: Returns the last element of a list, crashing if the list is empty.

name: System.Console.Ansi
comment:
ANSI terminal control codes for text styling and coloring.

This module defines types representing ANSI escape sequence parameters for controlling
terminal text appearance. It provides SGR (Select Graphic Rendition) commands that can
be converted to ANSI escape codes for styling terminal output with colors, intensity,
underlining, and other text effects.


# Colors

@docs Color, ColorIntensity, ConsoleLayer


# Text Styling

@docs ConsoleIntensity, Underlining


# SGR Commands

@docs SGR

unions.name: Color
unions.comment: Standard ANSI terminal colors.

unions.name: ColorIntensity
unions.comment: Intensity of ANSI colors: dull or vivid.

unions.name: ConsoleIntensity
unions.comment: ANSI text intensity affecting font weight and style.

unions.name: ConsoleLayer
unions.comment: Layer on which to apply colors: foreground (text) or background.

unions.name: SGR
unions.comment: ANSI Select Graphic Rendition commands for controlling terminal text appearance.

unions.name: Underlining
unions.comment: ANSI text underlining style.

name: System.Exit
comment:
Process exit code management for the Elm compiler.

This module provides functions to terminate the compiler process with appropriate
exit codes, following standard Unix conventions where 0 indicates success and
non-zero values indicate various failure conditions.

Ref.: <https://hackage.haskell.org/package/base-4.20.0.1/docs/System-Exit.html>


# Exit Codes

@docs ExitCode


# Exiting the Process

@docs exitWith, exitSuccess, exitFailure

unions.name: ExitCode
unions.comment: Exit code representing success (0) or failure (non-zero integer).

values.name: exitFailure
values.comment: Exit the program with exit code 1, indicating failure.

values.name: exitSuccess
values.comment: Exit the program with exit code 0, indicating success.

values.name: exitWith
values.comment: Exit the program with the specified exit code.

name: System.IO

unions.name: Handle
unions.comment: Opaque handle to an open file or stream, wrapping a file descriptor integer.

unions.name: IOMode
unions.comment: File opening mode specifying read, write, append, or read-write access.

unions.name: ReplState
unions.comment: State maintained by the REPL, containing three dictionaries for tracking REPL session data.

aliases.name: FilePath
aliases.comment: Type alias for file paths represented as strings.

aliases.name: Model
aliases.comment: The program's model state (unit type as we use tasks for state management).

aliases.name: Msg
aliases.comment: Messages are tasks to be executed.

aliases.name: Program
aliases.comment: Type alias for an IO program that runs impure tasks.

values.name: getLine
values.comment: Read a line of input from stdin.

values.name: hClose
values.comment: Close an open file handle.

values.name: hFileSize
values.comment: Get the size in bytes of the file associated with the handle.

values.name: hFlush
values.comment: Flush any buffered output on the handle (currently a no-op).

values.name: hIsTerminalDevice
values.comment: Check if the handle is connected to a terminal device (currently always returns True).

values.name: hPutStr
values.comment: Write a string to the specified handle without adding a newline.

values.name: hPutStrLn
values.comment: Write a string to the specified handle followed by a newline.

values.name: initialReplState
values.comment: Initial empty REPL state with empty dictionaries.

values.name: putStr
values.comment: Write a string to stdout without adding a newline.

values.name: putStrLn
values.comment: Write a string to stdout followed by a newline.

values.name: run
values.comment: Create and run an IO program from a task.

values.name: stderr
values.comment: Handle to the standard error stream.

values.name: stdout
values.comment: Handle to the standard output stream.

values.name: withFile
values.comment: Open a file with the specified mode, pass the handle to a callback, and automatically close it afterward.
values.type: String.String -> System.IO.IOMode -> (System.IO.Handle -> Task.Task Basics.Never a) -> Task.Task Basics.Never a

values.name: writeString
values.comment: Write a string to a file at the given path.

name: System.Process
comment:
External process creation and management for the Elm compiler.

This module provides functionality to spawn and control external processes,
allowing the compiler to invoke system tools, shell commands, and other
programs. It handles process configuration including standard stream redirection
and process lifetime management.

Ref.: <https://hackage.haskell.org/package/process-1.6.25.0/docs/System-Process.html>


# Process Configuration

@docs CreateProcess, CmdSpec, StdStream, proc


# Running Processes

@docs withCreateProcess, ProcessHandle, waitForProcess

unions.name: CmdSpec
unions.comment: Specification of a command to execute, with the executable and its arguments.

unions.name: ProcessHandle
unions.comment: Opaque handle to a running process, wrapping a process ID.

unions.name: StdStream
unions.comment: Specification for how to handle a standard stream (stdin, stdout, or stderr) when creating a process.

aliases.name: CreateProcess
aliases.comment: Configuration for creating a new process, specifying command and standard stream handling.
aliases.type: { cmdspec : System.Process.CmdSpec, std_in : System.Process.StdStream, std_out : System.Process.StdStream, std_err : System.Process.StdStream }

values.name: proc
values.comment: Create a process configuration for running a command with arguments, inheriting all standard streams.

values.name: waitForProcess
values.comment: Wait for a process to complete and return its exit code.

values.name: withCreateProcess
values.comment: Create and run a process with the given configuration, pass handles to a callback, and wait for completion.
values.type: System.Process.CreateProcess -> (Maybe.Maybe System.IO.Handle -> Maybe.Maybe System.IO.Handle -> Maybe.Maybe System.IO.Handle -> System.Process.ProcessHandle -> Task.Task Basics.Never System.Exit.ExitCode) -> Task.Task Basics.Never System.Exit.ExitCode

name: System.TypeCheck.IO

unions.name: Canonical
unions.comment:
A canonical module name referencing a type.

Contains the package name (as a tuple) and the module name within that package.
Used to uniquely identify types across different packages.

unions.name: Content
unions.comment:
The content of a type descriptor.

  - `FlexVar name`: A flexible type variable (can be unified with anything)
  - `FlexSuper supertype name`: A flexible variable constrained by a supertype
  - `RigidVar name`: A rigid type variable (cannot be unified)
  - `RigidSuper supertype name`: A rigid variable constrained by a supertype
  - `Structure type`: A concrete type structure (function, record, etc.)
  - `Alias canonical name args realType`: A type alias with its expansion
  - `Error`: Represents a type error

unions.name: Descriptor
unions.comment:
A type descriptor containing information about a type variable.

Descriptors are stored in the `ioRefsDescriptor` array and referenced by Points.
Each descriptor contains the actual type content, rank for generalization,
marking for traversal algorithms, and an optional copy field for cloning.

unions.name: FlatType
unions.comment:
The flattened representation of concrete type structures.

  - `App1 module name args`: Type constructor application (e.g., List Int)
  - `Fun1 arg result`: Function type
  - `EmptyRecord1`: The empty record type {}
  - `Record1 fields extension`: Record type with named fields and optional extension
  - `Unit1`: The unit type ()
  - `Tuple1 first second rest`: Tuple type (2 or more elements)

unions.name: Mark
unions.comment:
A mark used for graph traversal algorithms.

Marks prevent infinite loops when traversing cyclic type structures.
Each traversal uses a unique mark value to identify visited nodes.

unions.name: Point
unions.comment:
A reference to a type variable in the union-find structure.

Points are integer indices into the `ioRefsPointInfo` array in the State.
Used to implement path compression and union-by-rank for type unification.

unions.name: PointInfo
unions.comment:
Information stored at a Point in the union-find structure.

  - `Info rank weight`: A root node with its rank and weight
  - `Link parent`: A non-root node pointing to its parent

unions.name: Step
unions.comment:
Represents a step in a looping computation.

  - `Loop state`: Continue iterating with the given state
  - `Done a`: Terminate and return the result

unions.name: SuperType
unions.comment:
Supertypes that constrain type variables.

  - `Number`: Can be Int or Float
  - `Comparable`: Can be compared with (<), (>), etc.
  - `Appendable`: Can be concatenated with (++)
  - `CompAppend`: Both comparable and appendable

aliases.name: DescriptorProps
aliases.comment:
Properties of a type descriptor.

  - `content`: The actual type information (flex var, rigid var, structure, etc.)
  - `rank`: Used for let-generalization and determining type variable scope
  - `mark`: Used by traversal algorithms to avoid revisiting nodes
  - `copy`: Optional reference to a copied variable during cloning operations
aliases.type: { content : System.TypeCheck.IO.Content, rank : Basics.Int, mark : System.TypeCheck.IO.Mark, copy : Maybe.Maybe System.TypeCheck.IO.Variable }

aliases.name: IO
aliases.comment:
The IO monad for type inference computations.

An IO action is a function that takes a State and returns an updated State
along with a result value.

aliases.name: State
aliases.comment:
The mutable state threaded through IO computations.

Contains arrays acting as pseudo-mutable stores for:

  - `ioRefsWeight`: Union-find weights for path compression
  - `ioRefsPointInfo`: Point information (rank and parent links)
  - `ioRefsDescriptor`: Type descriptors for type variables
  - `ioRefsMVector`: Additional mutable vector storage
aliases.type: { ioRefsWeight : Array.Array Basics.Int, ioRefsPointInfo : Array.Array System.TypeCheck.IO.PointInfo, ioRefsDescriptor : Array.Array System.TypeCheck.IO.Descriptor, ioRefsMVector : Array.Array (Array.Array (Maybe.Maybe (List.List System.TypeCheck.IO.Variable))) }

aliases.name: Variable
aliases.comment:
A type variable is represented as a Point.

Variables are the fundamental unit of type inference, connected through
the union-find structure and associated with Descriptors.

values.name: andThen
values.comment:
Chain IO computations sequentially, threading state through each step.

The first IO action runs, then its result is passed to the continuation
function to produce the next IO action.
values.type: (a -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO b) -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO a -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO b

values.name: apply
values.comment:
Apply a function wrapped in IO to a value wrapped in IO.

Applicative functor operation for sequencing effects.
values.type: System.TypeCheck.IO.IO a -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO (a -> b) -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO b

values.name: foldM
values.comment:
Fold over a list from left to right with an IO-producing function.

Similar to `List.foldl`, but the combining function returns an IO action.
values.type: (b -> a -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO b) -> b -> List.List a -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO b

values.name: foldMDict
values.comment:
Fold over a dictionary from left to right with an IO-producing function.

The combining function receives the accumulator and value, ignoring keys.
values.type: (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> (b -> a -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO b) -> b -> Data.Map.Dict c k a -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO b

values.name: foldrM
values.comment:
Fold over a list from right to left with an IO-producing function.

Similar to `List.foldr`, but the combining function returns an IO action.
values.type: (a -> b -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO b) -> b -> List.List a -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO b

values.name: forM_
values.comment:
Flipped version of `mapM_` for convenient pipeline-style code.

Iterate over a list, executing IO actions for their side effects only.

values.name: loop
values.comment:
Execute a tail-recursive loop in the IO monad.

The callback function receives the current state and returns either `Loop newState`
to continue iterating or `Done result` to terminate.
values.type: (state -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO (System.TypeCheck.IO.Step state a)) -> state -> System.TypeCheck.IO.State -> ( System.TypeCheck.IO.State, a )

values.name: makeDescriptor
values.comment: Construct a Descriptor from its component properties.
values.type: System.TypeCheck.IO.Content -> Basics.Int -> System.TypeCheck.IO.Mark -> Maybe.Maybe System.TypeCheck.IO.Variable -> System.TypeCheck.IO.Descriptor

values.name: map
values.comment: Map a pure function over an IO computation.
values.type: (a -> b) -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO a -> System.TypeCheck.IO.State -> ( System.TypeCheck.IO.State, b )

values.name: mapM
values.comment:
Alias for `traverseList`.

Map an IO-producing function over a list, collecting results.
values.type: (a -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO b) -> List.List a -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO (List.List b)

values.name: mapM_
values.comment:
Map an IO-producing function over a list, discarding the results.

Used for executing side effects in sequence without collecting return values.

values.name: pure
values.comment: Lift a pure value into the IO monad without modifying state.

values.name: traverseList
values.comment:
Traverse a list, applying an IO-producing function to each element.

Collects results into a new list while threading state through each computation.
values.type: (a -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO b) -> List.List a -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO (List.List b)

values.name: traverseMap
values.comment:
Traverse a dictionary, applying an IO-producing function to each value.

The function ignores the key and only operates on values.
values.type: (k -> comparable) -> (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> (a -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO b) -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k a -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO (Data.Map.Dict comparable k b)

values.name: traverseMapWithKey
values.comment:
Traverse a dictionary, applying an IO-producing function to each key-value pair.

The function receives both the key and value, allowing key-dependent transformations.
values.type: (k -> comparable) -> (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> (k -> a -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO b) -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k a -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO (Data.Map.Dict comparable k b)

values.name: traverseTuple
values.comment:
Traverse the second element of a tuple with an IO-producing function.

The first element is left unchanged.
values.type: (b -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO c) -> ( a, b ) -> System.TypeCheck.IO.IO ( a, c )

values.name: unsafePerformIO
values.comment:
Execute an IO action and extract its result, discarding the final state.

This is the entry point for running IO computations. It initializes an empty
state (with no references allocated) and returns only the computed value.

name: Terminal.Bump
comment:
Version bump command for determining semantic versioning changes.

This module implements the `bump` command which analyzes API changes between
the current package code and the last published version, automatically determining
the appropriate version number according to semantic versioning rules (MAJOR, MINOR, or PATCH).


# Command Entry

@docs run

values.name: run
values.comment: Run the version bump command, analyzing API changes and suggesting appropriate version bumps.

name: Terminal.Diff
comment:
API difference reporting command for comparing package versions.

This module implements the `diff` command which compares the public API between
different versions of a package, showing what functions, types, and values have
been added, changed, or removed. This helps understand the impact of version changes.


# Command Entry

@docs run


# Configuration

@docs Args

unions.name: Args
unions.comment:
Arguments specifying what to compare.

CodeVsLatest compares local code to latest published version, CodeVsExactly
compares to a specific version, LocalInquiry compares two published versions,
GlobalInquiry compares versions of any package.

values.name: run
values.comment:
Compare package APIs between versions.

Shows additions, changes, and removals in the public API, helping understand
the impact of version changes and semantic versioning requirements.

name: Terminal.Format
comment:
Code formatting command for standardizing Elm code style.

This module implements the `format` command which automatically formats Elm source
files according to the official Elm style guide. It can format files in place,
validate formatting without changes, or output to different destinations.


# Command Entry

@docs run


# Configuration

@docs Flags, FlagsProps, makeFlags

unions.name: Flags
unions.comment:
Configuration flags for the format command.
Wraps FlagsProps to provide a typed configuration structure.

aliases.name: FlagsProps
aliases.comment:
Properties defining format command behavior.
Fields specify output destination, validation mode, input source, and confirmation settings.
aliases.type: { maybeOutput : Maybe.Maybe Utils.Main.FilePath, autoYes : Basics.Bool, doValidate : Basics.Bool, stdin : Basics.Bool }

values.name: makeFlags
values.comment:
Construct format command flags from configuration parameters.
Takes output path, auto-confirmation, validation mode, and stdin flag to create Flags.
values.type: Maybe.Maybe Utils.Main.FilePath -> Basics.Bool -> Basics.Bool -> Basics.Bool -> Terminal.Format.Flags

values.name: run
values.comment:
Execute the format command with given file paths and configuration.
Resolves input files, validates or formats them according to flags, and handles results.

name: Terminal.Init
comment:
Project initialization command for creating new Elm projects.

This module implements the `init` command which scaffolds a new Elm project by
creating an elm.json configuration file, source directories, and example test files.
It supports both application and package project types.


# Command Entry

@docs run


# Configuration

@docs Flags

unions.name: Flags
unions.comment:
Configuration flags for the init command.

Contains flags for package mode and auto-yes to skip confirmation prompts.

values.name: run
values.comment:
Initialize a new Elm project.

Creates an elm.json file with default dependencies, sets up source directories,
and creates an example test file. Supports both application and package projects.

name: Terminal.Install
comment:
Package installation command for managing dependencies.

This module implements the `install` command which fetches packages from the
Elm package registry and adds them to the project's elm.json file. It handles
dependency resolution, version constraints, and distinguishes between regular
dependencies and test dependencies.


# Command Entry

@docs run


# Configuration Types

@docs Args, Flags

unions.name: Args
unions.comment:
Arguments for the install command.

NoArgs means show help message, Install means add the named package.

unions.name: Flags
unions.comment:
Configuration flags for the install command.

Contains flags for test dependencies and auto-yes to skip confirmation prompts.

values.name: run
values.comment:
Add a package to the project's dependencies.

Fetches package information from the registry, resolves version constraints,
updates elm.json, and verifies the project builds with the new dependency.

name: Terminal.Main
comment:
Command-line interface entry point for the Guida/Elm compiler.

This module defines the main entry point for the compiler's command-line interface,
coordinating all available commands (repl, init, make, install, etc.) and providing
a unified interface for users interacting with the compiler through the terminal.


# Program Entry

@docs main

values.name: main
values.comment: The main entry point for the Guida/Elm compiler CLI application.

name: Terminal.Make

unions.name: Flags
unions.comment: Wrapper type for make command flags.

unions.name: Output
unions.comment: Output format and destination for the compiled code.

unions.name: ReportType
unions.comment: Report format type for compiler diagnostics.

aliases.name: FlagsData
aliases.comment: Configuration data for the make command, containing all flags and options.
aliases.type: { debug : Basics.Bool, optimize : Basics.Bool, withSourceMaps : Basics.Bool, output : Maybe.Maybe Terminal.Make.Output, report : Maybe.Maybe Terminal.Make.ReportType, docs : Maybe.Maybe String.String, showPackageErrors : Basics.Bool }

values.name: docsFile
values.comment: Parser definition for documentation file command-line arguments.

values.name: output
values.comment: Parser definition for output file command-line arguments.

values.name: parseDocsFile
values.comment: Parse a string into a documentation file path (must be .json extension).

values.name: parseOutput
values.comment: Parse a string into an Output value based on file extension.

values.name: parseReportType
values.comment: Parse a string into a ReportType value.

values.name: reportType
values.comment: Parser definition for report type command-line arguments.

values.name: run
values.comment: Execute the make command with the given source file paths and flags.

name: Terminal.Publish
comment:
Package publishing command for releasing packages to the Elm package registry.

This module implements the `publish` command which validates, versions, and uploads
packages to the public Elm package repository. It ensures packages meet quality
standards, have proper documentation, and follow semantic versioning rules.


# Command Entry

@docs run

values.name: run
values.comment:
TODO mandate no "exposing (..)" in packages to make
optimization to skip builds in Elm.Details always valid

name: Terminal.Repl

unions.name: CategorizedInput
unions.comment:
Result of analyzing user input to determine if it's complete.

Either the input is complete and can be evaluated (Done), or more lines
are needed and a prefill suggestion is provided (Continue).

unions.name: Flags
unions.comment:
Configuration flags for the REPL session.

Contains optional interpreter path and color output setting.

unions.name: Input
unions.comment:
Represents a categorized line of user input in the REPL.

Can be an import, type definition, port declaration, value declaration,
expression, or a REPL command (reset, exit, skip, help).

unions.name: Lines
unions.comment:
Accumulates multiple lines of user input.

Stores the most recent line and a reversed list of previous lines for
efficient appending during multi-line input.

unions.name: Output
unions.comment:
Describes what kind of output should be generated after evaluation.

OutputNothing for imports/types, OutputDecl for named declarations,
OutputExpr for expressions that should be printed.

unions.name: Prefill
unions.comment:
Pre-filled text for multi-line input continuation.

Provides either indentation or the start of a definition name to help
users continue typing multi-line declarations.

values.name: run
values.comment:
Start an interactive REPL session.

Initializes the REPL environment, displays the welcome message, and begins
the read-eval-print loop for evaluating Elm expressions and declarations.

name: Terminal.Terminal

values.name: app
values.comment:
Create and run a terminal application with commands.

Takes an intro message, outro message, and list of available commands.
Parses command-line arguments and routes to the appropriate command handler.
values.type: Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc -> Compiler.Reporting.Doc.Doc -> List.List Terminal.Terminal.Internal.Command -> Task.Task Basics.Never ()

values.name: flag
values.comment:
Define a flag that takes a value.

Takes the flag name, a parser for the value type, and a help description.
Example: flag "output" filePath "The output file path"

values.name: flags
values.comment:
Start building a flags specification.

Equivalent to noFlags, used as the starting point when adding flags with more.

values.name: more
values.comment:
Add a flag to a flags specification.

Builds up a list of flags by prepending each flag to the existing specification.

values.name: noArgs
values.comment:
Specify that a command takes no arguments.

Use this for commands that only use flags or don't take any input.

values.name: noFlags
values.comment:
A command with no flags.

Use this when your command doesn't accept any command-line flags.

values.name: onOff
values.comment:
Define a boolean on/off flag.

Takes the flag name and a help description. The flag is either present (True)
or absent (False). Example: onOff "verbose" "Enable verbose output"

values.name: oneOf
values.comment:
Specify that a command accepts multiple possible argument patterns.

Takes a list of argument specifications and allows any one of them to match.
Useful for commands that can be invoked in different ways.

values.name: require0
values.comment:
Specify that a command requires exactly zero arguments.

Equivalent to noArgs, used for consistency in the requireN family of functions.

values.name: require1
values.comment:
Specify that a command requires exactly one argument.

Takes a parser for the required argument type.

values.name: require2
values.comment:
Specify that a command requires exactly two arguments.

Takes parsers for both required argument types.

values.name: require3
values.comment:
Specify that a command requires exactly three arguments.

Takes parsers for all three required argument types.

values.name: zeroOrMore
values.comment:
Specify that a command accepts zero or more arguments.

Takes a parser for the argument type. All remaining command-line arguments
will be collected and parsed with this parser.

name: Terminal.Terminal.Chomp

unions.name: Chomper
unions.comment:
A parser that consumes and validates command-line argument chunks.

Takes suggestions and chunks, produces either a success with remaining chunks
and parsed value, or an error.

unions.name: Chunk
unions.comment:
A command-line argument string paired with its position index.

Used to track which argument is being parsed for error messages and completions.

unions.name: Suggest
unions.comment:
Tracks suggestions for tab completion.

NoSuggestion means no completion context, Suggest means we know the position,
Suggestions contains the actual completion options.

values.name: andThen
values.comment:
Chain chompers together, allowing the second to depend on the first's result.

Takes a function that produces a chomper based on a value, and a chomper that
produces that value. Enables dynamic parsing based on earlier results.
values.type: (a -> Terminal.Terminal.Chomp.Chomper x b) -> Terminal.Terminal.Chomp.Chomper x a -> Terminal.Terminal.Chomp.Chomper x b

values.name: apply
values.comment:
Apply a chomper producing a function to a chomper producing a value.

Sequences two chompers, applying the function from the first to the value
from the second.
values.type: Terminal.Terminal.Chomp.Chomper x a -> Terminal.Terminal.Chomp.Chomper x (a -> b) -> Terminal.Terminal.Chomp.Chomper x b

values.name: checkForUnknownFlags
values.comment:
Verify that all remaining flags are recognized.

Takes the valid flags specification and checks if any unrecognized flags remain
in the input, producing an error with suggestions if found.

values.name: chomp
values.comment:
Main entry point for parsing command-line arguments and flags.

Takes an optional completion index, the raw argument strings, argument parsers,
and a flag chomper. Returns suggestions for tab completion and either an error
or the parsed arguments and flags.
values.type: Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int -> List.List String.String -> List.List (Terminal.Terminal.Chomp.Suggest -> List.List Terminal.Terminal.Chomp.Chunk -> ( Terminal.Terminal.Chomp.Suggest, Result.Result Terminal.Terminal.Internal.ArgError args )) -> Terminal.Terminal.Chomp.Chomper Terminal.Terminal.Internal.FlagError flags -> ( Task.Task Basics.Never (List.List String.String), Result.Result Terminal.Terminal.Internal.Error ( args, flags ) )

values.name: chompArg
values.comment:
Create a chomper for a single required argument.

Takes the total number of chunks, a parser for the argument type, and a parse
function. Consumes one chunk and validates it.
values.type: Basics.Int -> Terminal.Terminal.Internal.Parser -> (String.String -> Maybe.Maybe a) -> Terminal.Terminal.Chomp.Chomper Terminal.Terminal.Internal.ArgError a

values.name: chompExactly
values.comment:
Parse arguments and ensure no extra arguments remain.

Takes a chomper and runs it, returning an error if any unparsed arguments are left.
values.type: Terminal.Terminal.Chomp.Chomper Terminal.Terminal.Internal.ArgError a -> Terminal.Terminal.Chomp.Suggest -> List.List Terminal.Terminal.Chomp.Chunk -> ( Terminal.Terminal.Chomp.Suggest, Result.Result Terminal.Terminal.Internal.ArgError a )

values.name: chompMultiple
values.comment:
Parse zero or more arguments of the same type.

Takes a chomper producing a function that accepts a list, a parser, and a parse
function. Collects all remaining arguments and applies them to the function.
values.type: Terminal.Terminal.Chomp.Chomper Terminal.Terminal.Internal.ArgError (List.List a -> b) -> Terminal.Terminal.Internal.Parser -> (String.String -> Maybe.Maybe a) -> Terminal.Terminal.Chomp.Suggest -> List.List Terminal.Terminal.Chomp.Chunk -> ( Terminal.Terminal.Chomp.Suggest, Result.Result Terminal.Terminal.Internal.ArgError b )

values.name: chompNormalFlag
values.comment:
Create a chomper for a flag that takes a value.

Takes the flag name, a parser, and a parse function. Returns Just the parsed
value if the flag is present, Nothing if absent.
values.type: String.String -> Terminal.Terminal.Internal.Parser -> (String.String -> Maybe.Maybe a) -> Terminal.Terminal.Chomp.Chomper Terminal.Terminal.Internal.FlagError (Maybe.Maybe a)

values.name: chompOnOffFlag
values.comment:
Create a chomper for a boolean on/off flag.

Takes the flag name and returns True if the flag is present, False if absent.

values.name: map
values.comment:
Transform the value produced by a chomper.

Applies a function to the successful result of a chomper without changing
the error type or parsing behavior.
values.type: (a -> b) -> Terminal.Terminal.Chomp.Chomper x a -> Terminal.Terminal.Chomp.Chomper x b

values.name: pure
values.comment:
Create a chomper that always succeeds with a given value.

Doesn't consume any input, just wraps the value in a successful chomper result.

name: Terminal.Terminal.Error
comment:
Error handling and help message generation for terminal commands.

This module provides functions to display error messages, help text, and command
overviews with proper formatting and exit codes.


# Exit Functions

@docs exitWithError, exitWithHelp, exitWithOverview, exitWithUnknown

values.name: exitWithError
values.comment:
Display a parsing error and exit with failure.

Takes an error from argument or flag parsing, formats it into a human-readable
message with suggestions, and exits with a non-zero code.

values.name: exitWithHelp
values.comment:
Display help text for a command and exit successfully.

Shows detailed help including the command description, argument patterns,
usage examples, and available flags.
values.type: Maybe.Maybe String.String -> String.String -> Text.PrettyPrint.ANSI.Leijen.Doc -> Terminal.Terminal.Internal.Args -> Terminal.Terminal.Internal.Flags -> Task.Task Basics.Never a

values.name: exitWithOverview
values.comment:
Display an overview of all available commands and exit successfully.

Shows the intro message, lists common commands with descriptions, shows all
commands, and displays the outro message.
values.type: Text.PrettyPrint.ANSI.Leijen.Doc -> Text.PrettyPrint.ANSI.Leijen.Doc -> List.List Terminal.Terminal.Internal.Command -> Task.Task Basics.Never a

values.name: exitWithUnknown
values.comment:
Display an error for an unknown command and exit with failure.

Shows an error message indicating the command wasn't recognized, suggests
similar command names if any exist, and exits with a non-zero code.
values.type: String.String -> List.List String.String -> Task.Task Basics.Never a

name: Terminal.Terminal.Helpers

values.name: filePath
values.comment:
Parser for general file paths.

Accepts any string as a file path without validation.

values.name: guidaOrElmFile
values.comment:
Parser for Guida or Elm source file paths.

Accepts file paths ending in .guida or .elm extensions.

values.name: package
values.comment:
Parser for Elm package names.

Accepts package names in the format "author/project" like "elm/core" or "elm/html".

values.name: parseFilePath
values.comment:
Parse a string as a file path.

Always succeeds, accepting any string as a valid file path.

values.name: parseGuidaOrElmFile
values.comment:
Parse a string as a Guida or Elm file path.

Returns Just the file path if it has a .guida or .elm extension, Nothing otherwise.

values.name: parsePackage
values.comment:
Parse a string as an Elm package name.

Returns Just the package name if valid (format: author/project), Nothing otherwise.

values.name: parseVersion
values.comment:
Parse a string as a version number.

Returns Just the parsed version or Nothing if invalid.

values.name: version
values.comment:
Parser for version numbers.

Accepts semantic version strings like "1.0.0" or "2.3.4".

name: Terminal.Terminal.Internal

unions.name: ArgError
unions.comment:
Error type for argument parsing failures.

ArgMissing means a required argument was not provided, ArgBad means an argument
was provided but couldn't be parsed, ArgExtras means unexpected extra arguments
were provided.

unions.name: Args
unions.comment:
A specification for command arguments.

Contains a list of possible complete argument patterns that the command accepts.

unions.name: Command
unions.comment:
A terminal command with all its configuration.

Wraps CommandData to provide a distinct type for commands.

unions.name: CompleteArgs
unions.comment:
A complete pattern of arguments for a command.

Exactly means a fixed sequence of required arguments, Multiple adds a repeating
argument that can appear zero or more times after the required arguments.

unions.name: Error
unions.comment:
Top-level error type for command parsing.

BadArgs contains a list of argument errors, BadFlag contains a single flag error.

unions.name: Expectation
unions.comment:
Expected value information for error messages.

Contains the singular type name and a task to generate example values.

unions.name: Flag
unions.comment:
A single command-line flag specification.

Flag takes a name, parser, and description for flags with values.
OnOff takes a name and description for boolean flags.

unions.name: FlagError
unions.comment:
Error type for flag parsing failures.

FlagWithValue means an on/off flag was given a value, FlagWithBadValue means
a flag value couldn't be parsed, FlagWithNoValue means a flag requiring a value
had none, FlagUnknown means an unrecognized flag was provided.

unions.name: Flags
unions.comment:
A collection of command-line flags.

FDone represents no flags, FMore adds a flag to the collection.
Built up recursively to form a list of flags.

unions.name: Parser
unions.comment:
A parser for command-line argument values.

Contains the singular and plural names for help text, a suggestion function
for tab completion, and an examples function for error messages.
cases: { singular : String.String, plural : String.String, suggest : String.String -> Task.Task Basics.Never (List.List String.String), examples : String.String -> Task.Task Basics.Never (List.List String.String) }

unions.name: RequiredArgs
unions.comment:
A sequence of required arguments.

Done represents no more required arguments, Required adds one required argument
to the sequence.

unions.name: Summary
unions.comment:
Indicates whether a command should be shown in the main help overview.

Common commands appear with a description in the overview, Uncommon commands
are only listed by name. Use Common for frequently-used commands with a brief
2-3 line description, Uncommon for specialized or advanced commands.

aliases.name: CommandData
aliases.comment:
Configuration data for a terminal command.

Includes the command name, summary, detailed help text, usage example,
argument and flag specifications, and the run function that executes the command.
aliases.type: { name : String.String, summary : Terminal.Terminal.Internal.Summary, details : String.String, example : Text.PrettyPrint.ANSI.Leijen.Doc, args : Terminal.Terminal.Internal.Args, flags : Terminal.Terminal.Internal.Flags, run : List.List String.String -> Result.Result Terminal.Terminal.Internal.Error (Task.Task Basics.Never ()) }

values.name: toName
values.comment:
Extract the name from a command.

Returns the command's name string.

name: Terminal.Test

unions.name: Flags
unions.comment:
Configuration flags for the test command.

Contains optional seed value, number of test runs, and report format.

unions.name: Report
unions.comment:
Test report output format.

Json for machine-readable JSON output, JUnit for XML test reports,
Console for human-readable terminal output.

values.name: format
values.comment:
Parser for test report format arguments.

Accepts format names: json, junit, or console.

values.name: parseReport
values.comment:
Parse a string as a test report format.

Returns Just the format if recognized, Nothing otherwise.

values.name: run
values.comment:
Run the test suite with the given file paths and configuration.

Discovers test files, generates test runners, compiles them, and executes
the tests using Node.js with the specified configuration.

name: Terminal.Uninstall
comment:
Package removal command for cleaning up dependencies.

This module implements the `uninstall` command which removes packages from the
project's elm.json file. It handles dependency resolution to ensure the project
remains in a valid state after package removal.


# Command Entry

@docs run


# Configuration

@docs Args, Flags

unions.name: Args
unions.comment:
Arguments for the uninstall command.

NoArgs means no package specified, Uninstall means remove the named package.

unions.name: Flags
unions.comment:
Configuration flags for the uninstall command.

Contains the auto-yes flag to skip confirmation prompts.

values.name: run
values.comment:
Remove a package from the project's dependencies.

Updates elm.json to remove the package, re-solves dependencies, and verifies
the project still builds correctly after removal.

name: Text.PrettyPrint.ANSI.Leijen

unions.name: Color
unions.comment: Represents ANSI terminal colors in both vivid and dull variants.

unions.name: SimpleDoc
unions.comment: A simplified document representation after layout, ready for rendering to a string.

aliases.name: Doc
aliases.comment: A document that can be pretty-printed with ANSI styling.

aliases.name: Style
aliases.comment: Represents the styling attributes applied to text in a document.
aliases.type: { bold : Basics.Bool, underline : Basics.Bool, color : Maybe.Maybe Text.PrettyPrint.ANSI.Leijen.Color }

values.name: a
values.comment: Concatenates two documents with a space or line break between them.

values.name: align
values.comment: Aligns a document to the current column position.

values.name: append
values.comment: Concatenates two documents without any separator.

values.name: black
values.comment: Applies vivid black color to a document.

values.name: blue
values.comment: Applies vivid blue color to a document.

values.name: cat
values.comment: Concatenates documents vertically or horizontally depending on available space.

values.name: cyan
values.comment: Applies vivid cyan color to a document.

values.name: displayIO
values.comment: Writes a SimpleDoc to the specified IO handle as a task.

values.name: displayS
values.comment: Converts a SimpleDoc to a string with ANSI escape codes, appending to an accumulator.

values.name: dullcyan
values.comment: Applies dull cyan color to a document.

values.name: dullred
values.comment: Applies dull red color to a document.

values.name: dullyellow
values.comment: Applies dull yellow color to a document.

values.name: empty
values.comment: An empty document.

values.name: fill
values.comment: Indents a document by the specified number of spaces.

values.name: fillSep
values.comment: Concatenates documents with soft line breaks that become spaces when possible.

values.name: green
values.comment: Applies vivid green color to a document.

values.name: hang
values.comment: Creates a hanging indent with the specified offset.

values.name: hcat
values.comment: Concatenates documents horizontally with no separator.

values.name: hsep
values.comment: Concatenates documents horizontally with spaces between them.

values.name: indent
values.comment: Indents a document by the specified number of spaces.

values.name: plain
values.comment: Removes all styling from a document.

values.name: plus
values.comment: Combines two documents with a space between them.

values.name: red
values.comment: Applies vivid red color to a document.

values.name: renderPretty
values.comment: Renders a Doc into a SimpleDoc with the specified page width.

values.name: sep
values.comment: Concatenates documents with line breaks or spaces depending on available space.

values.name: text
values.comment: Creates a document containing the given string.

values.name: underline
values.comment: Applies underline styling to a document.

values.name: vcat
values.comment: Concatenates documents vertically with line breaks between them.

values.name: yellow
values.comment: Applies vivid yellow color to a document.

name: Utils.Bytes.Decode

values.name: assocListDict
values.comment: Decodes a dictionary from a list of key-value pairs.
values.type: (k -> comparable) -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder k -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder v -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder (Data.Map.Dict comparable k v)

values.name: bool
values.comment: Decodes a boolean value from a single byte, where 1 is true and 0 is false.

values.name: everySet
values.comment: Decodes a set from a list of elements.
values.type: (a -> comparable) -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder a -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder (Data.Set.EverySet comparable a)

values.name: float
values.comment: Decodes a 64-bit floating point number in big-endian byte order.

values.name: int
values.comment: Decodes an integer stored as a 64-bit float and rounds it.

values.name: jsonPair
values.comment: Decodes a pair of values as a tuple.
values.type: Bytes.Decode.Decoder a -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder b -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder ( a, b )

values.name: lazy
values.comment: Creates a lazy decoder that defers construction until needed, enabling recursive decoders.

values.name: list
values.comment: Decodes a length-prefixed list of elements.

values.name: map6
values.comment: Combines six decoders using a function that takes six arguments.
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e -> f -> result) -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder a -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder b -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder c -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder d -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder e -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder f -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder result

values.name: map8
values.comment: Combines eight decoders using a function that takes eight arguments.
values.type: (a -> b -> c -> d -> e -> f -> g -> h -> result) -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder a -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder b -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder c -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder d -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder e -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder f -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder g -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder h -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder result

values.name: maybe
values.comment: Decodes a Maybe value, where a leading byte indicates presence (1) or absence (0).

values.name: nonempty
values.comment: Decodes a non-empty list, failing if the list is empty.

values.name: oneOrMore
values.comment: Decodes a binary tree structure with at least one element.

values.name: result
values.comment: Decodes a Result value, where a leading byte indicates Ok (0) or Err (1).
values.type: Bytes.Decode.Decoder x -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder a -> Bytes.Decode.Decoder (Result.Result x a)

values.name: string
values.comment: Decodes a length-prefixed UTF-8 string.

values.name: unit
values.comment: Decodes a unit value, expecting a single zero byte.

name: Utils.Bytes.Encode

values.name: assocListDict
values.comment: Encodes a dictionary as a list of key-value pairs.
values.type: (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> (k -> Bytes.Encode.Encoder) -> (v -> Bytes.Encode.Encoder) -> Data.Map.Dict c k v -> Bytes.Encode.Encoder

values.name: bool
values.comment: Encodes a boolean value as a single byte, where true is 1 and false is 0.

values.name: everySet
values.comment: Encodes a set as a list of elements.
values.type: (a -> a -> Basics.Order) -> (a -> Bytes.Encode.Encoder) -> Data.Set.EverySet c a -> Bytes.Encode.Encoder

values.name: float
values.comment: Encodes a 64-bit floating point number in big-endian byte order.

values.name: int
values.comment: Encodes an integer as a 64-bit float in big-endian byte order.

values.name: jsonPair
values.comment: Encodes a pair of values as a tuple.

values.name: list
values.comment: Encodes a list with a length prefix followed by encoded elements.

values.name: maybe
values.comment: Encodes a Maybe value with a leading byte indicating presence (1) or absence (0).

values.name: nonempty
values.comment: Encodes a non-empty list as a regular list.

values.name: oneOrMore
values.comment: Encodes a binary tree structure with at least one element.

values.name: result
values.comment: Encodes a Result value with a leading byte indicating Ok (0) or Err (1).
values.type: (x -> Bytes.Encode.Encoder) -> (a -> Bytes.Encode.Encoder) -> Result.Result x a -> Bytes.Encode.Encoder

values.name: string
values.comment: Encodes a UTF-8 string with a length prefix.

values.name: unit
values.comment: Encodes a unit value as a single zero byte.

name: Utils.Crash
comment:
Provides an intentionally divergent crash function for unrecoverable errors in the compiler.
This function creates an infinite recursive loop that never returns, allowing it to have any
return type while expressing that execution cannot continue.


# Crash Function

@docs crash

values.name: crash
values.comment:
Creates an infinite loop that crashes the program with the given error message.
This function has a polymorphic return type, allowing it to be used anywhere a value is expected.

name: Utils.Impure

unions.name: Body
unions.comment: Represents different types of HTTP request bodies.

unions.name: Resolver
unions.comment:
Defines how to resolve an HTTP response into a value.
All resolvers crash the program if the HTTP request fails.

values.name: customTask
values.comment:
Creates an HTTP task with a custom method, URL, headers, body, and response resolver.
Crashes the program if the request fails or the resolver cannot parse the response.
values.type: String.String -> String.String -> List.List Http.Header -> Utils.Impure.Body -> Utils.Impure.Resolver a -> Task.Task Basics.Never a

values.name: task
values.comment:
Creates a POST request task with the given URL, headers, body, and response resolver.
Crashes the program if the request fails or the resolver cannot parse the response.
values.type: String.String -> List.List Http.Header -> Utils.Impure.Body -> Utils.Impure.Resolver a -> Task.Task Basics.Never a

name: Utils.Main

unions.name: ChItem
unions.comment: A channel item containing a value and a pointer to the next item in the stream.

unions.name: Chan
unions.comment: A thread-safe channel for communication between threads, implemented using MVars.

unions.name: HttpExceptionContent
unions.comment: Content describing an HTTP exception that occurred during a request.

unions.name: HttpResponse
unions.comment: An HTTP response with status, headers, and optional body.

unions.name: HttpStatus
unions.comment: HTTP status with code and message.

unions.name: LockSharedExclusive
unions.comment: File locking mode for exclusive or shared access.

unions.name: MVar
unions.comment: A mutable variable for communication between threads, identified by an integer reference.

unions.name: ReplSettings
unions.comment: Settings for the REPL including history file, auto-history, and completion function.

unions.name: SomeException
unions.comment: A generic exception type representing any exception.

aliases.name: FilePath
aliases.comment: A file path represented as a string.

aliases.name: HttpResponseHeaders
aliases.comment: HTTP response headers as a list of key-value pairs.

aliases.name: ReplInputT
aliases.comment: The REPL input monad, which is just a Task in this implementation.

aliases.name: ThreadId
aliases.comment: A thread identifier for concurrent execution.

values.name: binaryDecodeFileOrFail
values.comment: Decode a binary file using the provided decoder, returning an error with position and message on failure.
values.type: Bytes.Decode.Decoder a -> Utils.Main.FilePath -> Task.Task Basics.Never (Result.Result ( Basics.Int, String.String ) a)

values.name: binaryEncodeFile
values.comment: Encode a value to binary and write it to a file.

values.name: bracket_
values.comment: Execute an action with setup and cleanup tasks, discarding the setup result.
values.type: Task.Task Basics.Never a -> Task.Task Basics.Never b -> Task.Task Basics.Never c -> Task.Task Basics.Never c

values.name: builderHPutBuilder
values.comment: Write a string to a file handle.

values.name: dictMapM_
values.comment: Map a monadic function over dictionary values, discarding the results and returning ().
values.type: (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> (a -> Task.Task Basics.Never b) -> Data.Map.Dict c k a -> Task.Task Basics.Never ()

values.name: dirCanonicalizePath
values.comment: Convert a path to its canonical form, resolving symbolic links and removing redundant components.

values.name: dirCreateDirectoryIfMissing
values.comment: Create a directory if it doesn't exist, optionally creating parent directories.

values.name: dirDoesDirectoryExist
values.comment: Check if a directory exists at the given path.

values.name: dirDoesFileExist
values.comment: Check if a file exists at the given path.

values.name: dirFindExecutable
values.comment: Search for an executable in the system PATH, returning its full path if found.

values.name: dirGetAppUserDataDirectory
values.comment: Get the application-specific user data directory for the given application name.

values.name: dirGetCurrentDirectory
values.comment: Get the current working directory.

values.name: dirGetModificationTime
values.comment: Get the last modification time of a file or directory.

values.name: dirListDirectory
values.comment: List all files and directories in the given directory path.

values.name: dirRemoveDirectoryRecursive
values.comment: Remove a directory and all its contents recursively.

values.name: dirRemoveFile
values.comment: Remove a file at the given path.

values.name: dirWithCurrentDirectory
values.comment: Run an action with a temporarily changed current directory, restoring the original directory afterward.
values.type: Utils.Main.FilePath -> Task.Task Basics.Never a -> Task.Task Basics.Never a

values.name: eitherLefts
values.comment: Extract all error values from a list of Results, discarding the Ok values.

values.name: envGetArgs
values.comment: Get the command-line arguments passed to the program.

values.name: envGetProgName
values.comment: Get the program name (hardcoded as "guida" in this implementation).

values.name: envLookupEnv
values.comment: Look up an environment variable by name, returning Nothing if not found.

values.name: filterM
values.comment: Filter a list using a monadic predicate, preserving elements where the predicate returns True.
values.type: (a -> Task.Task Basics.Never Basics.Bool) -> List.List a -> Task.Task Basics.Never (List.List a)

values.name: find
values.comment: Find a value by key in a dictionary, crashing if the key is not present. Use with caution.
values.type: (k -> comparable) -> k -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k a -> a

values.name: findMax
values.comment: Find the maximum key-value pair in a dictionary, crashing if the dictionary is empty.
values.type: (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k a -> ( k, a )

values.name: foldM
values.comment: Fold a list from the left using a monadic function, accumulating results in the RResult monad.
values.type: (b -> a -> Compiler.Reporting.Result.RResult info warnings error b) -> b -> List.List a -> Compiler.Reporting.Result.RResult info warnings error b

values.name: foldl1_
values.comment: Fold a non-empty list from the left with argument order flipped. Crashes if the list is empty.

values.name: foldr1
values.comment: Fold a non-empty list from the right, using the last element as the initial accumulator. Crashes if the list is empty.

values.name: forkIO
values.comment: Fork a new thread to execute the given task concurrently, returning the thread ID.

values.name: fpAddExtension
values.comment: Add a file extension to a path. Automatically adds a dot if not present in the extension.

values.name: fpAddTrailingPathSeparator
values.comment: Ensure a path ends with a trailing path separator.

values.name: fpCombine
values.comment:
An alias for `</>`.

Combine two paths with a path separator. If the second path starts with a
path separator or a drive letter, then it returns the second.
The intention is that readFile `(dir </> file)` will access the same file
as `setCurrentDirectory dir; readFile file`.

values.name: fpDropExtension
values.comment: Remove the extension from a path.

values.name: fpDropFileName
values.comment: Remove the file name component from a file path, leaving only the directory path.

values.name: fpIsRelative
values.comment: Check if a path is relative (does not start with a slash).

values.name: fpJoinPath
values.comment: Join a list of path components into a single path, handling leading slashes.

values.name: fpMakeRelative
values.comment: Make a path relative to a root directory by removing the root prefix if present.

values.name: fpPathSeparator
values.comment: The path separator character used by the file system (forward slash on Unix-like systems).

values.name: fpSplitDirectories
values.comment: Split a file path into its directory components.

values.name: fpSplitExtension
values.comment: Split a file path into the base name and extension (including the dot).

values.name: fpSplitFileName
values.comment: Split a path into the directory part and the file name part.

values.name: fpTakeDirectory
values.comment: Extract the directory part of a path (everything before the last slash).

values.name: fpTakeExtension
values.comment: Extract just the extension from a path (including the dot).

values.name: fpTakeFileName
values.comment: Extract just the file name from a path (everything after the last slash).

values.name: httpExceptionContentDecoder
values.comment: Decoder for HTTP exception content from binary data.

values.name: httpExceptionContentEncoder
values.comment: Encoder for HTTP exception content to binary data.

values.name: httpHLocation
values.comment: The "Location" HTTP header name constant.

values.name: httpResponseHeaders
values.comment: Extract the headers from an HTTP response.

values.name: httpResponseStatus
values.comment: Extract the status from an HTTP response.

values.name: httpStatusCode
values.comment: Extract the numeric status code from an HTTP status.

values.name: keysSet
values.comment: Extract all keys from a dictionary as a set.
values.type: (k -> comparable) -> (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> Data.Map.Dict comparable k a -> Data.Set.EverySet comparable k

values.name: liftIOInputT
